CREATE TABLE pipeline_importer_history (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   repository_uuid Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   last_checked_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE pull_request_importer_history (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   repository_uuid Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   last_checked_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE ci_pipeline_logs (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   repository_uuid VARCHAR NOT NULL,   pipeline_id TEXT NOT NULL,   executed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   duration_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (repository_uuid) REFERENCES repository_metadata(uuid) );  CREATE TABLE ci_build_jobs (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   pipeline_log_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   job_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   completed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   exit_code INTEGER,   FOREIGN KEY (pipeline_log_id) REFERENCES ci_pipeline_logs(id) );  CREATE TABLE ci_test_results (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   job_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   test_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   duration_seconds REAL,   FOREIGN KEY (job_id) REFERENCES ci_build_jobs(id) );  CREATE TABLE ci_artifacts (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   job_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   artifact_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   artifact_path VARCHAR NOT NULL,   size_bytes INTEGER NOT NULL,   upload_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (job_id) REFERENCES ci_build_jobs(id) );  CREATE TABLE vulnerability_scans (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   repository_uuid VARCHAR NOT NULL,   scan_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   scanner_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   total_vulnerabilities INTEGER NOT NULL,   critical_count INTEGER NOT NULL,   high_count INTEGER NOT NULL,   medium_count INTEGER NOT NULL,   low_count INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (repository_uuid) REFERENCES repository_metadata(uuid) );  CREATE TABLE vulnerability_details (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   scan_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   component_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   component_version VARCHAR NOT NULL,   vulnerability_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   severity VARCHAR NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   suggested_fix VARCHAR,   FOREIGN KEY (scan_id) REFERENCES vulnerability_scans(id) );  CREATE TABLE branch_protection_rules (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   repository_uuid VARCHAR NOT NULL,   branch_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   require_pull_request_reviews BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   require_status_checks BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   enforce_admins BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   FOREIGN KEY (repository_uuid) REFERENCES repository_metadata(uuid) );  CREATE TABLE repository_collaborators (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   repository_uuid VARCHAR NOT NULL,   user_login VARCHAR NOT NULL,   permission_level VARCHAR NOT NULL,   invited_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (repository_uuid) REFERENCES repository_metadata(uuid) );  CREATE TABLE repository_teams (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   repository_uuid VARCHAR NOT NULL,   team_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   permission_level VARCHAR NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (repository_uuid) REFERENCES repository_metadata(uuid) );  CREATE TABLE workflow_runs (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   repository_uuid VARCHAR NOT NULL,   workflow_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   run_id TEXT NOT NULL,   started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   completed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   conclusion VARCHAR,   FOREIGN KEY (repository_uuid) REFERENCES repository_metadata(uuid) );  CREATE TABLE workflow_jobs (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   run_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   job_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   completed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (run_id) REFERENCES workflow_runs(id) );  CREATE TABLE workflow_steps (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   job_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   step_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   completed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   output TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (job_id) REFERENCES workflow_jobs(id) );  CREATE TABLE issue_labels (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   repository_uuid VARCHAR NOT NULL,   label_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   color VARCHAR NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (repository_uuid) REFERENCES repository_metadata(uuid) );  CREATE TABLE pull_request_labels (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   pr_number INTEGER NOT NULL,   label_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (label_id) REFERENCES issue_labels(id) );  CREATE TABLE event_webhooks (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   repository_uuid VARCHAR NOT NULL,   hook_url TEXT NOT NULL,   events TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   FOREIGN KEY (repository_uuid) REFERENCES repository_metadata(uuid) );  CREATE TABLE notification_preferences (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_login VARCHAR NOT NULL,   repository_uuid VARCHAR NOT NULL,   email_notifications BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   slack_webhook TEXT,   push_notification_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   FOREIGN KEY (repository_uuid) REFERENCES repository_metadata(uuid) );

### Question:
Identify the three most frequently used job names in CI build jobs that ended with a non-zero exit code in the last month.

SELECT job_name, COUNT(*) AS count FROM ci_build_jobs JOIN ci_pipeline_logs ON ci_build_jobs.pipeline_log_id = ci_pipeline_logs.id WHERE exit_code != 0   AND executed_at >= datetime('now', '-30 days') GROUP BY job_name ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 3;

CREATE TABLE DEF_CCY_XRATES (   LAST_DATE Date NOT NULL,   CCY NCHAR NOT NULL,   VAL Decimal NOT NULL,   "DESC" NCHAR NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE CCY_XRATES (   DT Date NOT NULL,   CCY NCHAR NOT NULL,   VAL Decimal NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE CURRENCIES (      ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,      CODE NCHAR NOT NULL,      NAME TEXT NOT NULL,      SYMBOL TEXT,      COUNTRY TEXT,      ISO_CODE NCHAR NOT NULL,      UNIQUE(CODE)    );  CREATE TABLE BANKS (      ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,      NAME TEXT NOT NULL,      CODE NCHAR,      ADDRESS TEXT,      COUNTRY TEXT,      WEBSITE TEXT    );  CREATE TABLE EXCHANGE_PROVIDERS (      ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,      NAME TEXT NOT NULL,      API_URL TEXT,      LAST_UPDATED DATETIME,      ACTIVE BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1    );  CREATE TABLE TRANSACTIONS (      ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,      DATE DATETIME NOT NULL,      AMOUNT REAL NOT NULL,      FROM_CURRENCY NCHAR NOT NULL,      TO_CURRENCY NCHAR NOT NULL,      RATE REAL NOT NULL,      BANK_ID INTEGER,      USER_ID INTEGER,      PROVIDER_ID INTEGER,      FOREIGN KEY (FROM_CURRENCY) REFERENCES CURRENCIES(CODE),      FOREIGN KEY (TO_CURRENCY) REFERENCES CURRENCIES(CODE),      FOREIGN KEY (BANK_ID) REFERENCES BANKS(ID),      FOREIGN KEY (USER_ID) REFERENCES USERS(ID),      FOREIGN KEY (PROVIDER_ID) REFERENCES EXCHANGE_PROVIDERS(ID)    );  CREATE TABLE USERS (      ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,      USERNAME TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,      EMAIL TEXT NOT NULL,      PASSWORD_HASH TEXT NOT NULL,      ROLE_ID INTEGER,      CREATED_AT DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,      FOREIGN KEY (ROLE_ID) REFERENCES ROLES(ID)    );  CREATE TABLE ROLES (      ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,      NAME TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE    );  CREATE TABLE TRANSACTIONS_LOG (      ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,      TRANSACTION_ID INTEGER NOT NULL,      ACTION TEXT NOT NULL,      ACTION_DATE DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,      USER_ID INTEGER,      DETAILS TEXT,      FOREIGN KEY (TRANSACTION_ID) REFERENCES TRANSACTIONS(ID),      FOREIGN KEY (USER_ID) REFERENCES USERS(ID)    );  CREATE TABLE ERROR_LOGS (      ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,      ERROR_CODE INTEGER,      ERROR_MESSAGE TEXT NOT NULL,      ERROR_DATE DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,      CONTEXT TEXT,      USER_ID INTEGER,      FOREIGN KEY (USER_ID) REFERENCES USERS(ID)    );  CREATE TABLE REPORT_PARAMETERS (      ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,      REPORT_NAME TEXT NOT NULL,      PARAM_KEY TEXT NOT NULL,      PARAM_VALUE TEXT,      DESCRIPTION TEXT,      CREATED_BY INTEGER,      CREATED_AT DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,      FOREIGN KEY (CREATED_BY) REFERENCES USERS(ID)    );  CREATE TABLE REPORTS (       ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,       NAME TEXT NOT NULL,       DESCRIPTION TEXT,       CREATED_AT DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,       CREATED_BY INTEGER,       LAST_RUN DATETIME,       FOREIGN KEY (CREATED_BY) REFERENCES USERS(ID)     );  CREATE TABLE ALERTS (       ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,       USER_ID INTEGER NOT NULL,       ALERT_TYPE TEXT NOT NULL,       PARAMS TEXT,       ACTIVE BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1,       CREATED_AT DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,       LAST_TRIGGERED DATETIME,       FOREIGN KEY (USER_ID) REFERENCES USERS(ID)     );  CREATE TABLE COUNTRY_INFO (       ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,       COUNTRY_NAME TEXT NOT NULL,       ISO_CODE NCHAR NOT NULL,       CURRENCY_CODE NCHAR,       REGION TEXT,       CONTINENT TEXT,       FOREIGN KEY (CURRENCY_CODE) REFERENCES CURRENCIES(CODE)     );  CREATE TABLE HISTORY_ORIGINS (       ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,       ORIGIN_NAME TEXT NOT NULL,       DESCRIPTION TEXT,       IS_DEFAULT BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0     );  CREATE TABLE DEF_XREF (       ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,       CCY NCHAR NOT NULL,       DEFAULT_ID INTEGER,       ORIGIN_ID INTEGER,       FOREIGN KEY (CCY) REFERENCES CURRENCIES(CODE),       FOREIGN KEY (DEFAULT_ID) REFERENCES DEF_CCY_XRATES(CCY),       FOREIGN KEY (ORIGIN_ID) REFERENCES HISTORY_ORIGINS(ID)     );  CREATE TABLE XREFTYPES (       ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,       TYPE_NAME TEXT NOT NULL,       DESCRIPTION TEXT     );  CREATE TABLE RATE_DIFFERENCES (       ID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,       CCY NCHAR NOT NULL,       DATE DATE NOT NULL,       DEFAULT_RATE REAL,       HISTORICAL_RATE REAL,       DIFFERENCE REAL,       DIFF_PERCENT REAL,       FOREIGN KEY (CCY) REFERENCES CURRENCIES(CODE)     );

### Question:
Identify the currencies that have been used as the FROM_CURRENCY in at least 50 transactions and the average rate used for each.
SELECT FROM_CURRENCY, AVG(RATE) AS average_rate FROM TRANSACTIONS GROUP BY FROM_CURRENCY HAVING COUNT(*) >= 50;

CREATE TABLE user_role (   userid Int NOT NULL,   roleid Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE offer_product (   offerid Int NOT NULL,   productid Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE order_status (   statusid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   statusname VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE orders (   orderid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   userid Int NOT NULL,   orderdate DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   totalamount Decimal,   statusid Int NOT NULL,   shippingaddressid Int );  CREATE TABLE shipping_address (   addressid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   addressline1 VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   addressline2 VARCHAR(255),   city VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   state VARCHAR(255),   country VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   zipcode VARCHAR(255) );  CREATE TABLE order_items (   orderitemid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   orderid Int NOT NULL,   productid Int NOT NULL,   quantity Int NOT NULL,   unitprice Decimal );  CREATE TABLE payment (   paymentid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   orderid Int NOT NULL,   payment_method VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   amount Decimal,   transaction_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE product_reviews (   reviewid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   productid Int NOT NULL,   userid Int NOT NULL,   review_text TEXT NOT NULL,   review_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE product_ratings (   ratingid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   productid Int NOT NULL,   userid Int NOT NULL,   rating_score Int CHECK(rating_score >=1 AND rating_score <=5),   rating_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE user_activity_log (   activityid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   userid Int NOT NULL,   activity_type VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   activity_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE cart_items (   cartitemid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   userid Int NOT NULL,   productid Int NOT NULL,   quantity Int NOT NULL,   added_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE wishlists (   wishlistid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   userid Int NOT NULL,   productid Int NOT NULL,   added_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE helpdesk_tickets (   ticketid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   userid Int NOT NULL,   subject VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   status VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   created_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE inventory_levels (   inventoryid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   productid Int NOT NULL,   stock_quantity Int NOT NULL,   last_updated DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE discounts (   discountid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   discount_code VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   discount_type VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   discount_value Decimal NOT NULL,   is_active Boolean NOT NULL,   productid Int );  CREATE TABLE audit_log (   logid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   userid Int NOT NULL,   action_description TEXT NOT NULL,   action_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE notifications (   notificationid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   userid Int NOT NULL,   message TEXT NOT NULL,   is_read Boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE user_preferences (   preferenceid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   userid Int NOT NULL,   preference_key VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   preference_value TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE INDEX idx_orders_userid ON orders (userid);  CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_orderid ON order_items (orderid);  CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_productid ON order_items (productid);  CREATE INDEX idx_payment_orderid ON payment (orderid);  CREATE INDEX idx_product_reviews_productid ON product_reviews (productid);  CREATE INDEX idx_product_reviews_userid ON product_reviews (userid);  CREATE INDEX idx_product_ratings_productid ON product_ratings (productid);  CREATE INDEX idx_product_ratings_userid ON product_ratings (userid);  CREATE INDEX idx_user_activity_log_userid ON user_activity_log (userid);  CREATE INDEX idx_cart_items_userid ON cart_items (userid);  CREATE INDEX idx_cart_items_productid ON cart_items (productid);  CREATE INDEX idx_wishlists_userid ON wishlists (userid);  CREATE INDEX idx_wishlists_productid ON wishlists (productid);  CREATE INDEX idx_helpdesk_tickets_userid ON helpdesk_tickets (userid);  CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_levels_productid ON inventory_levels (productid);  CREATE INDEX idx_discounts_productid ON discounts (productid);  CREATE INDEX idx_audit_log_userid ON audit_log (userid);  CREATE INDEX idx_notifications_userid ON notifications (userid);  CREATE INDEX idx_user_preferences_userid ON user_preferences (userid);

SELECT d.discount_code, d.productid, il.stock_quantity FROM discounts d JOIN inventory_levels il ON d.productid = il.productid;

### Question:
List the names of users who have a task assignment along with the corresponding activity names they are assigned to.
SELECT UP.FIRST_NAME || ' ' || UP.LAST_NAME AS NAME, A.NAME AS ACTIVITY_NAME FROM TASK_ASSIGNMENT TA JOIN USER_PROFILE UP ON TA.USER_ID = UP.USER_ID JOIN ACTIVITY A ON TA.ACTIVITY_ID = A.ID;

 CREATE TABLE "examresults" (   "Id" integer PRIMARY KEY,   "ExamId" integer NOT NULL,   "CandidateId" Varchar NOT NULL,   "Date" datetime NOT NULL,   "Score" integer NOT NULL,   "IsPass" integer NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE language (   id integer PRIMARY KEY,   name Varchar,   shortname Varchar );  CREATE TABLE candidate (   "Id" Varchar PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   "Email" Varchar NOT NULL,   "PasswordHash" Varchar,   "IsLocked" integer NOT NULL,   "Phone" Varchar,   "LoginIPAddress" Varchar,   "FailedAttempts" integer NOT NULL,   "LastActivity" datetime );  CREATE TABLE notifications (   id integer PRIMARY KEY,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   message Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at datetime,   is_read integer,   user_id Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES aspnetuserlogins (UserId) );  CREATE TABLE status (   id integer PRIMARY KEY,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   sortorder integer NOT NULL,   isActive integer );  CREATE TABLE "questions" (   "Id" integer PRIMARY KEY,   "Text" Varchar NOT NULL,   "Option1" Varchar,   "Option2" Varchar,   "Option3" Varchar,   "Option4" Varchar,   "Answer" Varchar,   "Explanation" Varchar,   "IsCorrect" integer NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE "payment" (   "Id" integer PRIMARY KEY,   "PaymentMethodId" integer NOT NULL,   "Amount" integer NOT NULL,   "DateTime" datetime NOT NULL,   "Description" Varchar,   "Status" integer NOT NULL,   "EnrollmentId" integer NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (PaymentMethodId) REFERENCES paymentmethod (Id),   FOREIGN KEY (EnrollmentId) REFERENCES enrollments (Id) );  CREATE TABLE clientlocation (   clientId Varchar NOT NULL,   locationId integer NOT NULL,   arrivalDate datetime,   leaveDate datetime,   PRIMARY KEY (clientId, locationId),   FOREIGN KEY (clientId) REFERENCES clients (Id),   FOREIGN KEY (locationId) REFERENCES locations (id) ); ### Question:
List the names of all available exams that have a passing score higher than their default passing score threshold and occur within the next 30 days.
SELECT "Name" FROM "exams" WHERE "PassingScore" > "ByDefault" AND "Date" BETWEEN datetime('now') AND datetime('now', '+30 days');

CREATE TABLE estado (   registro_estado Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   tipo_estado Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE tratamiento (   codigo_tratamiento Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   tipo_tratamiento Varchar NOT NULL,   efectividad Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE ubicacion (   registro_ubicacion Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   direccion Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE contacto_fisico (   codigo_contacto Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   tipo_concacto Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE hospital (   codigo_sanitario Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nombre Varchar NOT NULL,   direccion Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE asociado (   numero_asociado Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nombre Varchar NOT NULL,   apellido Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE victima (   numero_victima Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nombre_victima Varchar NOT NULL,   apellido Varchar NOT NULL,   direccion_victima Varchar NOT NULL,   fecha_primera_sospecha Datetime NOT NULL,   registro_estado Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (registro_estado) REFERENCES estado (registro_estado) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE asocio_victima (   numero_asociacion Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   fecha_conocio Datetime NOT NULL,   numero_asociado Int NOT NULL,   numero_victima Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (numero_asociado) REFERENCES asociado (numero_asociado),   FOREIGN KEY (numero_victima) REFERENCES victima (numero_victima) );  CREATE TABLE contacto_socio_victima (   numero_asociacion Int NOT NULL,   codigo_contacto Int NOT NULL,   fecha_inicio_contacto Datetime,   fecha_fin_contacto Datetime,   FOREIGN KEY (numero_asociacion) REFERENCES asocio_victima (numero_asociacion),   FOREIGN KEY (codigo_contacto) REFERENCES contacto_fisico (codigo_contacto) );  CREATE TABLE paciente_hospital (   numero_paciente Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   numero_victima Int NOT NULL,   codigo_sanitario Int NOT NULL,   fecha_confirmacion Datetime,   fecha_muerte Datetime,   FOREIGN KEY (numero_victima) REFERENCES victima (numero_victima),   FOREIGN KEY (codigo_sanitario) REFERENCES hospital (codigo_sanitario) );  CREATE TABLE paciente_tratamiento (   numero_paciente Int NOT NULL,   codigo_tratamiento Int NOT NULL,   fecha_inicio_tratamiento Datetime NOT NULL,   fecha_fin_tratamiento Datetime NOT NULL,   efectividad_tratamiento Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (codigo_tratamiento) REFERENCES tratamiento (codigo_tratamiento),   FOREIGN KEY (numero_paciente) REFERENCES paciente_hospital (numero_paciente) );  CREATE TABLE bitacora_victima (   bitacora Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   numero_paciente Int NOT NULL,   registro_ubicacion Int NOT NULL,   fecha_llegada Datetime,   fecha_salida Datetime,   FOREIGN KEY (numero_paciente) REFERENCES paciente_hospital (numero_paciente),   FOREIGN KEY (registro_ubicacion) REFERENCES ubicacion (registro_ubicacion) ); ### Question:
List the names of victims and their associated individuals who had physical contact with them, including the type of contact, the hospitals where they were treated, and the treatments they received, along with the effectiveness of those treatments.

SELECT      v.nombre_victima || ' ' || v.apellido AS victim_name,     a.nombre || ' ' || a.apellido AS associated_individual,     cf.tipo_concacto,     h.nombre,     t.tipo_tratamiento,     pt.efectividad_tratamiento FROM victima v JOIN asocio_victima av ON v.numero_victima = av.numero_victima JOIN asociado a ON av.numero_asociado = a.numero_asociado JOIN contacto_socio_victima csv ON av.numero_asociacion = csv.numero_asociacion JOIN contacto_fisico cf ON csv.codigo_contacto = cf.codigo_contacto JOIN paciente_hospital ph ON v.numero_victima = ph.numero_victima JOIN hospital h ON ph.codigo_sanitario = h.codigo_sanitario JOIN paciente_tratamiento pt ON ph.numero_paciente = pt.numero_paciente JOIN tratamiento t ON pt.codigo_tratamiento = t.codigo_tratamiento;

CREATE TABLE employee (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   first_name Varchar NOT NULL,   last_name Varchar NOT NULL,   job_id Int NOT NULL,   leader_id Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (leader_id) REFERENCES employee (id) );  CREATE TABLE role (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   subtitle Varchar NOT NULL,   rate Decimal NOT NULL,   branch_id Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE department (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE project (   id Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   department_id Int NOT NULL,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   start_date DATE,   end_date DATE,   FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES department(id) );  CREATE TABLE department_branch (   id Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   department_id Int NOT NULL,   branch_id Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES department(id),   FOREIGN KEY (branch_id) REFERENCES branch(id) );  CREATE TABLE employee_department (   employee_id Int NOT NULL,   department_id Int NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE,   PRIMARY KEY (employee_id, department_id) );  CREATE TABLE employee_role_history (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   role_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id),   FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES role(id) );  CREATE TABLE equipment (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   type TEXT NOT NULL,   location_branch_id INTEGER,   FOREIGN KEY (location_branch_id) REFERENCES branch(id) );  CREATE TABLE equipment_assignment (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   equipment_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   assign_date DATE NOT NULL,   return_date DATE,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id),   FOREIGN KEY (equipment_id) REFERENCES equipment(id) );  CREATE TABLE training_program (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   title TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE employee_training (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   training_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   completion_date DATE NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id),   FOREIGN KEY (training_id) REFERENCES training_program(id) );  CREATE TABLE performance_review (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   review_date DATE NOT NULL,   rating INTEGER NOT NULL,   comments TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id) );  CREATE TABLE salary_history (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   amount NUMERIC NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id) );  CREATE TABLE attendance (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   date DATE NOT NULL,   status INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id) );  CREATE INDEX idx_project_department_id ON project (department_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_department_branch_department_id ON department_branch (department_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_department_branch_branch_id ON department_branch (branch_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_employee_role_history_employee_id ON employee_role_history (employee_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_employee_role_history_role_id ON employee_role_history (role_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_equipment_location_branch_id ON equipment (location_branch_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_equipment_assignment_employee_id ON equipment_assignment (employee_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_equipment_assignment_equipment_id ON equipment_assignment (equipment_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_employee_training_employee_id ON employee_training (employee_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_employee_training_training_id ON employee_training (training_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_performance_review_employee_id ON performance_review (employee_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_salary_history_employee_id ON salary_history (employee_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_attendance_employee_id ON attendance (employee_id);

### Question:
How many employees have had their performance reviews rated above 8 and have also participated in at least three different training programs in the last year?

SELECT COUNT(*)  FROM (     SELECT pr.employee_id     FROM performance_review pr     JOIN employee_training et ON pr.employee_id = et.employee_id     GROUP BY pr.employee_id     HAVING pr.rating > 8        AND COUNT(DISTINCT et.training_id) >= 3 );

### Question:
Which teams have at least 7 members who have earned the "Data Visionary" badge, and what is the total number of users in each team?

WITH EligibleTeams AS (     SELECT t.team_id     FROM user_to_team t     JOIN user_to_badge b ON t.user_id = b.user_id     JOIN badge d ON b.badge_id = d.id     WHERE d.name = 'Data Visionary'     GROUP BY t.team_id     HAVING COUNT(*) >= 7 ) SELECT team.name, COUNT(t.user_id) AS total_users FROM EligibleTeams e JOIN team ON e.team_id = team.id JOIN user_to_team t ON e.team_id = t.team_id GROUP BY team.id, team.name;

### Question:
For each DeviceId in the PDAUsageLog with a UsageStart between July 1, 2024, and September 1, 2024, determine the average RawValue from SensorData for SensorType 'Temperature' recorded during active usage sessions (where UsageEnd is after the SensorData.RecordedAt), and also count the number of related DeviceError entries with ErrorCode 1003, considering only those PDAs that have had at least two CalibrationRecord entries with Result 'Pass' since January 1, 2024.

SELECT      pu.DeviceId,     AVG(sd.RawValue) AS AverageTemperature,     COUNT(de.Id) AS ErrorCount FROM      dbo_PDAUsageLog pu INNER JOIN      dbo_SensorData sd     ON pu.DeviceId = sd.DeviceId     AND sd.SensorType = 'Temperature'     AND sd.RecordedAt BETWEEN pu.UsageStart AND pu.UsageEnd LEFT JOIN      dbo_DeviceError de     ON pu.DeviceId = de.DeviceId     AND de.ErrorCode = 1003     AND de.ErrorTimestamp <= sd.RecordedAt WHERE      pu.UsageStart >= '2024-07-01'     AND pu.UsageStart < '2024-09-01'     AND pu.DeviceId IN (         SELECT              cr.DeviceId          FROM              dbo_CalibrationRecord cr         WHERE              cr.CalibrationDate >= '2024-01-01'             AND cr.Result = 'Pass'         GROUP BY              cr.DeviceId         HAVING              COUNT(*) >= 2     ) GROUP BY      pu.DeviceId;

### Question:
Which box has the most recent expiration date for items of type 'perishable' and what is the average quantity of those items?

WITH max_expiry AS (     SELECT MAX(be.expiration_date) AS max_date     FROM box_expirations be     JOIN box_items bi ON be.item_id = bi.id     JOIN item_types it ON bi.item_type = it.id     WHERE it.type_name = 'perishable' ) SELECT      bi.box_id,     AVG(bi.quantity) AS avg_quantity FROM box_expirations be JOIN box_items bi ON be.item_id = bi.id JOIN item_types it ON bi.item_type = it.id JOIN max_expiry me ON be.expiration_date = me.max_date WHERE it.type_name = 'perishable' GROUP BY bi.box_id;

CREATE TABLE Course (   course_id Char NOT NULL,   division TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'NA' );  CREATE TABLE Department (   department_id Char NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Char NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Slot (   letter TEXT NOT NULL,   day TEXT NOT NULL,   start_time Time NOT NULL,   end_time Time NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Room (   room_number Char NOT NULL UNIQUE,   location TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE ScheduledIn (   course_id Char NOT NULL,   course_division TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'NA',   department Char NOT NULL,   slot_letter TEXT NOT NULL,   slot_day TEXT NOT NULL,   room Char NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (course_id, course_division) REFERENCES Course (course_id, division) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (department) REFERENCES Department (department_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (slot_letter, slot_day) REFERENCES Slot (letter, day) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (room) REFERENCES Room (room_number) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

### Question:
Which courses are scheduled in rooms located in the 'Main Building' on 'Monday'?

SELECT DISTINCT c.course_id, c.division FROM Course c JOIN ScheduledIn s ON c.course_id = s.course_id AND c.division = s.course_division JOIN Room r ON s.room = r.room_number WHERE r.location = 'Main Building' AND s.slot_day = 'Monday';

### Question:
Investigate the frequency of remote actions executed by users on devices assigned to them, focusing on actions that resulted in system status changes, and determine if there's a correlation between the execution of these actions and subsequent maintenance logs for the same devices.

SELECT      ra.target_device_id,     COUNT(*) AS status_change_actions,     COUNT(ml.id) AS maintenance_actions_after FROM      remote_actions ra LEFT JOIN      maintenance_logs ml     ON ra.target_device_id = ml.device_id      AND ml.performed_at > ra.action_request WHERE      ra.execution_result LIKE '%status change%' GROUP BY      ra.target_device_id;

Which student has the highest number of class attendances recorded for the course "Advanced Machine Learning" and what is their attendance percentage?

SELECT      s.student_id,     s.studentname,     COUNT(CASE WHEN a.status = 'Present' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 /          (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM class_schedule WHERE course_id = c.Cid) AS attendance_percentage FROM      attendance a JOIN      course c ON a.course_id = c.Cid JOIN      student s ON a.student_id = s.student_id WHERE      c.coursename = 'Advanced Machine Learning' GROUP BY      s.student_id, s.studentname ORDER BY      attendance_percentage DESC LIMIT 1;

### Question:
Identify the model versions and their associated data lineage steps that were used to derive datasets with a null ratio greater than 0.2.

SELECT mv.model_version_id, mv.model_name, mv.version_number, dl.transformation_step FROM data_quality dq JOIN data_lineage dl ON dq.dataset_id = dl.derived_dataset_id JOIN model_training_data mtd ON dq.dataset_id = mtd.source_dataset_id JOIN model_validation_metrics mvm ON mtd.pipeline_run_id = mvm.pipeline_run_id JOIN model_versions mv ON mvm.model_version_id = mv.model_version_id WHERE dq.null_ratio > 0.2;

### Question:
Show the number of support tickets per month for users who have a friend suspended for 'spam', have at least one item rating flagged for 'inappropriate content', and have used a browser other than Chrome.

SELECT     strftime('%Y-%m', created_at, 'start of month') AS month,     COUNT(*) AS support_ticket_count FROM     support_ticket WHERE     user_id IN (         SELECT             uf.user_id         FROM             user_friend uf         JOIN             user_suspend us ON uf.friend_user_id = us.user_id         WHERE             us.reason = 'spam'     )     AND user_id IN (         SELECT             ir.user_id         FROM             item_rating ir         JOIN             item_report irp ON ir.item_id = irp.item_id         WHERE             irp.report_type = 'inappropriate content'     )     AND user_id IN (         SELECT             ud.user_id         FROM             user_device ud         WHERE             ud.browser NOT IN ('Chrome')     ) GROUP BY     strftime('%Y-%m', created_at, 'start of month') ORDER BY     month;

### Question:
Which activity has the highest number of logged attendance records where members were marked as absent?
SELECT activity.name FROM attendance JOIN activity ON attendance.activity_id = activity.id WHERE attendance.present = 0 GROUP BY activity.id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;

CREATE TABLE user (   uid Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   username Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   password Varchar NOT NULL,   role Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE task (   tid Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   customer_id Int NOT NULL,   driver_id Int,   pickup_time Datetime NOT NULL,   deliver_time Datetime NOT NULL,   pickup_code Int NOT NULL,   deliver_code Int NOT NULL,   remarks Text,   contact_number Int,   status Int NOT NULL,   post_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES user (uid) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE payment (   pid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   task_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   amount REAL NOT NULL,   payment_time DATETIME NOT NULL,   method TEXT NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (task_id) REFERENCES task(tid) ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE NO ACTION );  CREATE TABLE driver_salary (   salary_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   driver_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   base_salary REAL,   bonus_amount REAL,   total_gross REAL,   period_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (driver_id) REFERENCES user(uid) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE document (   doc_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   doc_type TEXT NOT NULL,   file_path TEXT NOT NULL,   upload_time DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(uid) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE location (   lid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   task_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   pickup_lat REAL,   pickup_lng REAL,   deliver_lat REAL,   deliver_lng REAL,   FOREIGN KEY (task_id) REFERENCES task(tid) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE vehicle_insurance (   insurance_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   vehicle_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   policy_number TEXT NOT NULL,   provider TEXT NOT NULL,   annual_cost REAL,   expiry_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (vehicle_id) REFERENCES vehicle(vid) ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE NO ACTION ); ### Question:
Calculate the average fuel cost per task for each vehicle make, but only include vehicles with valid insurance, at least two recorded fuel log entries, and no pending maintenance or oil change reminders in the last 12 months.

WITH qualified_vehicles AS (     SELECT          v.make,         v.vid,         SUM(fl.cost) AS total_fuel_cost,         (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM task WHERE driver_id = v.driver_id) AS num_tasks     FROM vehicle v     JOIN vehicle_insurance ins ON v.vid = ins.vehicle_id     JOIN fuel_log fl ON v.vid = fl.vehicle_id     WHERE ins.expiry_date > date('now')     GROUP BY v.vid     HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ) SELECT      make,     AVG(total_fuel_cost / num_tasks) AS average_fuel_per_task FROM qualified_vehicles WHERE vid NOT IN (     SELECT mr.vehicle_id     FROM maintenance_reminder mr     WHERE status = 'pending'     AND service_type IN ('maintenance', 'oil change')     AND reminder_date >= date('now', '-12 months') ) GROUP BY make;

### Question:
What are the names and versions of apps that have at least one user who also has a feature usage record for "offline mode" and have a security rating higher than 3?

SELECT n.name, n.version FROM app_new n JOIN app_security s ON n.app_id = s.app_id WHERE s.security_rating > 3   AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM user_app ua     JOIN app_feature_usage f ON ua.app_id = f.app_id AND ua.uid = f.uid     WHERE ua.app_id = n.app_id       AND f.feature_name = 'offline mode'   );

### Question:
What is the average study session duration for users with a mobile_app_usage session count over 15 and who have at least one content_read_history entry for 'tutorial' content type in the last week?

SELECT AVG(s.duration_minutes) FROM study_session_stats s WHERE EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM mobile_app_usage m     WHERE m.user_id = s.user_id     AND m.session_count > 15 ) AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM content_read_history c     WHERE c.user_id = s.user_id     AND c.content_type = 'tutorial'     AND c.last_read_time >= date('2023-10-21', '-7 days') );

 CREATE TABLE WishlistItem (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   wishlist_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   product_variant_id Char NOT NULL,   quantity Integer NOT NULL CHECK (quantity > 0),   added_at INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (wishlist_id) REFERENCES Wishlist (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (product_variant_id) REFERENCES ProductVariant (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ); ### Question:
What are the distinct covered texts associated with entities that have spans starting with the letter 'E', and how do their corresponding entity IDs relate to other entities that share the same covered text but have different spans? Identify the relationships by comparing the spans and entity IDs across all matching pairs.

WITH covered_texts_with_E AS (     SELECT DISTINCT covered_text     FROM text_mined_assertions_entity     WHERE span LIKE 'E%' ) SELECT e1.covered_text, e1.entity_id AS entity_id1, e1.span AS span1, e2.entity_id AS entity_id2, e2.span AS span2 FROM text_mined_assertions_entity e1 JOIN text_mined_assertions_entity e2 ON e1.covered_text = e2.covered_text WHERE e1.span != e2.span AND e1.covered_text IN (SELECT covered_text FROM covered_texts_with_E);

### Question:
For users with at least three friends and a 'dark' theme preference, list their account name, the count of friends, the most common board game type they have participated in, and the count of game sessions they have organized. Include only users with at least one organized game session.

WITH user_friend_counts AS (     SELECT UserID, COUNT(*) AS friend_count     FROM (         SELECT User1ID AS UserID FROM user_friends         UNION ALL         SELECT User2ID AS UserID FROM user_friends     )     GROUP BY UserID     HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3 ), user_organized_sessions AS (     SELECT OrganizerID      FROM game_sessions      GROUP BY OrganizerID ), user_game_participation AS (     SELECT          OrganizerID AS UserID,         GameTypeID,         COUNT(*) AS participation_count     FROM game_sessions     GROUP BY OrganizerID, GameTypeID     UNION ALL     SELECT          ea.UserID,         gs.GameTypeID,         COUNT(*) AS participation_count     FROM event_attendance ea     JOIN calendar_event ce ON ea.EventID = ce.id     JOIN game_sessions gs ON ce.id = gs.SessionID     GROUP BY ea.UserID, gs.GameTypeID ) SELECT      u.Account,     f.friend_count,     (SELECT GameTypeID FROM (         SELECT GameTypeID, participation_count         FROM user_game_participation         WHERE UserID = u.ID         ORDER BY participation_count DESC         LIMIT 1     )) AS most_common_game_type,     COUNT(gs.SessionID) AS organized_sessions_count FROM users u JOIN user_preferences p ON u.ID = p.UserID JOIN user_friend_counts f ON u.ID = f.UserID JOIN user_organized_sessions o ON u.ID = o.OrganizerID LEFT JOIN game_sessions gs ON u.ID = gs.OrganizerID WHERE p.ThemePreference = 'dark' GROUP BY u.ID, u.Account, f.friend_count;

CREATE TABLE articles (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   description Text NOT NULL,   content Text NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   modified_at Timestamp,   deleted_at Timestamp,   slug Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id) );  CREATE TABLE ratings (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   article_id TEXT NOT NULL,   rating Int NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id) );  CREATE TABLE users (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   password Varchar NOT NULL,   slug Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id) );

### Question:
What is the average rating given to articles that were created in the last month, grouped by the user who wrote them?

SELECT articles.user_id, AVG(ratings.rating) AS average_rating FROM articles JOIN ratings ON articles.id = ratings.article_id WHERE articles.created_at >= datetime('now', '-1 month') GROUP BY articles.user_id;

### Question:
For each supplier, calculate the average delivery time (in days) of their orders, and then identify the top 3 suppliers with the highest average delivery time. Filter the results to include only those suppliers who have at least 5 orders delivered and whose supplier service reviews have an average rating of more than 4 stars. Include the supplier's name, average delivery time, and count of orders delivered in the final output.

WITH supplier_orders AS (     SELECT          Supplier_ID,         AVG(JULIANDAY(DeliveryDate) - JULIANDAY(OrderDate)) AS avg_delivery,         COUNT(*) AS orders_count     FROM ORDERS     GROUP BY Supplier_ID     HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5 ), supplier_quality AS (     SELECT          Supplier_ID,         AVG(CAST(REPLACE(QualityRating, '"', '') AS REAL)) AS avg_quality     FROM SUPPLIER_PERFORMANCE     GROUP BY Supplier_ID     HAVING AVG(CAST(REPLACE(QualityRating, '"', '') AS REAL)) > 4 ) SELECT      s.S_Name AS SupplierName,     so.avg_delivery AS AverageDeliveryTime,     so.orders_count AS OrdersDelivered FROM supplier_orders so JOIN supplier_quality sq ON so.Supplier_ID = sq.Supplier_ID JOIN SUPPLIER s ON so.Supplier_ID = s.Supplier_ID ORDER BY so.avg_delivery DESC LIMIT 3;

### Question:
Identify the users whose most recent check-in was in the last 7 days and who have at least one event they are attending soon.

SELECT u.id_usuario FROM usuarios u WHERE (     SELECT MAX(checkin_time)     FROM check_ins     WHERE user_id = u.id_usuario ) > datetime('now', '-7 days') AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM event_attendants ea     JOIN events e ON ea.event_id = e.event_id     WHERE ea.user_id = u.id_usuario     AND e.event_date > datetime('now')     AND ea.rsvp_status = 'attending' );

### Question:
Identify the guest with the highest number of check-ins and provide their name, email, and total number of check-ins.
SELECT h.nome, h.email, COUNT(*) AS total_checkins FROM hospede h JOIN checkin_checkout cc ON h.nTelefone = cc.n_hospede GROUP BY h.nTelefone ORDER BY total_checkins DESC LIMIT 1;

### Question:
List the sources that have a 'technology' category and an average coverage score above 0.85, along with the count of arguments they have.

SELECT      s.id,     s.domain,     AVG(sc.coverageScore) AS average_coverage,     COUNT(a.id) AS argument_count FROM Source s JOIN SourceCategory c ON s.id = c.sourceId AND c.categoryName = 'technology' JOIN SourceCoverage sc ON s.id = sc.sourceId LEFT JOIN Argument a ON s.id = a.sourceId GROUP BY s.id HAVING average_coverage > 0.85;

CREATE TABLE department (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE role (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   salary Decimal NOT NULL,   department_id Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES department (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE employee (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   first_name Varchar NOT NULL,   last_name Varchar NOT NULL,   role_id Int NOT NULL,   manager_id Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES role (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (manager_id) REFERENCES employee (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE project (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE,   department_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   manager_id INTEGER,   FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES department(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (manager_id) REFERENCES employee(id) ON DELETE SET NULL );  CREATE TABLE project_employee (   project_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   hours_worked DECIMAL DEFAULT 0,   role_in_project VARCHAR,   PRIMARY KEY (project_id, employee_id),   FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES project(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE training (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   date_completed DATE NOT NULL,   certificate_url VARCHAR,   employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE performance_review (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,   employee_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   reviewer_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   review_date DATE NOT NULL,   rating INTEGER CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5),   comments TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (reviewer_id) REFERENCES employee(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );

### Question:
List all employees and their assigned projects where the project's manager is also their direct manager in their role.
SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, p.name AS project_name FROM employee e JOIN project_employee pe ON e.id = pe.employee_id JOIN project p ON pe.project_id = p.id WHERE p.manager_id = e.manager_id;

### Question:
Identify the users whose login attempts have failed more than twice in the last month, have at least one role with permissions related to financial access, and have provided feedback with a rating below 3, along with their primary payment card types and the total invoice amounts due in their current billing cycle.

WITH FailedLogins AS (     SELECT userId, COUNT(*) AS failCount     FROM LoginAttempts     WHERE success = 0         AND attemptTime >= DATE('now', '-1 month')     GROUP BY userId     HAVING failCount > 2 ), UsersWithFinancialRoles AS (     SELECT DISTINCT r.userId     FROM RolePermissions p     JOIN UserRoles r ON p.roleId = r.roleId     WHERE p.permission LIKE '%financial%' ), UsersWithPoorFeedback AS (     SELECT userId     FROM Feedbacks     WHERE rating < 3 ), CurrentSubscriberInvoices AS (     SELECT s.userId, SUM(i.amount) AS totalAmountDue     FROM Subscriptions s     JOIN BillingCycles b ON s.subscriptionId = b.subscriptionId     JOIN Invoices i ON s.subscriptionId = i.subscriptionId     WHERE b.periodEnd >= DATE('now')       AND i.paid = 0     GROUP BY s.userId ), PrimaryPaymentCard AS (     SELECT userId, cardType     FROM (         SELECT userId, cardType,             ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY userId ORDER BY cardType) AS rn         FROM PaymentMethods     )     WHERE rn = 1 ) SELECT u.userId, u.name, p.cardType, c.totalAmountDue FROM Users u JOIN FailedLogins f ON u.userId = f.userId JOIN UsersWithFinancialRoles fr ON u.userId = fr.userId JOIN UsersWithPoorFeedback fb ON u.userId = fb.userId JOIN CurrentSubscriberInvoices c ON u.userId = c.userId JOIN PrimaryPaymentCard p ON u.userId = p.userId;

CREATE TABLE s_customer (   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   customer_name TEXT NOT NULL,   phone TEXT NOT NULL,   address TEXT NOT NULL,   city TEXT NOT NULL,   state TEXT NOT NULL,   country TEXT NOT NULL,   zip_code TEXT NOT NULL,   credit_rating TEXT NOT NULL,   sales_rep_id TEXT NOT NULL,   region_id TEXT NOT NULL,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   preferred_customer TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'N',   shipping_method TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'M',   FOREIGN KEY (sales_rep_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id),   FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_department (   department_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   department_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   region_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_employee (   employee_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   employee_last_name TEXT NOT NULL,   employee_first_name TEXT NOT NULL,   userid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   start_date Date NOT NULL,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   manager_id TEXT NOT NULL,   title TEXT NOT NULL,   department_id TEXT NOT NULL,   salary TEXT NOT NULL,   commission_pct TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (manager_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id),   FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES s_department (department_id),   FOREIGN KEY (title) REFERENCES s_title (title) );  CREATE TABLE s_inventory (   product_id TEXT NOT NULL,   warehouse_id TEXT NOT NULL,   amount_in_stock TEXT NOT NULL,   reorder_point TEXT NOT NULL,   max_in_stock TEXT NOT NULL,   out_of_stock_explanation TEXT NOT NULL,   restock_date Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id),   FOREIGN KEY (warehouse_id) REFERENCES s_warehouse (warehouse_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_item (   order_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   item_id TEXT NOT NULL,   product_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   price TEXT NOT NULL,   quantity TEXT NOT NULL,   quantity_shipped TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES s_order (order_id),   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_longtext (   longtext_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   use_filename TEXT NOT NULL,   filename TEXT NOT NULL,   text TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE s_order (   order_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   date_ordered Date NOT NULL,   date_shipped Date NOT NULL,   sales_rep_id TEXT NOT NULL,   total TEXT NOT NULL,   payment_type TEXT NOT NULL,   order_filled TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES s_customer (customer_id),   FOREIGN KEY (sales_rep_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_product (   product_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   product_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   short_desc TEXT NOT NULL,   longtext_id TEXT NOT NULL,   image_id TEXT NOT NULL,   suggested_wholesale_price TEXT NOT NULL,   wholesale_units TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (longtext_id) REFERENCES s_longtext (longtext_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_region (   region_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   region_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE s_title (   title TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE s_warehouse (   warehouse_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   region_id TEXT NOT NULL,   phone TEXT NOT NULL,   address TEXT NOT NULL,   city TEXT NOT NULL,   state TEXT NOT NULL,   country TEXT NOT NULL,   zip_code TEXT NOT NULL,   manager_id TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (manager_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id),   FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_supplier (   supplier_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   supplier_name TEXT NOT NULL,   contact_name TEXT NOT NULL,   phone TEXT NOT NULL,   address TEXT NOT NULL,   city TEXT NOT NULL,   state TEXT NOT NULL,   country TEXT NOT NULL,   zip_code TEXT NOT NULL,   region_id TEXT,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_purchase_order (   purchase_order_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   supplier_id TEXT NOT NULL,   order_date DATE NOT NULL,   expected_delivery_date DATE NOT NULL,   total_amount TEXT NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (supplier_id) REFERENCES s_supplier (supplier_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_shipper (   shipper_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   shipper_name TEXT NOT NULL,   phone TEXT NOT NULL,   address TEXT NOT NULL,   region_id TEXT,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_order_shipping (   order_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   shipper_id TEXT NOT NULL,   tracking_number TEXT NOT NULL,   shipping_cost TEXT NOT NULL,   expected_delivery_date DATE NOT NULL,   actual_delivery_date DATE,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES s_order (order_id),   FOREIGN KEY (shipper_id) REFERENCES s_shipper (shipper_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_customer_return (   return_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   order_id TEXT NOT NULL,   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   return_date DATE NOT NULL,   total_amount TEXT NOT NULL,   reason_id TEXT NOT NULL,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES s_order (order_id),   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES s_customer (customer_id),   FOREIGN KEY (reason_id) REFERENCES s_return_reason (reason_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_return_reason (   reason_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   reason_name TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE s_employee_shift (   shift_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,   shift_start DATE NOT NULL,   shift_end DATE NOT NULL,   location TEXT NOT NULL,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_employee_training (   training_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,   training_topic TEXT NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE NOT NULL,   completion_status TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_product_rating (   product_id TEXT NOT NULL,   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   rating_date DATE NOT NULL,   rating_value TEXT NOT NULL,   comment TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id),   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES s_customer (customer_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_campaign (   campaign_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   campaign_name TEXT NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE NOT NULL,   target_region_id TEXT,   budget TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (target_region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_promotion (   promotion_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   product_id TEXT NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE NOT NULL,   discount_percent TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_payment_method (   method_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   method_name TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE s_invoice (   invoice_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   order_id TEXT NOT NULL,   issue_date DATE NOT NULL,   due_date DATE NOT NULL,   total_amount TEXT NOT NULL,   payment_status TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES s_order (order_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_discount (   discount_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   product_id TEXT NOT NULL,   customer_id TEXT,   discount_amount TEXT NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id),   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES s_customer (customer_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_inventory_audit (   audit_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   product_id TEXT NOT NULL,   warehouse_id TEXT NOT NULL,   audit_date DATE NOT NULL,   old_stock TEXT NOT NULL,   new_stock TEXT NOT NULL,   reason TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id),   FOREIGN KEY (warehouse_id) REFERENCES s_warehouse (warehouse_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_system_log (   log_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   log_date DATE NOT NULL,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   action TEXT NOT NULL,   details TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES s_employee (userid) );  CREATE TABLE s_website_content_update (     update_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,     page_url TEXT NOT NULL,     update_date DATE NOT NULL,     updated_by_employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,     summary TEXT NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (updated_by_employee_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id) );

### Question:
What is the average suggested wholesale price of products that have at least one customer return within the last six months?

SELECT AVG(CAST(p.suggested_wholesale_price AS REAL)) FROM s_product p WHERE EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM s_customer_return cr     JOIN s_item i ON cr.order_id = i.order_id     WHERE cr.return_date >= DATE('now', '-6 months')     AND p.product_id = i.product_id );

### Schema:
CREATE TABLE appointment (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   appointmentDateTime Datetime NOT NULL,   status TinyInt NOT NULL,   medicalRecordId Int NOT NULL,   nextAppointment Int NOT NULL,   appointmentMessage Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (medicalRecordId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (id),   FOREIGN KEY (nextAppointment) REFERENCES appointment (id) );  CREATE TABLE devicepatient (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   wristbandAddress Varchar NOT NULL,   deviceId Int NOT NULL,   patientId Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE foodingredient (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   animalFat Float NOT NULL,   animalProtein Float NOT NULL,   breakTimeAfternoon Float NOT NULL,   breakTimeMorning Float NOT NULL,   breakfast Float NOT NULL,   calcium Float NOT NULL,   dinner Float NOT NULL,   eatLateAtNight Float NOT NULL,   fat Float NOT NULL,   fiber Float NOT NULL,   iron Float NOT NULL,   lunch Float NOT NULL,   protein Float NOT NULL,   sodium Float NOT NULL,   starch Float NOT NULL,   vitaminB1 Float NOT NULL,   vitaminB2 Float NOT NULL,   vitaminC Float NOT NULL,   vitaminPP Float NOT NULL,   zinc Float NOT NULL,   appointmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (appointmentId) REFERENCES appointment (id) );  CREATE TABLE foodtreatment (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   advice Varchar NOT NULL,   numberOfTime Int NOT NULL,   quantitative Varchar NOT NULL,   foodId Int NOT NULL,   treatmentId Int NOT NULL,   unitName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (treatmentId) REFERENCES treatment (id) );  CREATE TABLE medicalrecord (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   endTime Datetime NOT NULL,   medicalHistory Varchar NOT NULL,   startTime Datetime NOT NULL,   status TinyInt NOT NULL,   clinicalSymptoms Varchar NOT NULL,   doctorId Int NOT NULL,   illnessId Int NOT NULL,   patientId Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE medicalrecorddata (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   collectedDate Datetime NOT NULL,   ratioCompletePractice Int NOT NULL,   type TinyInt NOT NULL,   appointmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (appointmentId) REFERENCES appointment (id) );  CREATE TABLE medicinetreatment (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   advice Varchar NOT NULL,   numberOfTime Int NOT NULL,   quantitative Int NOT NULL,   unit Varchar NOT NULL,   medicineId Int NOT NULL,   treatmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (treatmentId) REFERENCES treatment (id) );  CREATE TABLE practicetreatment (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   advice Varchar NOT NULL,   numberOfTime Int NOT NULL,   timeDuration Varchar NOT NULL,   practiceId Int NOT NULL,   treatmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (treatmentId) REFERENCES treatment (id) );  CREATE TABLE preventioncheck (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   basalMetabolicRate Int NOT NULL,   bloodPressure Int NOT NULL,   bmi Float NOT NULL,   bodyFat Float NOT NULL,   bodyWater Float NOT NULL,   heartBeat Int NOT NULL,   height Double NOT NULL,   impedance Int NOT NULL,   muscleMass Float NOT NULL,   phaseAngle Float NOT NULL,   visceralFat TinyInt NOT NULL,   waists Int NOT NULL,   weight Double NOT NULL,   appointmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (appointmentId) REFERENCES appointment (id) );  CREATE TABLE propertyrecord (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   paramMeasurementValue Varchar NOT NULL,   medicalRecordDataId Int NOT NULL,   paramMeasurementId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (medicalRecordDataId) REFERENCES medicalrecorddata (id) );  CREATE TABLE treatment (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   adviseFood Varchar NOT NULL,   adviseMedicine Varchar NOT NULL,   advisePractice Varchar NOT NULL,   caloriesBurnEveryday Int NOT NULL,   fromDate Datetime NOT NULL,   status TinyInt NOT NULL,   toDate Datetime NOT NULL,   appointmentId Int NOT NULL,   note Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (appointmentId) REFERENCES appointment (id) );  CREATE TABLE allergiesrecord (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   allergyType Varchar NOT NULL,   severity Varchar NOT NULL,   reactionDetails Varchar NOT NULL,   medicalRecordId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (medicalRecordId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (id) );  CREATE TABLE bloodtestresult (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   hemoglobin Double NOT NULL,   whiteBloodCells Double NOT NULL,   redBloodCells Double NOT NULL,   platelets Int NOT NULL,   appointmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (appointmentId) REFERENCES appointment (id) );  CREATE TABLE deviceuselog (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   usageDateTime Datetime NOT NULL,   duration Seconds NOT NULL,   deviceId Int NOT NULL,   patientId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (deviceId) REFERENCES devicepatient (deviceId),   FOREIGN KEY (patientId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (patientId) );  CREATE TABLE vitaminlevel (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   vitaminA Double NOT NULL,   vitaminD Double NOT NULL,   vitaminE Double NOT NULL,   appointmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (appointmentId) REFERENCES appointment (id) );  CREATE TABLE medicationhistory (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   drugName Varchar NOT NULL,   dosage Varchar NOT NULL,   startDate Datetime NOT NULL,   endDate Datetime NOT NULL,   patientId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (patientId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (patientId) );  CREATE TABLE prescriptionrecord (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   drugName Varchar NOT NULL,   quantity Int NOT NULL,   refillCount Int NOT NULL,   issuedDate Datetime NOT NULL,   treatmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (treatmentId) REFERENCES treatment (id) );  CREATE TABLE labtestappointment (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   testType Varchar NOT NULL,   requestedDate Datetime NOT NULL,   resultDate Datetime NOT NULL,   appointmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (appointmentId) REFERENCES appointment (id) );  CREATE TABLE electronicvitalsignal (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   heartRate Int NOT NULL,   respiratoryRate Int NOT NULL,   oxygenSaturation Int NOT NULL,   medicalRecordDataId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (medicalRecordDataId) REFERENCES medicalrecorddata (id) );  CREATE TABLE patientactivitylog (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   steps Int NOT NULL,   activeMinutes Int NOT NULL,   deviceId Int NOT NULL,   recordedDate Datetime NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (deviceId) REFERENCES devicepatient (deviceId) );  CREATE TABLE nutritionplan (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   recommendedCalories Int NOT NULL,   mealPlanText Varchar NOT NULL,   startDate Datetime NOT NULL,   endDate Datetime NOT NULL,   treatmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (treatmentId) REFERENCES treatment (id) );  CREATE TABLE patientinsurance (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   providerName Varchar NOT NULL,   policyNumber Varchar NOT NULL,   coverageType Varchar NOT NULL,   expirationDate Datetime NOT NULL,   patientId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (patientId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (patientId) );  CREATE TABLE bedassignment (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   roomNumber Int NOT NULL,   bedNumber Int NOT NULL,   assignedDate Datetime NOT NULL,   releasedDate Datetime NOT NULL,   medicalRecordId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (medicalRecordId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (id) );  CREATE TABLE appointmentreminder (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   reminderType Varchar NOT NULL,   sentDateTime Datetime NOT NULL,   status TinyInt NOT NULL,   appointmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (appointmentId) REFERENCES appointment (id) );  CREATE TABLE treatmentprogress (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   notes Varchar NOT NULL,   progressPercentage Int NOT NULL,   reviewedDate Datetime NOT NULL,   treatmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (treatmentId) REFERENCES treatment (id) );  CREATE TABLE vitalsummary (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   systolic Int NOT NULL,   diastolic Int NOT NULL,   temperature Double NOT NULL,   appointmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (appointmentId) REFERENCES appointment (id) );  CREATE TABLE diseasemanagementplan (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   planName Varchar NOT NULL,   startDatetime Datetime NOT NULL,   endDatetime Datetime NOT NULL,   illnessId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (illnessId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (illnessId) );  CREATE TABLE doctoravailability (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   availableStart Datetime NOT NULL,   availableEnd Datetime NOT NULL,   isBooked TinyInt NOT NULL,   doctorId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (doctorId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (doctorId) );  CREATE TABLE medicalreport (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   reportContent Text NOT NULL,   generatedDate Datetime NOT NULL,   status TinyInt NOT NULL,   medicalRecordId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (medicalRecordId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (id) );  CREATE TABLE patientnote (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   noteText Text NOT NULL,   createdAt Datetime NOT NULL,   patientId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (patientId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (patientId) );  CREATE TABLE medicalrecordversion (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   versionNumber Int NOT NULL,   modifiedDate Datetime NOT NULL,   changesSummary Text NOT NULL,   medicalRecordId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (medicalRecordId) REFERENCES medicalrecord (id) );  CREATE TABLE foodconsumptionlog (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   consumedQuantity Double NOT NULL,   mealType Varchar NOT NULL,   recordedDate Datetime NOT NULL,   foodId Int NOT NULL,   appointmentId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (foodId) REFERENCES foodingredient (id),   FOREIGN KEY (appointmentId) REFERENCES appointment (id) );

### Schema:
CREATE TABLE customer_faults (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   fault_id Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   assigned_to Int NOT NULL,   user_id Int NOT NULL,   resolution Varchar NOT NULL,   status Varchar NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Pending Assessment',   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT '0000-00-00 00:00:00' );  CREATE TABLE dist_points (   id Int NOT NULL,   street Varchar NOT NULL,   city Varchar NOT NULL,   code Varchar NOT NULL,   availableSlots Int NOT NULL DEFAULT '10',   maxSlots Int NOT NULL DEFAULT '10',   description Varchar NOT NULL,   dpPairNo Varchar NOT NULL,   dpNo Varchar NOT NULL,   distributionSide Varchar NOT NULL,   exchangeSide Varchar NOT NULL,   cabinetNo Varchar NOT NULL,   mdfPair Varchar NOT NULL,   mdfBar Varchar NOT NULL,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT '0000-00-00 00:00:00',   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE dp_applications (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   service Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   resolution Varchar NOT NULL,   dist_block Int NOT NULL,   client_id Int NOT NULL,   assigned_to Int NOT NULL,   sketchMap Varchar NOT NULL,   assessment Varchar NOT NULL,   applicationDate Timestamp NOT NULL,   status Varchar NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Pending Assessment',   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT '0000-00-00 00:00:00',   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT '0000-00-00 00:00:00' );  CREATE TABLE migrations (   migration Varchar NOT NULL,   batch Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE password_resets (   email Varchar NOT NULL,   token Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE permissions (   id TEXT NOT NULL,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   display_name Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp,   updated_at Timestamp );  CREATE TABLE permission_role (   permission_id TEXT NOT NULL,   role_id TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE roles (   id TEXT NOT NULL,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   display_name Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp,   updated_at Timestamp );  CREATE TABLE role_user (   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   role_id TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE users (   id TEXT NOT NULL,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   password Varchar NOT NULL,   phone Varchar NOT NULL,   remember_token Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT '0000-00-00 00:00:00',   province Varchar NOT NULL,   city Varchar NOT NULL,   streetName Varchar NOT NULL,   houseNumber Varchar NOT NULL,   plotNumber Varchar NOT NULL,   postal Varchar NOT NULL,   physicalAddress Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE service_requests (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   service_type VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   priority VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Normal',   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT '0000-00-00 00:00:00',   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE service_activities (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   request_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   action_taken VARCHAR(255),   notes TEXT,   performed_by TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (request_id) REFERENCES service_requests (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (performed_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE NO ACTION );  CREATE TABLE service_status_logs (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   request_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   old_status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,   new_status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,   updated_by TEXT NOT NULL,   updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (request_id) REFERENCES service_requests (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (updated_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE NO ACTION );  CREATE TABLE fault_logs (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   fault_id VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,   location VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   reported_by TEXT,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   priority VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Low',   status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Open',   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (reported_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE NO ACTION );  CREATE TABLE fault_assignments (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   fault_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   assigned_to TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (fault_id) REFERENCES fault_logs (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (assigned_to) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE NO ACTION );  CREATE TABLE technician_feedback (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   fault_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   technician_id TEXT NOT NULL,   feedback TEXT,   feedback_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (fault_id) REFERENCES fault_logs (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (technician_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE NO ACTION );  CREATE TABLE location_zones (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,   area VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   max_capacity INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 100,   current_usage INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   description TEXT,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE service_zone_maps (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   zone_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   service_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,   zone_constraints TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (zone_id) REFERENCES location_zones (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE usage_reports (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   zone_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   report_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT (DATE('now')),   peak_usage INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   average_usage REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,   anomaly TEXT,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (zone_id) REFERENCES location_zones (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE equipment_maintenance (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   location_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   equipment_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,   last_maintenance TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   next_scheduled TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Due',   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (location_id) REFERENCES dist_points (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE NO ACTION );  CREATE TABLE maintenance_logs (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   equipment_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   performed_by TEXT NOT NULL,   start_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   end_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   issues_found TEXT,   actions_taken TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (equipment_id) REFERENCES equipment_maintenance (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (performed_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE NO ACTION );  CREATE TABLE zone_performance (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   zone_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   metrics TEXT,   reported_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (zone_id) REFERENCES location_zones (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE technician_skills (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   skill_area VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,   certification VARCHAR(100),   rating INTEGER,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE service_requests_closure (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   request_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   closing_user TEXT NOT NULL,   notes TEXT,   closure_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (request_id) REFERENCES service_requests (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE NO ACTION,   FOREIGN KEY (closing_user) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE NO ACTION );  CREATE TABLE reclosing_attempts (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   attempt_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   cables_restored VARCHAR(200),   protected_area VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   total_restored INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   FOREIGN KEY (attempt_date) REFERENCES service_requests (created_at) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE NO ACTION ); ### Question:
List the names of providers who supplied materials for projects that have an order change approved after January 1, 2024 and that involves "structural adjustment" in the change description.

SELECT DISTINCT p.nombre FROM proveedores p JOIN materiales_construccion mc ON p.id = mc.proveedor_id JOIN ordenes_cambio oc ON mc.proyecto_id = oc.proyecto_id WHERE oc.fecha_aprobacion > '2024-01-01'    AND oc.descripcion_cambio LIKE '%structural adjustment%';

### Question:
List all aaa ids that have a log action of 'update' within the last 30 days and also have a setting named 'timeout' with a value greater than '300' and a metadata key 'category' with a value of 'priority'.

SELECT DISTINCT aaa.id FROM aaa INNER JOIN aaa_log ON aaa.id = aaa_log.aaa_id     AND aaa_log.action = 'update'     AND aaa_log.log_timestamp >= datetime('now', '-30 days') INNER JOIN aaa_setting ON aaa.id = aaa_setting.aaa_id     AND aaa_setting.setting_name = 'timeout'     AND aaa_setting.setting_value > '300' INNER JOIN aaa_metadata ON aaa.id = aaa_metadata.aaa_id     AND aaa_metadata.metadata_key = 'category'     AND aaa_metadata.metadata_value = 'priority';

### Question:
Show the count of audit logs per app_id from props_audit where the new_value is different than the old_value and the change was made by a user with 'admin' in their username.

SELECT pa.app_id, COUNT(*) AS change_count  FROM props_audit pa JOIN props_audit_user pau ON pa.audit_id = pau.audit_id JOIN props_user pu ON pau.user_id = pu.user_id WHERE pa.new_value != pa.old_value    AND pu.username LIKE '%admin%' GROUP BY pa.app_id;

### Question:
List the top 5 documents with the highest number of unique user visits in the last 90 days, where each document has at least one associated tag, at least one attribute, and has had a status change recorded in the last 30 days. Include the number of visits, the latest status change date, and the count of attributes for each document.

WITH visit_counts AS (     SELECT doc_id, COUNT(DISTINCT profile_id) AS unique_visits     FROM cnv_visit_log     WHERE visit_time >= datetime('now', '-90 days')     GROUP BY doc_id ), tags_exists AS (     SELECT DISTINCT doc_id     FROM cnv_doc2tag ), attributes_count AS (     SELECT doc_id, COUNT(attr_id) AS attr_count     FROM cnv_doc2attr     GROUP BY doc_id ), status_latest AS (     SELECT doc_id, MAX(status_time) AS latest_status_time     FROM cnv_document_status     WHERE status_time >= datetime('now', '-30 days')     GROUP BY doc_id ) SELECT      v.doc_id,     v.unique_visits,     s.latest_status_time,     a.attr_count FROM visit_counts v JOIN tags_exists t ON v.doc_id = t.doc_id JOIN attributes_count a ON v.doc_id = a.doc_id JOIN status_latest s ON v.doc_id = s.doc_id ORDER BY v.unique_visits DESC LIMIT 5;

### Question:
List the usernames and their associated group names for users who have at least one login activity in the last 30 days and belong to a group with a menu item named 'Dashboard' that has the 'can_edit' permission set to 1.

SELECT DISTINCT u.username, g.mg_nama FROM alus_u u JOIN alus_ug ug ON u.id = ug.user_id JOIN m_grup g ON ug.group_id = g.mg_id JOIN alus_mga mga ON g.mg_id = mga.id_group JOIN alus_mg m ON mga.id_menu = m.menu_id JOIN alus_la la ON u.id = la.id WHERE m.menu_nama = 'Dashboard'   AND mga.can_edit = 1   AND la.time >= date('now', '-30 days');

### Schema:
CREATE TABLE account_credentials (   credential Varchar NOT NULL,   account_id TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (credential, account_id) );

### Question:
Which credentials are associated with accounts that have the highest number of overlapping credentials?

WITH cred_counts AS (     SELECT credential, COUNT(account_id) AS cred_count     FROM account_credentials     GROUP BY credential ), account_overlap AS (     SELECT a.account_id, SUM(cc.cred_count - 1) AS overlap_count     FROM account_credentials a     JOIN cred_counts cc ON a.credential = cc.credential     GROUP BY a.account_id ), max_overlap AS (     SELECT MAX(overlap_count) AS max_count     FROM account_overlap ) SELECT DISTINCT a.credential FROM account_credentials a JOIN account_overlap ao ON a.account_id = ao.account_id JOIN max_overlap mo ON ao.overlap_count = mo.max_count;
