### Schema:
CREATE TABLE problems (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   name Text NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description Text NOT NULL,   short_description Text NOT NULL,   stream TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'console',   author_id BigInt NOT NULL,   visible Boolean NOT NULL,   difficulty TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'easy',   grade TEXT NOT NULL,   credits Text NOT NULL,   time_limit Float NOT NULL,   memory_limit Int NOT NULL,   stack_limit Int NOT NULL,   source_size Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE problem_keywords (   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   keyword TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (problem_id, keyword),   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE problem_notes (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   note TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE problem_versions (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   version_number INTEGER NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   changed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user_preferences (   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   preference_key TEXT NOT NULL,   preference_value TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, preference_key),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE problem_ratings (   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   rating INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5),   PRIMARY KEY (problem_id, user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE submission_attempts (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   submission_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE submission_details (   submission_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   input TEXT NOT NULL,   output TEXT NOT NULL,   error_message TEXT,   time_used Float,   memory_used Int,   FOREIGN KEY (submission_id) REFERENCES submission_attempts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE problem_difficulty_feedback (   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   difficulty_rating TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (difficulty_rating IN ('too_easy', 'easy', 'medium', 'hard', 'too_hard')),   feedback TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY (problem_id, user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE problem_solution_submissions (   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   submission_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   is_official BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   PRIMARY KEY (problem_id, submission_id),   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (submission_id) REFERENCES submission_attempts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE competition_participants (   competition_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   joined_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   role TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('participant', 'observer')),   PRIMARY KEY (competition_id, user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE competition_problems (   competition_id TEXT NOT NULL,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   points INTEGER NOT NULL,   time_limit_override Float,   memory_limit_override Int,   PRIMARY KEY (competition_id, problem_id),   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE competitions (   id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   start_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   end_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   author_id BigInt NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE contest_history (   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   competition_id TEXT NOT NULL,   rank INTEGER,   score INTEGER,   participated BOOLEAN NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, competition_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (competition_id) REFERENCES competitions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user_follows (   follower_id BigInt NOT NULL,   followed_id BigInt NOT NULL,   followed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (follower_id, followed_id),   FOREIGN KEY (follower_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (followed_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE solution_comments (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   submission_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   comment TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (submission_id) REFERENCES submission_attempts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE problem_solution_votes (   solution_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   vote BOOLEAN NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (solution_id, user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (solution_id) REFERENCES submission_attempts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user_activity_logs (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   activity_type TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   activity_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE problem_tags (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   tag TEXT NOT NULL,   parent_tag_id INTEGER,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (parent_tag_id) REFERENCES problem_tags(id) ON DELETE SET NULL );  CREATE INDEX idx_problem_tags_problem_id ON problem_tags(problem_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_problem_tags_parent_tag_id ON problem_tags(parent_tag_id);  CREATE TABLE problem_surveys (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   question TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE survey_responses (   survey_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   response TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (survey_id, user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (survey_id) REFERENCES problem_surveys(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE problem_revisions (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   revised_by BigInt NOT NULL,   revision_notes TEXT,   revised_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (revised_by) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE problem_reviews (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   reviewer_id BigInt NOT NULL,   comments TEXT,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   reviewed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (reviewer_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE submission_bookmarks (   submission_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   bookmarked_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (submission_id, user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (submission_id) REFERENCES submission_attempts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE publication_schedule (   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   scheduled_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (problem_id),   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE problem_discussion_threads (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   creator_id BigInt NOT NULL,   title TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (creator_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE discussion_messages (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   thread_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   message TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (thread_id) REFERENCES problem_discussion_threads(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE problem_suggestions (   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   suggestion TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, problem_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE contest_templates (   id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   author_id BigInt NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE template_items (   template_id TEXT NOT NULL,   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   order_index INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (template_id, problem_id),   FOREIGN KEY (template_id) REFERENCES contest_templates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user_achievements (   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   achievement_type TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   earned_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, achievement_type),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE problem_contributors (   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   contribution_type TEXT NOT NULL,   contribution_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (problem_id, user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE problem_access_control (   problem_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   access_level TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (access_level IN ('view', 'edit', 'admin')),   granted_by BigInt NOT NULL,   granted_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (problem_id, user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (problem_id) REFERENCES problems(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (granted_by) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE contest_rule_sets (   id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   rules TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE contest_template_rules (   template_id TEXT NOT NULL,   rule_set_id TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (template_id, rule_set_id),   FOREIGN KEY (template_id) REFERENCES contest_templates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (rule_set_id) REFERENCES contest_rule_sets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE INDEX idx_discussion_messages_thread_id ON discussion_messages(thread_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_problem_discussion_threads_problem_id ON problem_discussion_threads(problem_id);

### Question:
How many submission attempts have been made for problems that are not visible?
SELECT COUNT(*)  FROM submission_attempts  JOIN problems ON submission_attempts.problem_id = problems.id  WHERE problems.visible = 0;

CREATE TABLE locations (   locId Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   country Text NOT NULL,   region Text NOT NULL,   city Text NOT NULL,   postalCode Text NOT NULL,   latitude Real NOT NULL,   longitude Real NOT NULL,   metroCode Text NOT NULL,   areaCode Text NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE blocks (   startIpNum Integer NOT NULL,   endIpNum Integer NOT NULL,   locId Integer NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (locId) REFERENCES locations (locId) );  CREATE TABLE timezone_info (   timezone_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   locId INTEGER NOT NULL,   timezone_name TEXT NOT NULL,   timezone_offset_seconds INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (locId) REFERENCES locations(locId) );  CREATE TABLE language_distribution (   language_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   locId INTEGER NOT NULL,   language_code TEXT NOT NULL,   percentage REAL NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (locId) REFERENCES locations(locId) );  CREATE TABLE currency_usage (   currency_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   locId INTEGER NOT NULL,   currency_code TEXT NOT NULL,   currency_name TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (locId) REFERENCES locations(locId) );  CREATE TABLE isp_details (   isp_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   startIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   endIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   isp_name TEXT NOT NULL,   website TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (startIpNum, endIpNum) REFERENCES blocks(startIpNum, endIpNum) );  CREATE TABLE network_provider (   network_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   startIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   endIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   provider_name TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (startIpNum, endIpNum) REFERENCES blocks(startIpNum, endIpNum) );  CREATE TABLE connection_types (   connection_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   startIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   endIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   connection_type TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (startIpNum, endIpNum) REFERENCES blocks(startIpNum, endIpNum) );  CREATE TABLE ip_usage_patterns (   usage_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   startIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   endIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   usage_type TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (startIpNum, endIpNum) REFERENCES blocks(startIpNum, endIpNum) );  CREATE TABLE traffic_stats (   traffic_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   startIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   endIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   avg_daily_traffic REAL NOT NULL,   peak_hour_traffic REAL NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (startIpNum, endIpNum) REFERENCES blocks(startIpNum, endIpNum) );  CREATE TABLE geolocation_sources (   source_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   startIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   endIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   source_name TEXT NOT NULL,   source_url TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (startIpNum, endIpNum) REFERENCES blocks(startIpNum, endIpNum) );  CREATE TABLE online_activity_logs (   log_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   startIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   endIpNum INTEGER NOT NULL,   activity_type TEXT NOT NULL,   activity_timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (startIpNum, endIpNum) REFERENCES blocks(startIpNum, endIpNum) );  CREATE TABLE security_threat_risk (   threat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   locId INTEGER NOT NULL,   risk_level INTEGER NOT NULL,   threat_description TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (locId) REFERENCES locations(locId) );  CREATE TABLE accessibility_features (   accessibility_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   locId INTEGER NOT NULL,   feature_name TEXT NOT NULL,   is_available BOOLEAN NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (locId) REFERENCES locations(locId) );  CREATE TABLE regional_statistics (   region_stat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   locId INTEGER NOT NULL,   stat_name TEXT NOT NULL,   stat_value TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (locId) REFERENCES locations(locId) );  CREATE TABLE country_population_data (   population_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   locId INTEGER NOT NULL,   population_count INTEGER NOT NULL,   year_recorded INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (locId) REFERENCES locations(locId) );  CREATE INDEX idx_timezone_locId ON timezone_info(locId);  CREATE INDEX idx_language_locId ON language_distribution(locId);  CREATE INDEX idx_currency_locId ON currency_usage(locId);  CREATE INDEX idx_isp_ip_range ON isp_details(startIpNum, endIpNum);  CREATE INDEX idx_network_ip_range ON network_provider(startIpNum, endIpNum);  CREATE INDEX idx_connection_ip_range ON connection_types(startIpNum, endIpNum);  CREATE INDEX idx_usage_ip_range ON ip_usage_patterns(startIpNum, endIpNum);  CREATE INDEX idx_traffic_ip_range ON traffic_stats(startIpNum, endIpNum);  CREATE INDEX idx_source_ip_range ON geolocation_sources(startIpNum, endIpNum);  CREATE INDEX idx_activity_ip_range ON online_activity_logs(startIpNum, endIpNum);  CREATE INDEX idx_security_locId ON security_threat_risk(locId);  CREATE INDEX idx_accessibility_locId ON accessibility_features(locId);  CREATE INDEX idx_statistics_locId ON regional_statistics(locId);  CREATE INDEX idx_population_locId ON country_population_data(locId);

### Question:
List all locations with a population count exceeding 10 million as of 2020.

SELECT locations.country, locations.region, locations.city  FROM locations  JOIN country_population_data ON locations.locId = country_population_data.locId  WHERE country_population_data.population_count > 10000000    AND country_population_data.year_recorded = 2020;

CREATE TABLE attack (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   attack Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE defense (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   defense Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE generations (   id_generations Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   generation Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE hp (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   hp Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE infos (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   url Varchar NOT NULL,   total Int NOT NULL,   hp Int NOT NULL,   attack Int NOT NULL,   defense Int NOT NULL,   special_attack Int NOT NULL,   special_defense Int NOT NULL,   speed Int NOT NULL,   generations_id_generations Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (generations_id_generations) REFERENCES generations (id_generations) );  CREATE TABLE special_attack (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   special_attack Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE special_defense (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   special_defense Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE speed (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   speed Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE stats (   id_stats Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   value Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE total (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   total Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE types (   id_type Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   type Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE types_has_infos (   types_id_type Int NOT NULL,   infos_id_pokemon Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE weaknesses (   id_weaknesses Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   weaknesses Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE weaknesses_has_infos (   weaknesses_id_weaknesses Int NOT NULL,   infos_id_pokemon Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE weight (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   weight Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_pokemon) REFERENCES infos (id_pokemon) );  CREATE TABLE gender_distribution (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   gender_ratio REAL NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_pokemon) REFERENCES infos (id_pokemon) );  CREATE TABLE egg_groups (   id_egroup Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE egg_groups_has_infos (   egg_groups_id_egroup Int NOT NULL,   infos_id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (egg_groups_id_egroup, infos_id_pokemon),   FOREIGN KEY (egg_groups_id_egroup) REFERENCES egg_groups (id_egroup),   FOREIGN KEY (infos_id_pokemon) REFERENCES infos (id_pokemon) );  CREATE TABLE abilities (   id_ability Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE abilities_has_infos (   abilities_id_ability Int NOT NULL,   infos_id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (abilities_id_ability, infos_id_pokemon),   FOREIGN KEY (abilities_id_ability) REFERENCES abilities (id_ability),   FOREIGN KEY (infos_id_pokemon) REFERENCES infos (id_pokemon) );  CREATE TABLE moves (   id_move Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE moves_details (   moves_id_move Int NOT NULL,   type_id Int NOT NULL,   power Int NOT NULL,   pp Int NOT NULL,   accuracy Int NOT NULL,   category Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (moves_id_move),   FOREIGN KEY (moves_id_move) REFERENCES moves (id_move),   FOREIGN KEY (type_id) REFERENCES types (id_type) );  CREATE TABLE pokemon_moves (   id_pokemon Int NOT NULL,   moves_id_move Int NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id_pokemon, moves_id_move),   FOREIGN KEY (id_pokemon) REFERENCES infos (id_pokemon),   FOREIGN KEY (moves_id_move) REFERENCES moves (id_move) ); ### Question:
For each account with a 'notification_preference' for 'workspace_notification' enabled, calculate the average number of event_sources per event for events of type 'workspace_modified' in the last 30 days, where the account has at least one 'access_token' not revoked, belongs to an organization with 'workspace' named 'main_workspace', and has a 'user_login_history' from 'location' 'Asia' in the last 7 days?

WITH eligible_accounts AS (     SELECT DISTINCT np.account_id     FROM notification_preference np     JOIN access_token at ON np.account_id = at.account_id     JOIN organization_member om ON np.account_id = om.account_id     JOIN workspace w ON om.organization_id = w.organization_id     JOIN user_login_history ulh ON np.account_id = ulh.account_id     WHERE np.notification_type = 'workspace_notification'       AND np.is_enabled = 1       AND at.revoked = 0       AND w.name = 'main_workspace'       AND ulh.location = 'Asia'       AND ulh.created_at >= datetime('now', '-7 days') ) SELECT ea.account_id,         AVG(es.source_count) AS average_sources_per_event FROM eligible_accounts ea JOIN event e ON ea.account_id = e.account_id JOIN (     SELECT event_id, COUNT(*) AS source_count     FROM event_source     GROUP BY event_id ) es ON e.id = es.event_id WHERE e.event_type_id = 'workspace_modified'   AND e.created >= datetime('now', '-30 days') GROUP BY ea.account_id;

### Question:
For each unique combination of login provider and user ID, calculate the total count of role assignments and include the number of users who share the same login provider and token name combination.

SELECT      lul.LoginProvider,     lul.UserId,     COALESCE(ur.RoleCount, 0) AS TotalRoleAssignments,     COALESCE(ut.TokenUserCount, 0) AS SharedTokenLoginUserCount FROM dbo_AspNetUserLogins lul LEFT JOIN (     SELECT UserId, COUNT(*) AS RoleCount     FROM dbo_AspNetUserRoles     GROUP BY UserId ) ur ON lul.UserId = ur.UserId LEFT JOIN (     SELECT ut.UserId,             (SELECT COUNT(*)              FROM dbo_AspNetUserTokens t2             WHERE t2.LoginProvider = ut.LoginProvider                AND t2.Name = ut.Name) AS TokenUserCount     FROM dbo_AspNetUserTokens ut ) ut ON lul.UserId = ut.UserId GROUP BY      lul.LoginProvider,     lul.UserId,     COALESCE(ur.RoleCount, 0),     COALESCE(ut.TokenUserCount, 0) ORDER BY      lul.LoginProvider,     lul.UserId;

CREATE TABLE oss_user (   id BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   username Varchar NOT NULL,   password Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE oss_bucket (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE oss_user_notification (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,   message TEXT NOT NULL,   is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,   created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES oss_user(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_file_access (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,   file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   access_time DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES oss_user(id),   FOREIGN KEY (file_id) REFERENCES oss_file(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_file_metadata (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   metadata_key TEXT NOT NULL,   metadata_value TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (file_id) REFERENCES oss_file(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_file_conversion (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   version_id INTEGER,   source_format TEXT NOT NULL,   target_format TEXT NOT NULL,   status INTEGER,   started_at DATETIME,   completed_at DATETIME,   FOREIGN KEY (file_id) REFERENCES oss_file(id),   FOREIGN KEY (version_id) REFERENCES oss_file_version(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_user_activity (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,   action TEXT NOT NULL,   ip_address TEXT,   user_agent TEXT,   time DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES oss_user(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_file_audit_log (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,   file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   action TEXT NOT NULL,   old_value TEXT,   new_value TEXT,   time DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES oss_user(id),   FOREIGN KEY (file_id) REFERENCES oss_file(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_file_backup (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   backup_time DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   backup_path TEXT NOT NULL,   status INTEGER,   FOREIGN KEY (file_id) REFERENCES oss_file(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_file_trash (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   deleted_by BIGINT NOT NULL,   deleted_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   reason TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (file_id) REFERENCES oss_file(id),   FOREIGN KEY (deleted_by) REFERENCES oss_user(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_user_session (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,   session_token TEXT NOT NULL,   ip_address TEXT,   created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   expires_at DATETIME,   is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES oss_user(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_user_role (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,   role_name TEXT NOT NULL,   assigned_by BIGINT NOT NULL,   assigned_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES oss_user(id),   FOREIGN KEY (assigned_by) REFERENCES oss_user(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_encryption_key (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   key_type TEXT NOT NULL,   key_value TEXT NOT NULL,   file_id INTEGER,   version_id INTEGER,   created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   expires_at DATETIME,   FOREIGN KEY (file_id) REFERENCES oss_file(id),   FOREIGN KEY (version_id) REFERENCES oss_file_version(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_bucket_acl (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   bucket_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,   permission INTEGER,   FOREIGN KEY (bucket_id) REFERENCES oss_bucket(id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES oss_user(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_file_version_access (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,   version_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   access_time DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES oss_user(id),   FOREIGN KEY (version_id) REFERENCES oss_file_version(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_file_retention_policy (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   policy_name TEXT NOT NULL,   duration_days INTEGER NOT NULL,   apply_to_bucket INTEGER,   apply_to_file INTEGER,   created_by BIGINT NOT NULL,   created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (apply_to_bucket) REFERENCES oss_bucket(id),   FOREIGN KEY (apply_to_file) REFERENCES oss_file(id),   FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES oss_user(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_file_version_trash (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   version_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   deleted_by BIGINT NOT NULL,   deleted_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   reason TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (version_id) REFERENCES oss_file_version(id),   FOREIGN KEY (deleted_by) REFERENCES oss_user(id) );  CREATE TABLE oss_file_comment (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_id BIGINT NOT NULL,   comment TEXT NOT NULL,   parent_comment_id INTEGER,   created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (file_id) REFERENCES oss_file(id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES oss_user(id),   FOREIGN KEY (parent_comment_id) REFERENCES oss_file_comment(id) );

### Question:
What percentage of tracks that have been streamed over 10,000 times are part of an album that has received more than 500 likes, and whose artists have at least one comment with a rating of 4 or higher?

WITH HighStreamTracks AS (     SELECT trackId     FROM TrackStream     GROUP BY trackId     HAVING COUNT(*) > 10000 ), AlbumsWithEnoughLikes AS (     SELECT albumId     FROM AlbumLike     GROUP BY albumId     HAVING COUNT(*) > 500 ), ArtistsWithHighRatings AS (     SELECT artistId     FROM ArtistRating     WHERE rating >= 4     GROUP BY artistId ) SELECT      (COUNT(DISTINCT hst.trackId) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM HighStreamTracks)) AS percentage FROM HighStreamTracks hst JOIN TrackAlbumMapping tam ON hst.trackId = tam.trackId JOIN AlbumsWithEnoughLikes alel ON tam.albumId = alel.albumId JOIN TrackArtistMapping trar ON hst.trackId = trar.trackId JOIN ArtistsWithHighRatings awhr ON trar.artistId = awhr.artistId;

### Question:
Identify the earliest created timestamp for each (class_name, method_name) combination where orchestr_params contains 'priority: high' and compensate_if_failure is set to false.

SELECT     class_name,     method_name,     MIN(json_extract(activity_params, '$.created')) AS earliest_created_timestamp FROM     mu_process_step WHERE     json_extract(orchestr_params, '$.priority') = 'high'     AND compensate_if_failure = 0 GROUP BY     class_name, method_name;

### Question:
What are the titles of news articles that have the highest average ratings and are associated with at least three different categories, along with their corresponding category titles and the number of views each has received?

WITH ranked_news AS (     SELECT          n.news_id,         AVG(r.rating_score) AS avg_rating,         COUNT(DISTINCT nc.cat_id) AS category_count,         COALESCE(v.view_count, 0) AS total_views     FROM apps_news n     JOIN apps_news_ratings r ON n.news_id = r.news_id     JOIN apps_news_category_relations nc_rel ON n.news_id = nc_rel.news_id     JOIN apps_news_categories nc ON nc_rel.cat_id = nc.cat_id     LEFT JOIN apps_news_views v ON n.news_id = v.news_id     GROUP BY n.news_id, v.view_count     HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT nc.cat_id) >= 3 ), top_rated_news AS (     SELECT          news_id, avg_rating, category_count, total_views,         ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY avg_rating DESC) AS rank     FROM ranked_news ) SELECT      n.news_id,     nt.news_title,     GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT c.cat_title) AS category_titles,     t.total_views FROM top_rated_news t JOIN apps_news n ON t.news_id = n.news_id JOIN apps_news_translation nt ON n.news_id = nt.news_id JOIN apps_news_category_relations nc_rel ON n.news_id = nc_rel.news_id JOIN apps_news_categories c ON nc_rel.cat_id = c.cat_id WHERE t.rank = 1 GROUP BY n.news_id, nt.news_title, t.total_views;

### Question:
Which job names and their corresponding trigger groups have a non-concurrent setting of 'true' in both qz_job_details and qz_triggers tables, and are linked through a cron trigger configuration?

SELECT qz_job_details.JOB_NAME, qz_triggers.TRIGGER_GROUP FROM qz_job_details JOIN qz_triggers ON qz_job_details.JOB_NAME = qz_triggers.JOB_NAME AND qz_job_details.JOB_GROUP = qz_triggers.JOB_GROUP JOIN qz_cron_triggers ON qz_triggers.TRIGGER_NAME = qz_cron_triggers.TRIGGER_NAME AND qz_triggers.TRIGGER_GROUP = qz_cron_triggers.TRIGGER_GROUP WHERE qz_job_details.IS_NONCONCURRENT = 'true';

CREATE TABLE category_tab_p (   category_id_p Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   category_name_p Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE contact (   nom Varchar NOT NULL,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   phone Varchar NOT NULL,   company Varchar NOT NULL,   message Text NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE pet_tab (   id_p Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description_p Varchar NOT NULL,   category_id_p Int NOT NULL,   imgs_p Varchar NOT NULL,   status_p Varchar NOT NULL,   price Double NOT NULL,   name_p Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (category_id_p) REFERENCES category_tab_p (category_id_p) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE order_summary (   order_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   customer_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   order_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   total_amount REAL NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customer_tab (customer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE order_items (   item_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   order_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   pet_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   quantity INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   price REAL NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES order_summary (order_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (pet_id) REFERENCES pet_tab (id_p) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE reviews_rating (   review_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   pet_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   customer_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   rating INTEGER CHECK(rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5) NOT NULL,   comment TEXT,   created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (pet_id) REFERENCES pet_tab (id_p) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customer_tab (customer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE inventory_levels (   inventory_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   pet_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   stock_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   last_restocked DATETIME,   FOREIGN KEY (pet_id) REFERENCES pet_tab (id_p) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE payment_methods_info (   method_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   customer_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   card_number VARCHAR NOT NULL,   expiry_date VARCHAR NOT NULL,   card_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   is_default BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customer_tab (customer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE transaction_payments (   transaction_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   order_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   amount REAL NOT NULL,   payment_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES order_summary (order_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE newsletter_subscribers (   subscriber_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   email VARCHAR NOT NULL,   subscription_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   is_subscribed BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 );  CREATE TABLE user_accounts (   user_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   email VARCHAR NOT NULL,   password_hash VARCHAR NOT NULL,   created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   last_login DATETIME,   is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 );  CREATE TABLE PurchaseOrderDetails (   detailID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   orderID INTEGER,   productID INTEGER,   quantityOrdered INT NOT NULL,   unitPrice NUMERIC NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (orderID) REFERENCES PurchaseOrder (orderID),   FOREIGN KEY (productID) REFERENCES Product (productID) );  CREATE TABLE Customer (   customerID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   customerName VARCHAR NOT NULL,   address VARCHAR NOT NULL,   phone VARCHAR NOT NULL,   email VARCHAR NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Orders (   orderNo INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   customerID INTEGER,   orderDate DATE NOT NULL,   orderTotal NUMERIC NOT NULL,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (customerID) REFERENCES Customer (customerID) );  CREATE TABLE OrderDetails (   detailNo INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   orderNo INTEGER,   productID INTEGER,   quantity INT NOT NULL,   unitPrice NUMERIC NOT NULL,   subTotal NUMERIC NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (orderNo) REFERENCES Orders (orderNo),   FOREIGN KEY (productID) REFERENCES Product (productID) ); ### Question:
Provide a list of customers who have placed orders with a total amount exceeding $400 and have received shipments with tracking numbers containing the word "Overnight". Include the customer's name, the order date, the total amount, and the shipment date.

SELECT c.customerName, o.orderDate, o.orderTotal, s.shipmentDate FROM Customer c JOIN Orders o ON c.customerID = o.customerID JOIN Shipment s ON o.orderNo = s.orderNo WHERE o.orderTotal > 400 AND s.trackingNumber LIKE '%Overnight%';

### Question:
For each user, how many message drafts have they created compared to the number of form submissions they have made?

WITH draft_counts AS (     SELECT user_id, COUNT(draft_id) AS draft_count     FROM ph_message_drafts     GROUP BY user_id ), form_counts AS (     SELECT user_id, COUNT(submission_id) AS submission_count     FROM ph_form_submissions     GROUP BY user_id ) SELECT d.user_id, d.draft_count, f.submission_count FROM draft_counts d LEFT JOIN form_counts f ON d.user_id = f.user_id;

### Question:
Which keyword is most frequently associated with posts that have received the highest average rating in reviews from the past year?

WITH PostRatings AS (     SELECT          rrp.new_post_id,         AVG(r.rating) AS avg_rating     FROM new_Review r     JOIN new_ReviewPost rrp ON r.id = rrp.new_review_id     WHERE r.created_at >= date('now', '-1 year')     GROUP BY rrp.new_post_id ), MaxAvgRating AS (     SELECT MAX(avg_rating) AS max_avg     FROM PostRatings ) SELECT      k.keyword,     COUNT(*) AS keyword_count FROM PostRatings pr JOIN MaxAvgRating mar ON pr.avg_rating = mar.max_avg JOIN new_PostKeyword pk ON pr.new_post_id = pk.new_post_id JOIN new_Keyword k ON pk.new_keyword_id = k.id GROUP BY k.keyword ORDER BY keyword_count DESC LIMIT 1;

CREATE TABLE easy_access (   role_id TEXT NOT NULL,   node_id TEXT NOT NULL,   level TinyInt NOT NULL,   module Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE easy_role_user (   role_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id Char NOT NULL );

### Question:
For each node, identify the number of users who have access to it via roles that belong to modules starting with 'security' and have a level less than the average level of all roles assigned to that node, while also ensuring that the roles are associated with at least one other user and the nodes are connected to at least three distinct modules.

WITH node_modules AS (     SELECT node_id     FROM easy_access     GROUP BY node_id     HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT module) >= 3 ), avg_node_level AS (     SELECT ea.node_id, AVG(ea.level) AS avg_level     FROM easy_access ea     WHERE ea.node_id IN (SELECT node_id FROM node_modules)     GROUP BY ea.node_id ), security_valid_roles AS (     SELECT ea.role_id, ea.node_id     FROM easy_access ea     JOIN node_modules nm ON ea.node_id = nm.node_id     WHERE ea.module LIKE 'security%'       AND ea.level < (SELECT avg_level FROM avg_node_level WHERE node_id = ea.node_id)       AND ea.role_id IN (           SELECT role_id           FROM easy_role_user           GROUP BY role_id           HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) >= 2       ) ), users_with_access AS (     SELECT eru.user_id, svr.node_id     FROM security_valid_roles svr     JOIN easy_role_user eru ON svr.role_id = eru.role_id ) SELECT node_id, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS user_count FROM users_with_access GROUP BY node_id;

### Question:
Identify the deployment_resources that have at least two health_check_results with result_code 500, are part of a deployment with backup_type 'full' and at least one configuration_version applied by user ID 11223, and are connected to at least two other resources via resource_connections of type 'api'. For each such resource, list its ID, the number of associated health_check_results with result_code over 400, the earliest and latest check_time, and the count of distinct network_policies referencing it either as source or destination resource.

WITH eligible_resources AS (     SELECT DISTINCT dc.resource_id     FROM deployment_components dc     JOIN deployment_backups db ON db.deployment_id = dc.deployment_id AND db.backup_type = 'full'     WHERE EXISTS (         SELECT 1         FROM configuration_snapshots cs         JOIN configuration_versions cv ON cv.configuration_snapshot_id = cs.id         WHERE cs.deployment_id = dc.deployment_id           AND cv.applied_by = 11223     )     AND (         SELECT COUNT(*)          FROM resource_connections rc         WHERE rc.deployment_id = dc.deployment_id           AND (rc.source_resource_id = dc.resource_id OR rc.target_resource_id = dc.resource_id)           AND rc.connection_type = 'api'     ) >= 2     AND (         SELECT COUNT(*)         FROM health_check_results hcr         WHERE hcr.deployment_resource_id = dc.resource_id           AND hcr.result_code = 500     ) >= 2 ) SELECT      er.resource_id AS id,     (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM health_check_results hcr WHERE hcr.deployment_resource_id = er.resource_id AND hcr.result_code > 400) AS over_400_count,     (SELECT MIN(checked_at) FROM health_check_results hcr WHERE hcr.deployment_resource_id = er.resource_id) AS earliest_check_time,     (SELECT MAX(checked_at) FROM health_check_results hcr WHERE hcr.deployment_resource_id = er.resource_id) AS latest_check_time,     (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT np.id) FROM network_policies np WHERE np.source_resource_id = er.resource_id OR np.destination_resource_id = er.resource_id) AS distinct_network_policies FROM eligible_resources er;

### Question:
Show the role names assigned to users who have unread notifications and have never successfully logged in using 2FA.

SELECT r.role_name FROM eum_notifications n JOIN eum_2fa_configurations c ON n.user_id = c.user_id JOIN eum_user_roles ur ON n.user_id = ur.user_id JOIN eum_roles r ON ur.role_id = r.role_id WHERE n.is_read = 0   AND c.enabled = 1   AND n.user_id NOT IN (     SELECT DISTINCT a.user_id     FROM eum_authentication_attempts a     WHERE a.success = 1   );

CREATE TABLE Ingredient (   id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   type Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Taco_Ingredients (   taco_id BigInt NOT NULL,   ingredients_id Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (ingredients_id) REFERENCES Ingredient (id) );  CREATE TABLE Taco_Order_Tacos (   taco_order_id BigInt NOT NULL,   taco_id BigInt NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Supplier (   id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   contact_info Varchar );  CREATE TABLE Ingredient_Supplier (   ingredient_id Varchar NOT NULL,   supplier_id Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (ingredient_id) REFERENCES Ingredient (id),   FOREIGN KEY (supplier_id) REFERENCES Supplier (id) );  CREATE TABLE Payment_Method (   id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   method_type Varchar NOT NULL,   details Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Taco_Order_Payment (   order_id BigInt NOT NULL,   payment_method_id Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES Taco_Order (id),   FOREIGN KEY (payment_method_id) REFERENCES Payment_Method (id) );  CREATE TABLE Order_Status (   status_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   status_name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Taco_Order_Status (   order_id BigInt NOT NULL,   status_id Varchar NOT NULL,   status_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES Taco_Order (id),   FOREIGN KEY (status_id) REFERENCES Order_Status (status_id) );  CREATE TABLE User_Preference (   user_id Varchar NOT NULL,   preference_key Varchar NOT NULL,   preference_value Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id) );  CREATE TABLE Restaurant (   id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   street Varchar NOT NULL,   city Varchar NOT NULL,   state Varchar NOT NULL,   zip Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Restaurant_Ingredient (   restaurant_id Varchar NOT NULL,   ingredient_id Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (restaurant_id) REFERENCES Restaurant (id),   FOREIGN KEY (ingredient_id) REFERENCES Ingredient (id) );  CREATE TABLE Zone (   zone_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   zone_name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Restaurant_Zone (   restaurant_id Varchar NOT NULL,   zone_id Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (restaurant_id) REFERENCES Restaurant (id),   FOREIGN KEY (zone_id) REFERENCES Zone (zone_id) );  CREATE TABLE Loyalty_Program (   user_id Varchar NOT NULL,   points Int NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id),   PRIMARY KEY (user_id) );  CREATE TABLE Loyalty_Transaction (   transaction_id Integer PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_id Varchar NOT NULL,   order_id BigInt NOT NULL,   points_used Int NOT NULL,   transaction_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id),   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES Taco_Order (id) );  CREATE TABLE Promotion (   id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   discount_percentage Numeric NOT NULL,   valid_from Timestamp NOT NULL,   valid_to Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Order_Promotion (   order_id BigInt NOT NULL,   promotion_id Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES Taco_Order (id),   FOREIGN KEY (promotion_id) REFERENCES Promotion (id) );  CREATE TABLE Allergy (   id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id) );  CREATE TABLE User_Allergy (   user_id Varchar NOT NULL,   allergy_id Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id),   FOREIGN KEY (allergy_id) REFERENCES Allergy (id) );  CREATE TABLE Ingredient_Allergy (   ingredient_id Varchar NOT NULL,   allergy_id Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (ingredient_id) REFERENCES Ingredient (id),   FOREIGN KEY (allergy_id) REFERENCES Allergy (id) );  CREATE TABLE Delivery_Address (   address_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id Varchar NOT NULL,   street Varchar NOT NULL,   city Varchar NOT NULL,   state Varchar NOT NULL,   zip Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id) );  CREATE TABLE Taco_Order_Delivery (   order_id BigInt NOT NULL,   address_id Varchar NOT NULL,   delivery_time Timestamp,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES Taco_Order (id),   FOREIGN KEY (address_id) REFERENCES Delivery_Address (address_id) );  CREATE TABLE Rating (   rating_id Integer PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   taco_id BigInt NOT NULL,   user_id Varchar NOT NULL,   restaurant_id Varchar NOT NULL,   score Int NOT NULL,   comment Varchar,   rating_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (taco_id) REFERENCES Taco (id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id),   FOREIGN KEY (restaurant_id) REFERENCES Restaurant (id) );  CREATE TABLE Restaurant_Rating (   restaurant_id Varchar NOT NULL,   user_id Varchar NOT NULL,   score Int NOT NULL,   comment Varchar,   rating_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (restaurant_id) REFERENCES Restaurant (id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id) );  CREATE TABLE Weekly_Menu (   id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   restaurant_id Varchar NOT NULL,   start_date Timestamp NOT NULL,   end_date Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (restaurant_id) REFERENCES Restaurant (id) );  CREATE TABLE Weekly_Menu_Item (   menu_id Varchar NOT NULL,   taco_id BigInt NOT NULL,   price Numeric NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (menu_id) REFERENCES Weekly_Menu (id),   FOREIGN KEY (taco_id) REFERENCES Taco (id) );  CREATE TABLE Supply_Request (   request_id Integer PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   restaurant_id Varchar NOT NULL,   ingredient_id Varchar NOT NULL,   requested_quantity Numeric NOT NULL,   unit Varchar NOT NULL,   request_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   status Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (restaurant_id) REFERENCES Restaurant (id),   FOREIGN KEY (ingredient_id) REFERENCES Ingredient (id) );  CREATE TABLE Supply_Request_Supplier (   request_id Integer NOT NULL,   supplier_id Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (request_id) REFERENCES Supply_Request (request_id),   FOREIGN KEY (supplier_id) REFERENCES Supplier (id) );  CREATE TABLE User_Loyalty_History (   history_id Integer PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_id Varchar NOT NULL,   change_points Int NOT NULL,   change_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   reason Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id) );  CREATE TABLE Loyalty_Reward (   reward_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   points_required Int NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (reward_id) );  CREATE TABLE User_Reward (   user_id Varchar NOT NULL,   reward_id Varchar NOT NULL,   redemption_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id),   FOREIGN KEY (reward_id) REFERENCES Loyalty_Reward (reward_id) );  CREATE TABLE Ingredient_Unit (   id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   unit_name Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id) );  CREATE TABLE Inventory_Unit (   batch_id Varchar NOT NULL,   unit_id Varchar NOT NULL,   conversion_rate Numeric NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (batch_id) REFERENCES Inventory (batch_id),   FOREIGN KEY (unit_id) REFERENCES Ingredient_Unit (id) );

### Question:
What is the average rating score of tacos that have 'chili' as an ingredient and have been rated by users in the 'City' zone?

SELECT AVG(r.score) FROM Rating r JOIN Taco_Ingredients ti ON r.taco_id = ti.taco_id JOIN Ingredient i ON ti.ingredients_id = i.id JOIN Delivery_Address da ON r.user_id = da.user_id JOIN Zone z ON da.city = z.zone_name WHERE i.name = 'chili' AND z.zone_name = 'City';

### Question:
How many donations were made by female donors in the month of March?
SELECT COUNT(*)  FROM donasi JOIN donatur ON donasi.kd_donatur = donatur.kd_donatur WHERE donatur.jeniskelamin = 'female'   AND strftime('%m', donasi.tanggal_donasi) = '03';

### Question:
How many students have scored below the KKM (Minimum Competency Standard) in at least one subject this term?

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT m.pendaftaran_masuk_id_pendaftaran_masuk) FROM mean_mapel m JOIN mata_pelajaran mp ON m.mata_pelajaran_id_mata_pelajaran = mp.id_mata_pelajaran WHERE CAST(m.nilai AS INTEGER) < mp.kkm;

CREATE TABLE ChestRefill (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   contents Text NOT NULL,   rangeMax BigInt NOT NULL,   rangeMin BigInt NOT NULL,   refillDate Datetime NOT NULL,   pitch Float NOT NULL,   world Varchar NOT NULL,   x Double NOT NULL,   y Double NOT NULL,   yaw Float NOT NULL,   z Double NOT NULL ); ### Question:
What is the average number of mob summons per player who has unlocked at least one advanced crafting recipe?

SELECT AVG(num_mob_summons) FROM (     SELECT MobSummons.playerId, COUNT(*) AS num_mob_summons     FROM MobSummons     INNER JOIN (         SELECT DISTINCT RecipeUnlocked.playerId         FROM RecipeUnlocked         INNER JOIN RecipeUpgrade ON RecipeUnlocked.recipeId = RecipeUpgrade.recipeId         WHERE RecipeUpgrade.upgradeLevel > 1     ) AS advanced_players ON MobSummons.playerId = advanced_players.playerId     GROUP BY MobSummons.playerId );

 CREATE TABLE tag (   tag_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   tag_name VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE post_tag (   post_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   tag_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (post_id, tag_id),   FOREIGN KEY (post_id) REFERENCES post (post_id),   FOREIGN KEY (tag_id) REFERENCES tag (tag_id) );  CREATE TABLE user_follow (   follower_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   followee_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   followed_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (follower_id, followee_id),   FOREIGN KEY (follower_id) REFERENCES user(user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (followee_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) );  CREATE TABLE post_view (   view_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   user_id INTEGER,   post_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   viewed_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (post_id) REFERENCES post(post_id) );  CREATE TABLE post_analytics (   post_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   date_bucket DATE NOT NULL,   total_views INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   total_likes INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   UNIQUE (post_id, date_bucket),   FOREIGN KEY (post_id) REFERENCES post(post_id) );  CREATE TABLE saved_post (   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   post_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   saved_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, post_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (post_id) REFERENCES post(post_id) );  CREATE TABLE user_message (   message_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   sender_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   receiver_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   subject TEXT NOT NULL,   body TEXT NOT NULL,   is_read INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   sent_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (sender_id) REFERENCES user(user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (receiver_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) );  CREATE TABLE user_message_attachment (   attachment_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   message_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   file_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   file_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   file_data BLOB NOT NULL,   uploaded_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (message_id) REFERENCES user_message(message_id) );  CREATE TABLE user_role (   user_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   role_name VARCHAR NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user',   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) );  CREATE TABLE role_permission (   role_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   permission_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (role_name, permission_name) );  CREATE TABLE post_translations (   post_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   language_code VARCHAR NOT NULL,   translated_text TEXT NOT NULL,   translated_title VARCHAR NOT NULL,   translated_picture_text VARCHAR,   PRIMARY KEY (post_id, language_code),   FOREIGN KEY (post_id) REFERENCES post(post_id) ); CREATE TABLE movies (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title TEXT NOT NULL,   categories TEXT NOT NULL,   summary Text NOT NULL,   description Text NOT NULL,   tsv TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE logs (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   query Text NOT NULL,   timestamp Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE actors (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   birth_date DATE,   nationality TEXT );  CREATE TABLE directors (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   birth_year INTEGER,   bio TEXT );  CREATE TABLE genres (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE movie_actors (   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   actor_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   role TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY (movie_id, actor_id),   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id),   FOREIGN KEY (actor_id) REFERENCES actors(id) );  CREATE TABLE movie_directors (   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   director_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (movie_id, director_id),   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id),   FOREIGN KEY (director_id) REFERENCES directors(id) );  CREATE TABLE movie_genres (   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   genre_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (movie_id, genre_id),   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id),   FOREIGN KEY (genre_id) REFERENCES genres(id) );  CREATE TABLE reviews (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_name TEXT,   rating INTEGER CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5),   review_text TEXT NOT NULL,   submitted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id) );  CREATE TABLE ratings (   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   average_rating REAL,   total_votes INTEGER,   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id) );  CREATE TABLE showtimes (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   theater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   show_start TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id),   FOREIGN KEY (theater_id) REFERENCES theaters(id) );  CREATE TABLE theaters (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   location TEXT NOT NULL,   capacity INTEGER );  CREATE TABLE keywords (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   keyword TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE movie_keywords (   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   keyword_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (movie_id, keyword_id),   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id),   FOREIGN KEY (keyword_id) REFERENCES keywords(id) );  CREATE TABLE awards (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   award_name TEXT NOT NULL,   year_awarded INTEGER );  CREATE TABLE movie_awards (   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   award_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (movie_id, award_id),   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id),   FOREIGN KEY (award_id) REFERENCES awards(id) );  CREATE TABLE studios (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   country TEXT );  CREATE TABLE movie_studios (   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   studio_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (movie_id, studio_id),   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id),   FOREIGN KEY (studio_id) REFERENCES studios(id) );  CREATE TABLE user_activity (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   user_id INTEGER,   movie_id INTEGER,   activity_type TEXT NOT NULL,   timestamp TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id) );  CREATE TABLE movie_director_awards (   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   award_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   director_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (movie_id, award_id, director_id),   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id),   FOREIGN KEY (award_id) REFERENCES awards(id),   FOREIGN KEY (director_id) REFERENCES directors(id) );  CREATE TABLE movie_releases (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   release_platform TEXT NOT NULL,   release_date DATE,   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id) );  CREATE TABLE movie_og_data (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   og_title TEXT,   og_description TEXT,   og_url TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id) );  CREATE TABLE movie_metadata_sources (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   movie_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   source_name TEXT NOT NULL,   source_url TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (movie_id) REFERENCES movies(id) ); ### Question:
Find the studios located in the United States that have produced at least 10 movies with an average rating above 4.0.

SELECT s.id, s.name FROM studios s JOIN movie_studios ms ON s.id = ms.studio_id JOIN ratings r ON ms.movie_id = r.movie_id WHERE s.country = 'United States' AND r.average_rating > 4.0 GROUP BY s.id, s.name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 10;

### Question:
Identify the email templates that have been used in the past week, where the template includes at least one parameter of type 'string', and the recipient is part of a role with write access to a specific hierarchy.

SELECT DISTINCT ELog.templateId FROM T_EMAIL_LOG AS ELog JOIN T_EMAIL_TEMPLATES AS ETemplates ON ELog.templateId = ETemplates.id JOIN T_EMAIL_TEMPLATE_PARAMETERS AS EParams ON ETemplates.id = EParams.templateId JOIN T_USERS AS RecipientUser ON ELog.userId = RecipientUser.id JOIN T_USER_ROLES AS UserRoles ON RecipientUser.id = UserRoles.id JOIN T_USER_PERMISSIONS AS UserPerms ON UserRoles.role_name = UserPerms.roleId WHERE ELog.sentTime >= strftime('%Y-%m-%d 00:00', 'now', '-7 days')   AND EParams.valueType = 'string'   AND UserPerms.writeAccess = 1;

CREATE TABLE transaction_details (   transaction_id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   flag Varchar NOT NULL,   inventory_name Varchar NOT NULL,   message Varchar NOT NULL,   project Varchar NOT NULL,   serial_number Varchar NOT NULL,   time BigInt NOT NULL,   user_name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE project_budgets (   project_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   allocated_budget BIGINT NOT NULL,   spent_budget BIGINT DEFAULT 0,   remaining_budget BIGINT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (allocated_budget - spent_budget) VIRTUAL,   FOREIGN KEY (project_name) REFERENCES v1_dml(project) );  CREATE TABLE user_activity (   activity_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   action VARCHAR NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_name) REFERENCES vault_items(user_name) );  CREATE TABLE serial_number_history (   history_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   serial_number VARCHAR NOT NULL,   previous_owner VARCHAR,   new_owner VARCHAR NOT NULL,   transfer_time BIGINT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (serial_number) REFERENCES vault_items(serial_number) );  CREATE TABLE message_logs (   log_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   message VARCHAR NOT NULL,   sender VARCHAR NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (message) REFERENCES vault_transactions(message) );  CREATE TABLE transaction_types (   type_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   type_name VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description TEXT );  CREATE TABLE project_members (   project_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   user_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   role VARCHAR NOT NULL,   join_date DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   PRIMARY KEY (project_name, user_name),   FOREIGN KEY (project_name) REFERENCES v1_dml(project) );  CREATE TABLE inventory_status (   status_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   inventory_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   last_checked BIGINT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (inventory_name) REFERENCES vault_items(inventory_name) );  CREATE TABLE system_alerts (   alert_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   alert_message VARCHAR NOT NULL,   triggered_time DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   severity VARCHAR NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE vault_access_logs (   access_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   access_time DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   access_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_name) REFERENCES vault_items(user_name) );  CREATE TABLE budget_transactions (   transaction_id INT NOT NULL,   project_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   amount BIGINT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (transaction_id) REFERENCES transaction_details(transaction_id),   FOREIGN KEY (project_name) REFERENCES project_budgets(project_name) );  CREATE TABLE inventory_transfers (   transfer_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   from_project VARCHAR NOT NULL,   to_project VARCHAR NOT NULL,   inventory_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   transfer_time BIGINT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (from_project) REFERENCES v1_dml(project),   FOREIGN KEY (to_project) REFERENCES v1_dml(project),   FOREIGN KEY (inventory_name) REFERENCES vault_items(inventory_name) );  CREATE TABLE user_permissions (   user_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   permission_level VARCHAR NOT NULL,   granted_time DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_name) REFERENCES vault_items(user_name) );  CREATE TABLE user_contributions (   contribution_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   contribution_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   contribution_amount BIGINT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_name) REFERENCES vault_items(user_name) ); ### Question:
What percentage of entries in the inventory_status table have a last_checked timestamp less than 1650000000?
SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN last_checked < 1650000000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage FROM inventory_status;

### Question:
What is the average number of messages per user in the channel with the highest activity?

WITH MostActiveChannel AS (     SELECT channelId     FROM ChannelStatistic     ORDER BY messagesCount DESC     LIMIT 1 ) SELECT      (CAST(cs.messagesCount AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT cu.userId)) AS average_messages_per_user FROM MostActiveChannel mac JOIN ChannelStatistic cs ON mac.channelId = cs.channelId JOIN ChannelUser cu ON mac.channelId = cu.channelId;

### Question:
List all products that are categorized under both Action and Adventure genres, are available in English and German, sold in Germany and the United Kingdom, have a discount higher than 20%, and a stock level above 100 units.

WITH valid_genres AS (     SELECT pg.prod_id     FROM public_prod_genre pg     JOIN public_genres g ON pg.genre_id = g.genre_id     WHERE g.genre IN ('Action', 'Adventure')     GROUP BY pg.prod_id     HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT g.genre) = 2 ), valid_languages AS (     SELECT lp.prod_id     FROM public_languagepref lp     JOIN public_languages l ON lp.language_id = l.language_id     WHERE l.language IN ('English', 'German')     GROUP BY lp.prod_id     HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.language) = 2 ), valid_countries AS (     SELECT cg.prod_id     FROM public_countrygroups cg     JOIN public_countries c ON cg.country_id = c.country_id     WHERE c.country IN ('Germany', 'United Kingdom')     GROUP BY cg.prod_id     HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) = 2 ) SELECT p.* FROM public_products p WHERE p.prod_id IN (SELECT prod_id FROM valid_genres)   AND p.prod_id IN (SELECT prod_id FROM valid_languages)   AND p.prod_id IN (SELECT prod_id FROM valid_countries)   AND p.discount_numeric > 20   AND p.stock > 100;

### Question:
Show the job titles and company names for jobs that require a "Project Management" skill and are located in the city of Vancouver.

SELECT DISTINCT job.JobTitle, job.Company FROM job JOIN JobLocation ON job.JobID = JobLocation.JobID LEFT JOIN JobDocument ON job.JobID = JobDocument.JobID WHERE JobLocation.City = 'Vancouver'   AND (job.Description LIKE '%Project Management%' OR JobDocument.DocumentTitle LIKE '%Project Management%');

### Question:
Identify the events where the top three podium finishers in a race all came from different teams and had an average age above 25.

SELECT DISTINCT evento_id FROM (     SELECT p.evento_id, p.prova_id     FROM podium p     JOIN piloto pi ON p.evento_id = pi.evento_id AND p.piloto_id = pi.piloto_id     WHERE p.posicao IN (1, 2, 3)     GROUP BY p.evento_id, p.prova_id     HAVING COUNT(*) = 3         AND COUNT(DISTINCT p.equipe_id) = 3         AND AVG(pi.idade) > 25 );

### Question:
Find the most recent announcement posted in each group that has unread messages for at least one user.

WITH UnreadAnnouncements AS (     SELECT a.GROUP_ID, a.ANNOUNCEMENT_ID, a.POSTED_AT     FROM SAKAI_SITE_GROUPS_ANNOUNCEMENTS a     JOIN SAKAI_SITE_GROUPS_ANNOUNCEMENT_READ_STATUS rs          ON a.ANNOUNCEMENT_ID = rs.ANNOUNCEMENT_ID AND a.GROUP_ID = rs.GROUP_ID     WHERE rs.IS_READ = 0 ), LatestUnreadPerGroup AS (     SELECT GROUP_ID, MAX(POSTED_AT) AS latest_posted     FROM UnreadAnnouncements     GROUP BY GROUP_ID ) SELECT a.* FROM SAKAI_SITE_GROUPS_ANNOUNCEMENTS a JOIN LatestUnreadPerGroup l ON a.GROUP_ID = l.GROUP_ID AND a.POSTED_AT = l.latest_posted;

 CREATE TABLE invoice (   id Int NOT NULL,   number Varchar NOT NULL,   date Timestamp NOT NULL,   date_from Timestamp NOT NULL,   date_to Timestamp NOT NULL,   company_id Int NOT NULL,   remarks longtext NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (company_id) REFERENCES company (id) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE invoice_item (   id Int NOT NULL,   invoice_id Int NOT NULL,   description longtext NOT NULL,   unit Varchar NOT NULL,   count Decimal NOT NULL,   price Decimal NOT NULL,   tax_rate_id Int NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE sys_city (   id Int NOT NULL,   post_code Varchar NOT NULL,   city Varchar NOT NULL,   country Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE sys_tax_rate (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   value Decimal NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE sys_user (   id Int NOT NULL,   username Varchar NOT NULL,   password Varchar NOT NULL,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   surname Varchar NOT NULL,   is_admin TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   is_active TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '1' );  CREATE TABLE sys_role (   id Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE sys_user_role (   user_id Int NOT NULL,   role_id Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES sys_user (id) ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES sys_role (id) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE product_category (   id Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   parent_id Int,   description Varchar,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES product_category (id) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE product (   id Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   category_id Int NOT NULL,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   unit Varchar NOT NULL,   price Decimal NOT NULL,   description longtext,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (category_id) REFERENCES product_category (id) ON UPDATE CASCADE ); ### Question:
Provide the count of invoices per company type, considering only companies that have at least one contact person with a provided email address.

SELECT ct.name, COUNT(*) AS invoice_count FROM invoice i JOIN company_type_mapping ctm ON i.company_id = ctm.company_id JOIN company_type ct ON ctm.type_id = ct.id WHERE i.company_id IN (     SELECT DISTINCT company_id     FROM company_contact     WHERE email IS NOT NULL AND email != '' ) GROUP BY ct.name;

### Question:
Which course has the highest number of forum threads with more than 10 votes, and what is the title of that course's syllabus?

WITH course_thread_counts AS (     SELECT cf.course_id, COUNT(*) AS thread_count     FROM course_forum cf     JOIN course_forum_thread cft ON cf.id = cft.topic_id     WHERE cft.votes_nbr > 10     GROUP BY cf.course_id ) SELECT ct.course_id, cs.about AS syllabus_title FROM course_thread_counts ct JOIN course_syllabus cs ON ct.course_id = cs.course_id ORDER BY ct.thread_count DESC LIMIT 1;

 CREATE TABLE clusterstate (   cluster_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   current_cluster_state TEXT,   current_stack_version TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY (cluster_id),   FOREIGN KEY (cluster_id) REFERENCES clusters (cluster_id) );  CREATE TABLE hostcomponentdesiredstate (   cluster_id TEXT NOT NULL,   component_name TEXT NOT NULL,   desired_stack_version TEXT,   desired_state TEXT NOT NULL,   host_name TEXT NOT NULL,   service_name TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (cluster_id, component_name, host_name, service_name),   FOREIGN KEY (host_name) REFERENCES hosts (host_name),   FOREIGN KEY (component_name, cluster_id, service_name) REFERENCES servicecomponentdesiredstate (component_name, cluster_id, service_name) );  CREATE TABLE hostcomponentstate (   cluster_id TEXT NOT NULL,   component_name TEXT NOT NULL,   current_stack_version TEXT NOT NULL,   current_state TEXT NOT NULL,   host_name TEXT NOT NULL,   service_name TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (cluster_id, component_name, host_name, service_name),   FOREIGN KEY (component_name, cluster_id, service_name) REFERENCES servicecomponentdesiredstate (component_name, cluster_id, service_name),   FOREIGN KEY (host_name) REFERENCES hosts (host_name) );  CREATE TABLE hosts (   host_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   cpu_count Integer NOT NULL,   cpu_info TEXT,   discovery_status TEXT,   host_attributes Clob,   ipv4 TEXT,   ipv6 TEXT,   last_registration_time Integer NOT NULL,   os_arch TEXT,   os_info TEXT,   os_type TEXT,   ph_cpu_count Integer NOT NULL,   public_host_name TEXT,   rack_info TEXT NOT NULL,   total_mem Integer NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (host_name) );  CREATE TABLE execution_command (   task_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   command Blob,   PRIMARY KEY (task_id),   FOREIGN KEY (task_id) REFERENCES host_role_command (task_id) );  CREATE TABLE host_role_command (   task_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   attempt_count TEXT NOT NULL,   event Clob,   exitcode TEXT NOT NULL,   host_name TEXT NOT NULL,   last_attempt_time TEXT NOT NULL,   request_id TEXT NOT NULL,   role TEXT,   role_command TEXT,   stage_id TEXT NOT NULL,   start_time TEXT NOT NULL,   status TEXT,   std_error Blob,   std_out Blob,   PRIMARY KEY (task_id),   FOREIGN KEY (stage_id, request_id) REFERENCES stage (stage_id, request_id),   FOREIGN KEY (host_name) REFERENCES hosts (host_name) );  CREATE TABLE role_success_criteria (   role TEXT NOT NULL,   request_id TEXT NOT NULL,   stage_id TEXT NOT NULL,   success_factor TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (role, request_id, stage_id),   FOREIGN KEY (stage_id, request_id) REFERENCES stage (stage_id, request_id) );  CREATE TABLE ambari_confgroupclusterconfigmapping (   config_group_id TEXT NOT NULL,   cluster_id TEXT NOT NULL,   config_type TEXT NOT NULL,   version_tag TEXT NOT NULL,   user_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '_db',   create_timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (config_group_id, cluster_id, config_type) );  CREATE TABLE ambari_configgrouphostmapping (   config_group_id TEXT NOT NULL,   host_name TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (config_group_id, host_name) );  CREATE TABLE ambari_action (   action_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   action_type TEXT NOT NULL,   inputs TEXT NOT NULL,   target_service TEXT NOT NULL,   target_component TEXT NOT NULL,   default_timeout TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   target_type TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (action_name) );  CREATE TABLE workflow (   workflowId TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   workflowName TEXT NOT NULL,   parentWorkflowId TEXT NOT NULL,   workflowContext TEXT NOT NULL,   userName TEXT NOT NULL,   startTime Integer NOT NULL,   lastUpdateTime Integer NOT NULL,   numJobsTotal Integer NOT NULL,   numJobsCompleted Integer NOT NULL,   inputBytes Integer NOT NULL,   outputBytes Integer NOT NULL,   duration Integer NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (workflowId),   FOREIGN KEY (parentWorkflowId) REFERENCES workflow (workflowId) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE job (   jobId TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   workflowId TEXT NOT NULL,   jobName TEXT NOT NULL,   workflowEntityName TEXT NOT NULL,   userName TEXT NOT NULL,   queue Clob NOT NULL,   acls Clob NOT NULL,   confPath Clob NOT NULL,   submitTime Integer NOT NULL,   launchTime Integer NOT NULL,   finishTime Integer NOT NULL,   maps Integer NOT NULL,   reduces Integer NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   priority TEXT NOT NULL,   finishedMaps Integer NOT NULL,   finishedReduces Integer NOT NULL,   failedMaps Integer NOT NULL,   failedReduces Integer NOT NULL,   mapsRuntime Integer NOT NULL,   reducesRuntime Integer NOT NULL,   mapCounters TEXT NOT NULL,   reduceCounters TEXT NOT NULL,   jobCounters TEXT NOT NULL,   inputBytes Integer NOT NULL,   outputBytes Integer NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (jobId),   FOREIGN KEY (workflowId) REFERENCES workflow (workflowId) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE taskAttempt (   taskAttemptId TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   taskId TEXT NOT NULL,   jobId TEXT NOT NULL,   taskType TEXT NOT NULL,   taskTracker TEXT NOT NULL,   startTime Integer NOT NULL,   finishTime Integer NOT NULL,   mapFinishTime Integer NOT NULL,   shuffleFinishTime Integer NOT NULL,   sortFinishTime Integer NOT NULL,   locality TEXT NOT NULL,   avataar TEXT NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   error Clob NOT NULL,   counters TEXT NOT NULL,   inputBytes Integer NOT NULL,   outputBytes Integer NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (taskAttemptId),   FOREIGN KEY (jobId) REFERENCES job (jobId) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (taskId) REFERENCES task (taskId) ON DELETE CASCADE ); ### Question:
Identify the hosts that have executed the most tasks in the 'execution_command' table, where the task status is 'COMPLETED' and the command execution started in the year 2022. For each host, report the number of completed tasks, the average exit code of these tasks, and the average duration of the tasks (calculated as the difference between last_attempt_time and start_time). Include only hosts that have at least 50 completed tasks and sort the results by the number of tasks in descending order.

SELECT hr.host_name, COUNT(*) AS num_tasks, AVG(CAST(hr.exitcode AS INTEGER)) AS avg_exit_code, AVG(CAST(hr.last_attempt_time AS INTEGER) - t.startTime) AS avg_duration FROM execution_command ec JOIN task t ON ec.task_id = t.taskId JOIN host_role_command hr ON t.taskId = hr.task_id WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'   AND strftime('%Y', datetime(t.startTime, 'unixepoch')) = '2022' GROUP BY hr.host_name HAVING num_tasks >= 50 ORDER BY num_tasks DESC;

 CREATE TABLE department (     id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,     parent_id Int NULL,     name Char(50) NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES department(id) );  CREATE TABLE role (     id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,     name Char(50) NOT NULL,     description Text,     PRIMARY KEY (id) );  CREATE TABLE employee_role (     id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,     employee_id Int NOT NULL,     role_id Int NOT NULL,     start_date Timestamp NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id),     FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES role(id) );  CREATE TABLE service_availability (     id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     service_id INT NOT NULL,     location_id INT NOT NULL,     start_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,     end_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,     is_available BOOLEAN NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (service_id) REFERENCES service(id),     FOREIGN KEY (location_id) REFERENCES location(id) );  CREATE TABLE employee_schedule (     id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     employee_id INT NOT NULL,     location_id INT NOT NULL,     date DATE NOT NULL,     shift_start TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,     shift_end TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id),     FOREIGN KEY (location_id) REFERENCES location(id) );  CREATE TABLE location_hours (     id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     location_id INT NOT NULL,     day_of_week INT NOT NULL,     open_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,     close_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,     is_closed BOOLEAN NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (location_id) REFERENCES location(id) );  CREATE TABLE role_permission (     id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     role_id INT NOT NULL,     permission_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,     is_granted BOOLEAN NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES role(id) );  CREATE TABLE department_budget (     id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     department_id INT NOT NULL,     fiscal_year INT NOT NULL,     allocated_amount DECIMAL(15, 2) NOT NULL,     spent_amount DECIMAL(15, 2) NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES department(id) );  CREATE TABLE location_budget (     id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     location_id INT NOT NULL,     fiscal_year INT NOT NULL,     allocated_amount DECIMAL(15, 2) NOT NULL,     spent_amount DECIMAL(15, 2) NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (location_id) REFERENCES location(id) );  CREATE TABLE employee_compensation (     id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     employee_id INT NOT NULL,     base_salary DECIMAL(15, 2) NOT NULL,     bonus DECIMAL(15, 2) DEFAULT 0,     adjustment_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employee(id) );  CREATE TABLE promotion_budget (     id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     promotion_id INT NOT NULL,     total_allocated DECIMAL(15, 2) NOT NULL,     total_spent DECIMAL(15, 2),     FOREIGN KEY (promotion_id) REFERENCES promotion(id) );  CREATE TABLE service_budget (     id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     service_id INT NOT NULL,     fiscal_year INT NOT NULL,     allocated_cost DECIMAL(15, 2) NOT NULL,     actual_cost DECIMAL(15, 2),     FOREIGN KEY (service_id) REFERENCES service(id) );  CREATE TABLE product_budget (     id INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     product_id INT NOT NULL,     fiscal_year INT NOT NULL,     allocated_cost DECIMAL(15, 2) NOT NULL,     actual_cost DECIMAL(15, 2),     FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES product(id) ); ### Question:
List the average daily revenue for each location in the month of February 2025, excluding locations that have been superseded.

SELECT r.id_location, AVG(r.sum) AS average_daily_revenue FROM revenue r JOIN location l ON r.id_location = l.id WHERE DATE(r.date) BETWEEN '2025-02-01' AND '2025-02-28'   AND l.superseded_by IS NULL GROUP BY r.id_location;

 CREATE TABLE Syllabus (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   courseId TEXT NOT NULL,   version INTEGER NOT NULL,   content TEXT NOT NULL,   updatedAt TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (courseId) REFERENCES Course(id) ); ### Question:
Identify the medications that are used to treat diseases with the highest number of associated symptoms, and list the pharmacies that stock them.

WITH diseases_with_max_symptoms AS (   SELECT p.id AS id, p.nama AS nama_penyakit,          ((p.gejala1 IS NOT NULL) + (p.gejala2 IS NOT NULL) +            (p.gejala3 IS NOT NULL) + (p.gejala4 IS NOT NULL) +            (p.gejala5 IS NOT NULL)) AS symptom_count   FROM penyakit p ), max_symptoms_count AS (   SELECT MAX(symptom_count) AS max_count FROM diseases_with_max_symptoms ), filtered_diseases AS (   SELECT d.id, d.nama_penyakit    FROM diseases_with_max_symptoms d   JOIN max_symptoms_count m ON d.symptom_count = m.max_count ), medications AS (   SELECT DISTINCT o.id AS obat_id, o.nama AS obat_nama    FROM obat o   WHERE o.sakit1 IN (SELECT id FROM filtered_diseases)      OR o.sakit2 IN (SELECT id FROM filtered_diseases)      OR o.sakit3 IN (SELECT id FROM filtered_diseases) ) SELECT DISTINCT a.nama AS apotek_nama, m.obat_nama FROM apotek a JOIN proses pr ON CAST(pr.data1 AS INTEGER) = a.id JOIN medications m ON CAST(pr.data5 AS INTEGER) = m.obat_id;

### Question:
List the names of users who have provided feedback for an application but have never rated it.

SELECT DISTINCT u.name FROM applicationFeedback af JOIN users u ON af.userUuid = u.uuid LEFT JOIN applicationRating ar ON af.userUuid = ar.userUuid AND af.applicationUuid = ar.applicationUuid WHERE ar.userUuid IS NULL;

### Question:
List the names of all clients who have had interactions with employees holding the title of 'Sales Manager'.

SELECT DISTINCT c.client_name FROM client c JOIN client_interactions ci ON c.client_id = ci.client_id JOIN employee_titles et ON ci.emp_id = et.emp_id WHERE et.title_name = 'Sales Manager'   AND ci.interaction_date >= et.start_date   AND (et.end_date IS NULL OR ci.interaction_date <= et.end_date);

### Question:
What is the total number of referral rewards that have not yet been processed for users who signed up in the last month?

SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_unprocessed_referrals FROM referral_program JOIN user ON referral_program.referred_user_id = user.id JOIN wallet_account ON user.id = wallet_account.user_id WHERE wallet_account.created_at >= datetime('now', '-1 month')   AND referral_program.is_rewarded = 0;
