### Schema:
CREATE TABLE customers (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   image Text NOT NULL,   name Text NOT NULL,   age Int NOT NULL,   cnh_number Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   cpf_number Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE agencies (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   address_id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   image Text NOT NULL,   name Text NOT NULL,   phone Text NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE addresses (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   country Text NOT NULL,   state Text NOT NULL,   city Text NOT NULL,   street Text NOT NULL,   cep Int NOT NULL,   complement Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id) REFERENCES agencies (address_id) );  CREATE TABLE cars (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   model_id Int NOT NULL,   name Text NOT NULL,   series Text NOT NULL,   color Text NOT NULL,   car_plate Text NOT NULL,   price Int NOT NULL,   year Int NOT NULL,   status Int NOT NULL,   last_maintenance Timestamp NOT NULL,   next_maintenance Timestamp NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (model_id) REFERENCES models (id) );  CREATE TABLE models (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   type Text NOT NULL,   brand Text NOT NULL,   fuel_type Text NOT NULL,   seats Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE orders (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   agencies_id Int NOT NULL,   customers_id Int NOT NULL,   kilometers Int NOT NULL,   pickup_location Text NOT NULL,   pickup_date Timestamp NOT NULL,   devolution_location Text NOT NULL,   devolution_date Timestamp NOT NULL,   discount Int NOT NULL,   total_price Int NOT NULL,   number_drivers Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (agencies_id) REFERENCES agencies (id),   FOREIGN KEY (customers_id) REFERENCES customers (id) );  CREATE TABLE cars_orders (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   car_id Int NOT NULL,   order_id Int NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (car_id) REFERENCES cars (id),   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES orders (id) );  CREATE TABLE maintenance_records (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   car_id TEXT NOT NULL,   service_type TEXT NOT NULL,   cost INTEGER NOT NULL,   date_performed TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   provider TEXT NOT NULL,   notes TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (car_id) REFERENCES cars (id) );  CREATE TABLE insurance_policies (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   car_id TEXT NOT NULL,   provider TEXT NOT NULL,   policy_number TEXT NOT NULL,   start_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   end_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   coverage TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE payments (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   order_id TEXT NOT NULL,   amount INTEGER NOT NULL,   payment_method TEXT NOT NULL,   payment_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE customer_agreements (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   order_id TEXT NOT NULL,   agreement_type TEXT NOT NULL,   signed_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   terms_version TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE agency_ratings (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   agency_id TEXT NOT NULL,   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   rating INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5),   comment TEXT,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE return_incidents (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   order_id TEXT NOT NULL,   incident_type TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   resolution TEXT,   incident_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE driver_licenses (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   license_number TEXT NOT NULL,   issuance_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   expiration_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   category TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE car_inventory (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   agency_id TEXT NOT NULL,   car_id TEXT NOT NULL,   stock_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,   updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE support_tickets (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   subject TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE maintenance_providers (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   address_id TEXT NOT NULL,   contact_person TEXT NOT NULL,   phone TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE vehicle_inspections (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   car_id TEXT NOT NULL,   inspector TEXT NOT NULL,   inspection_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   result TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE agency_licenses (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   agency_id TEXT NOT NULL,   license_number TEXT NOT NULL,   issuing_authority TEXT NOT NULL,   expiration_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE user_roles (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT );  CREATE TABLE agency_staff (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   agency_id TEXT NOT NULL,   role_id TEXT NOT NULL,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   position TEXT NOT NULL,   hired_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE departments (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   agency_id TEXT NOT NULL,   staff_id TEXT NOT NULL,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   manager_id TEXT );  CREATE TABLE vehicle_types (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   model_id TEXT NOT NULL,   type_category TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT );  CREATE TABLE fuel_type_changes (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   model_id TEXT NOT NULL,   old_type TEXT NOT NULL,   new_type TEXT NOT NULL,   change_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   reason TEXT );  CREATE TABLE car_features (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   car_id TEXT NOT NULL,   feature_type TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT );  CREATE TABLE rental_packages (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   base_price INTEGER NOT NULL,   included_kilometers INTEGER NOT NULL,   extra_kilometer_rate INTEGER NOT NULL,   discounts TEXT );  CREATE INDEX idx_maintenance_car_id ON maintenance_records(car_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_insurance_car_id ON insurance_policies(car_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_payments_order_id ON payments(order_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_agreements_order_id ON customer_agreements(order_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_ratings_agency_id ON agency_ratings(agency_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_ratings_customer_id ON agency_ratings(customer_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_incidents_order_id ON return_incidents(order_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_licenses_customer_id ON driver_licenses(customer_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_agency_id ON car_inventory(agency_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_car_id ON car_inventory(car_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_tickets_customer_id ON support_tickets(customer_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_maintenance_address_id ON maintenance_providers(address_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_inspections_car_id ON vehicle_inspections(car_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_agency_licenses ON agency_licenses(agency_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_staff_agency ON agency_staff(agency_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_staff_role ON agency_staff(role_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_departments_agency ON departments(agency_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_departments_staff ON departments(staff_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_departments_manager ON departments(manager_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_vehicle_types_model ON vehicle_types(model_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_fuel_changes_model ON fuel_type_changes(model_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_car_features_car_id ON car_features(car_id);

### Question:
What is the total number of maintenance records for cars that have a "Power Windows" feature and were serviced by a maintenance provider in the city of "Brasília"?

SELECT COUNT(*)  FROM maintenance_records JOIN car_features ON maintenance_records.car_id = car_features.car_id JOIN maintenance_providers ON maintenance_records.provider = maintenance_providers.id JOIN addresses ON maintenance_providers.address_id = addresses.id WHERE car_features.feature_type = 'Power Windows' AND addresses.city = 'Brasília';

CREATE TABLE clientes (   codigo TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nome Varchar NOT NULL,   cidade Varchar NOT NULL,   uf Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (codigo) );  CREATE TABLE produtos (   codigo TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   descricao Varchar NOT NULL,   precovenda Double NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   PRIMARY KEY (codigo) );  CREATE TABLE pedidos_dados_gerais (   numero TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   codigo_cli TEXT NOT NULL,   emissao Date NOT NULL,   valortotal Double NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   PRIMARY KEY (numero),   FOREIGN KEY (codigo_cli) REFERENCES clientes (codigo) );  CREATE TABLE pedidos_produtos (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   numeropedido TEXT NOT NULL,   codigoproduto TEXT NOT NULL,   quantidade Double NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   valorunitario Double NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   valortotal Double NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (numeropedido) REFERENCES pedidos_dados_gerais (numero),   FOREIGN KEY (codigoproduto) REFERENCES produtos (codigo) );

### Question:
What is the total value of orders placed by customers from the state of São Paulo?
SELECT SUM(pdg.valortotal) AS total_value FROM pedidos_dados_gerais pdg JOIN clientes cli ON pdg.codigo_cli = cli.codigo WHERE cli.uf = 'SP';

### Question:
Which employees have had their status changed more than twice in the last six months, and what are the average intervals between consecutive status changes for those employees, along with the most common reasons for these changes and the associated maintenance actions recorded in their maintenance records?

WITH recent_status_changes AS (     SELECT employee_id     FROM status_log     WHERE date >= date('now', '-6 months')     GROUP BY employee_id     HAVING COUNT(*) > 2 ), ordered_statuses AS (     SELECT s.employee_id, s.date,             ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.employee_id ORDER BY s.date) AS rn     FROM status_log s     WHERE s.date >= date('now', '-6 months')     AND s.employee_id IN (SELECT employee_id FROM recent_status_changes) ), status_intervals AS (     SELECT a.employee_id,            strftime('%s', b.date) - strftime('%s', a.date) AS interval_seconds     FROM ordered_statuses a     JOIN ordered_statuses b ON a.employee_id = b.employee_id AND a.rn = b.rn - 1 ), status_reasons AS (     SELECT employee_id, reason, COUNT(*) as reason_count     FROM status_log     WHERE date >= date('now', '-6 months')     AND employee_id IN (SELECT employee_id FROM recent_status_changes)     GROUP BY employee_id, reason ), top_reasons AS (     SELECT employee_id, reason     FROM status_reasons     WHERE (employee_id, reason_count) IN (         SELECT employee_id, MAX(reason_count)         FROM status_reasons         GROUP BY employee_id     ) ), maintenance_actions AS (     SELECT mr.employee_id, GROUP_CONCAT(mr.action_taken, ', ') AS actions     FROM maintenance_record mr     WHERE mr.employee_id IN (SELECT employee_id FROM recent_status_changes)     GROUP BY mr.employee_id ) SELECT      rsc.employee_id,     AVG(si.interval_seconds) AS avg_interval_seconds,     tr.reason AS most_common_reason,     ma.actions FROM recent_status_changes rsc LEFT JOIN status_intervals si ON rsc.employee_id = si.employee_id LEFT JOIN top_reasons tr ON rsc.employee_id = tr.employee_id LEFT JOIN maintenance_actions ma ON rsc.employee_id = ma.employee_id GROUP BY rsc.employee_id, tr.reason, ma.actions;

created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );

 CREATE TABLE Class (   Name Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   DepName Varchar NOT NULL,   Timetable Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (DepName, ColName) REFERENCES Department (Name, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE Studies (   RollNo Varchar NOT NULL,   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (RollNo, ColName) REFERENCES Students (RollNo, ColName),   FOREIGN KEY (CID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE Teachers (   Name Varchar NOT NULL,   MobileNo Varchar NOT NULL,   EmailId Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (ColName) REFERENCES College (Name) );  CREATE TABLE Teaches (   Name Varchar NOT NULL,   EmailId Varchar NOT NULL,   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Name, EmailId, ColName) REFERENCES Teachers (Name, EmailId, ColName),   FOREIGN KEY (CID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE Chapters (   Name Varchar NOT NULL,   Number Int NOT NULL,   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (CID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE Materials (   MatName Varchar NOT NULL,   ChapName Varchar NOT NULL,   link Varchar NOT NULL,   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (ChapName, ColName, CID) REFERENCES Chapters (Name, ColName, CID) );  CREATE TABLE AuthStudent (   RollNo Varchar NOT NULL,   Password Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (RollNo, ColName) REFERENCES Students (RollNo, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE AuthTeacher (   Name Varchar NOT NULL,   EmailId Varchar NOT NULL,   Password Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Name, EmailId, ColName) REFERENCES Teachers (Name, EmailId, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE Announcement (   Name Varchar NOT NULL,   EmailId Varchar NOT NULL,   Link Varchar NOT NULL,   ClassName Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   DepName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Name, EmailId, ColName) REFERENCES Teachers (Name, EmailId, ColName),   FOREIGN KEY (ClassName, DepName, ColName) REFERENCES Class (Name, DepName, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE StudentAchievements (   RollNo Varchar NOT NULL,   Achievement Varchar NOT NULL,   Description Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (RollNo, ColName) REFERENCES Students (RollNo, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE ProjectSubmissions (   RollNo Varchar NOT NULL,   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   ProjectName Varchar NOT NULL,   SubmissionDate Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (RollNo, ColName) REFERENCES Students (RollNo, ColName),   FOREIGN KEY (CID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE TeacherFeedback (   RollNo Varchar NOT NULL,   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FeedbackText Varchar NOT NULL,   SubmissionDate Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (RollNo, ColName) REFERENCES Students (RollNo, ColName),   FOREIGN KEY (CID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE SocialLinks (   RollNo Varchar NOT NULL,   Platform Varchar NOT NULL,   Link Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (RollNo, ColName) REFERENCES Students (RollNo, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE LibraryResources (   ResourceID Varchar NOT NULL,   Title Varchar NOT NULL,   ResourceType Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (ColName) REFERENCES College (Name) );  CREATE TABLE ExamDetails (   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   ExamName Varchar NOT NULL,   Date Date NOT NULL,   MaxMarks Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (CID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE Marks (   RollNo Varchar NOT NULL,   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   ExamName Varchar NOT NULL,   MarksObtained Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (RollNo, ColName) REFERENCES Students (RollNo, ColName),   FOREIGN KEY (CID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE ClassRepresentatives (   ClassName Varchar NOT NULL,   DepName Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   RepName Varchar NOT NULL,   Email Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (ClassName, DepName, ColName) REFERENCES Class (Name, DepName, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE CourseMaterials (   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   MaterialName Varchar NOT NULL,   Link Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (CID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE CourseSchedules (   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   StartTime Time NOT NULL,   EndTime Time NOT NULL,   WeekDays Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (CID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE StudentSemester (   RollNo Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   Semester Int NOT NULL,   Current bool NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (RollNo, ColName) REFERENCES Students (RollNo, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE StudentClubs (   RollNo Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   ClubName Varchar NOT NULL,   Role Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (RollNo, ColName) REFERENCES Students (RollNo, ColName) );  CREATE TABLE CoursePreRequisites (   CID Varchar NOT NULL,   ColName Varchar NOT NULL,   PrerequisiteCID Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (CID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName),   FOREIGN KEY (PrerequisiteCID, ColName) REFERENCES Course (CID, ColName) ); ### Question:
List the names of students who have submitted projects for the course with CID "CS101" in the college located in "Chennai, Tamil Nadu", and also show their corresponding project submission dates, course names, class names, department names, college names, and the average marks obtained by all students in that course.

SELECT      Students.Name,      ProjectSubmissions.SubmissionDate,      Course.Name AS CourseName,      Course.ClassName,      Course.DepName,      College.Name AS CollegeName,     (SELECT AVG(MarksObtained) FROM Marks WHERE CID = 'CS101' AND ColName = Course.ColName) AS AverageMarks FROM ProjectSubmissions JOIN Students ON ProjectSubmissions.RollNo = Students.RollNo JOIN Course ON ProjectSubmissions.CID = Course.CID AND ProjectSubmissions.ColName = Course.ColName JOIN College ON Course.ColName = College.Name WHERE ProjectSubmissions.CID = 'CS101'   AND College.City = 'Chennai'    AND College.State = 'Tamil Nadu';

CREATE TABLE data (   data_id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   label Integer NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE attr (   data_id Integer NOT NULL,   attr_id Integer NOT NULL,   val Float NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data (data_id) );  CREATE TABLE model (   attr_id Float NOT NULL,   val Double NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE metadata (   meta_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (meta_id) );  CREATE TABLE data_metadata (   data_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   meta_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id),   FOREIGN KEY (meta_id) REFERENCES metadata(meta_id) );  CREATE TABLE logs (   log_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   message TEXT NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (log_id) );  CREATE TABLE user_access (   access_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user TEXT NOT NULL,   access_time DATETIME NOT NULL,   data_id INTEGER,   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id),   PRIMARY KEY (access_id) );  CREATE TABLE tags (   tag_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   tag_name TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (tag_id) );  CREATE TABLE data_tags (   data_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   tag_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id),   FOREIGN KEY (tag_id) REFERENCES tags(tag_id) );  CREATE TABLE versions (   version_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   data_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   version_label TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (version_id),   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id) );  CREATE TABLE config (   config_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   parameter TEXT NOT NULL,   value TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (config_id) );  CREATE TABLE model_metadata (   model_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   meta_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (model_id) REFERENCES model(attr_id),   FOREIGN KEY (meta_id) REFERENCES metadata(meta_id) );  CREATE TABLE model_versions (   model_version_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   model_attr_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   version_label TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (model_version_id),   FOREIGN KEY (model_attr_id) REFERENCES model(attr_id) );  CREATE TABLE feature_importances (   feature_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   model_attr_id FLOAT NOT NULL,   importance FLOAT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (feature_id),   FOREIGN KEY (model_attr_id) REFERENCES model(attr_id) );  CREATE TABLE predictions (   prediction_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   model_attr_id FLOAT NOT NULL,   input_data_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   output FLOAT NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (prediction_id),   FOREIGN KEY (model_attr_id) REFERENCES model(attr_id),   FOREIGN KEY (input_data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id) );  CREATE TABLE job_logs (   job_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   job_name TEXT NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   start_time DATETIME NOT NULL,   end_time DATETIME,   PRIMARY KEY (job_id) );  CREATE TABLE job_details (   job_detail_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   job_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (job_id) REFERENCES job_logs(job_id),   PRIMARY KEY (job_detail_id) );  CREATE TABLE user_jobs (   user_job_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user TEXT NOT NULL,   job_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (job_id) REFERENCES job_logs(job_id),   PRIMARY KEY (user_job_id) );  CREATE TABLE data_jobs (   data_job_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   data_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   job_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id),   FOREIGN KEY (job_id) REFERENCES job_logs(job_id),   PRIMARY KEY (data_job_id) );  CREATE TABLE data_sources (   source_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   data_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   source_name TEXT NOT NULL,   source_type TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id),   PRIMARY KEY (source_id) );  CREATE TABLE data_source_metadata (   source_meta_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   source_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   meta_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (source_id) REFERENCES data_sources(source_id),   FOREIGN KEY (meta_id) REFERENCES metadata(meta_id),   PRIMARY KEY (source_meta_id) );  CREATE TABLE training_sessions (   session_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   job_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   model_attr_id FLOAT NOT NULL,   start_time DATETIME NOT NULL,   end_time DATETIME,   FOREIGN KEY (job_id) REFERENCES job_logs(job_id),   FOREIGN KEY (model_attr_id) REFERENCES model(attr_id),   PRIMARY KEY (session_id) );  CREATE TABLE hyperparameters (   param_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   session_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   parameter_name TEXT NOT NULL,   value TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES training_sessions(session_id),   PRIMARY KEY (param_id) );  CREATE TABLE data_validations (   validation_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   data_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   rule_name TEXT NOT NULL,   result TEXT NOT NULL,   validation_time DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id),   PRIMARY KEY (validation_id) );  CREATE TABLE data_lineage (   lineage_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   source_version_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   target_version_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   transformation TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (source_version_id) REFERENCES versions(version_id),   FOREIGN KEY (target_version_id) REFERENCES versions(version_id),   PRIMARY KEY (lineage_id) );  CREATE TABLE access_controls (   access_control_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   access_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   permission_level TEXT NOT NULL,   effective_from DATETIME NOT NULL,   effective_to DATETIME,   FOREIGN KEY (access_id) REFERENCES user_access(access_id),   PRIMARY KEY (access_control_id) );  CREATE TABLE notifications (   notification_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user TEXT NOT NULL,   message TEXT NOT NULL,   is_read BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (notification_id) );  CREATE TABLE audit_logs (   audit_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user TEXT NOT NULL,   action TEXT NOT NULL,   details TEXT,   log_time DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (audit_id) );  CREATE TABLE caching_records (   cache_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   data_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   cache_key TEXT NOT NULL,   cached_value BLOB NOT NULL,   expires_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id),   PRIMARY KEY (cache_id) );  CREATE TABLE model_performance (   performance_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   model_version_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   metric_name TEXT NOT NULL,   score FLOAT NOT NULL,   evaluated_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (model_version_id) REFERENCES model_versions(model_version_id),   PRIMARY KEY (performance_id) );  CREATE TABLE role_permissions (   role_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   role_name TEXT NOT NULL,   permissions TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY (role_id) );  CREATE TABLE dataset_splits (   split_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   data_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   split_type TEXT NOT NULL,   proportion FLOAT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id),   PRIMARY KEY (split_id) );  CREATE TABLE security_policies (   policy_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   policy_name TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   rule TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (policy_id) );  CREATE TABLE dependency_graphs (   dependency_id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   data_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   model_attr_id FLOAT NOT NULL,   relation_type TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_id) REFERENCES data(data_id),   FOREIGN KEY (model_attr_id) REFERENCES model(attr_id),   PRIMARY KEY (dependency_id) );

### Question:
For each user, count the number of notes they have created and the number of those notes that have at least one tag.

SELECT      users.id AS user_id,     COUNT(notes.id) AS total_notes,     COUNT(DISTINCT CASE          WHEN note_tags.note_id IS NOT NULL THEN notes.id      END) AS tagged_notes FROM users LEFT JOIN notes ON users.id = notes.created_by LEFT JOIN note_tags ON notes.id = note_tags.note_id GROUP BY users.id;

### Question:
Analyze the relationship between system error logs and user-reported issues by correlating error types with user activity, app versions, and device details. Include joins between systemErrorLog, userActivityLog, user, deviceInfo, appVersionUsage, paymentMethod, and userSession tables.

SELECT      systemErrorLog.errorType,     userActivityLog.actionType,     user.nickName,     deviceInfo.deviceType,     deviceInfo.osVersion,     appVersionUsage.appVersion,     paymentMethod.cardType,     userSession.deviceId,     userActivityLog.timestamp,     systemErrorLog.occurredAt FROM systemErrorLog JOIN userActivityLog ON systemErrorLog.occurredAt BETWEEN (userActivityLog.timestamp - 60) AND (userActivityLog.timestamp + 60) JOIN user ON userActivityLog.userId = user.id JOIN deviceInfo ON user.id = deviceInfo.userId JOIN appVersionUsage ON user.id = appVersionUsage.userId JOIN paymentMethod ON user.id = paymentMethod.userId JOIN userSession ON user.id = userSession.userId WHERE userActivityLog.actionType IN (5, 6) ;

CREATE TABLE users (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Text NOT NULL UNIQUE,   "key" Text NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE emails (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id Int NOT NULL,   email Text,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE login_attempts (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),   success BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT (0),   ip_address TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE followers (   follower_id TEXT NOT NULL,   followee_id TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),   PRIMARY KEY (follower_id, followee_id),   CHECK (follower_id != followee_id),   FOREIGN KEY (follower_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (followee_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user_blocks (   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   blocked_id TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, blocked_id),   CHECK (user_id != blocked_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (blocked_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user_settings (   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   setting_key TEXT NOT NULL,   value TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, setting_key),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE roles (   id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description TEXT );  CREATE TABLE user_roles (   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   role_id TEXT NOT NULL,   assigned_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, role_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES roles (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE permissions (   id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description TEXT );  CREATE TABLE role_permissions (   role_id TEXT NOT NULL,   permission_id TEXT NOT NULL,   granted_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),   PRIMARY KEY (role_id, permission_id),   FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES roles (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (permission_id) REFERENCES permissions (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE groups (   id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),   creator_id TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (creator_id) REFERENCES users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE INDEX idx_user_roles_role_id ON user_roles (role_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_role_permissions_permission_id ON role_permissions (permission_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_groups_name ON groups (name);  CREATE INDEX idx_group_members_user_id ON group_members (user_id);

### Question:
List the names of users who have the 'admin' role and are granted the 'delete_posts' permission through their roles, along with their user key.

SELECT users.name, users.key FROM users JOIN user_roles ON users.id = user_roles.user_id JOIN roles ON user_roles.role_id = roles.id JOIN role_permissions ON roles.id = role_permissions.role_id JOIN permissions ON role_permissions.permission_id = permissions.id WHERE roles.name = 'admin'   AND permissions.name = 'delete_posts';

### Question:
List the names of workstations that have had more than three security patches applied since January 1, 2023, and are running Windows 10 as their operating system.

SELECT W.Name FROM Workstation W JOIN Operating_System OS ON W.ID = OS.WorkstationID JOIN (     SELECT WorkstationID     FROM Security_patch     WHERE PatchDate >= '2023-01-01'     GROUP BY WorkstationID     HAVING COUNT(*) > 3 ) SP ON W.ID = SP.WorkstationID WHERE OS.OSName = 'Windows 10';

### Question:
Find the total number of orders that include both 'Pepperoni' and 'Mushroom' toppings.

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT po.order_id) FROM pizza_order po JOIN pizza_topping pt1 ON pt1.pizza_id = pizzas.id JOIN pizza_topping pt2 ON pt2.pizza_id = pizzas.id JOIN pizzas ON pizzas.id = po.pizza_id JOIN toppings t1 ON pt1.topping_id = t1.id AND t1.name = 'Pepperoni' JOIN toppings t2 ON pt2.topping_id = t2.id AND t2.name = 'Mushroom';

CREATE TABLE Webhook (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   "key" Text NOT NULL,   url Text NOT NULL,   createdAt Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Event (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   webhookId Integer NOT NULL,   platform Text NOT NULL,   createdAt Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (webhookId) REFERENCES Webhook (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE DeliveryStatus (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        eventId TEXT NOT NULL,        status TEXT NOT NULL,        retryCount INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,        lastAttempt TIMESTAMP,        FOREIGN KEY (eventId) REFERENCES Event (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE Payload (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        eventId TEXT NOT NULL,        data BLOB NOT NULL,        size INTEGER NOT NULL,        checksum TEXT,        FOREIGN KEY (eventId) REFERENCES Event (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE RetryAttempt (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        eventId TEXT NOT NULL,        attemptNumber INTEGER NOT NULL,        timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,        responseCode INTEGER,        FOREIGN KEY (eventId) REFERENCES Event (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE ErrorLog (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        eventId TEXT NOT NULL,        errorMessage TEXT NOT NULL,        timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,        resolved BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,        FOREIGN KEY (eventId) REFERENCES Event (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE WebhookLog (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        webhookId TEXT NOT NULL,        action TEXT NOT NULL,        details TEXT,        timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,        FOREIGN KEY (webhookId) REFERENCES Webhook (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE Subscription (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        userId TEXT NOT NULL,        webhookId TEXT NOT NULL,        active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,        subscribedAt TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,        FOREIGN KEY (webhookId) REFERENCES Webhook (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE EventType (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,        description TEXT );  CREATE TABLE Platform (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,        version TEXT );  CREATE TABLE User (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,        username TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,        createdAt TIMESTAMP NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Session (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        userId TEXT NOT NULL,        token TEXT NOT NULL,        expiresAt TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,        FOREIGN KEY (userId) REFERENCES User (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE Token (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        userId TEXT NOT NULL,        key TEXT NOT NULL,        scope TEXT NOT NULL,        createdAt TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,        FOREIGN KEY (userId) REFERENCES User (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE Device (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        deviceId TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,        type TEXT NOT NULL,        lastSeen TIMESTAMP );  CREATE TABLE Location (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        latitude REAL NOT NULL,        longitude REAL NOT NULL,        timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Statistics (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        webhookId TEXT NOT NULL,        date DATE NOT NULL,        totalCount INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,        successCount INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,        errorCount INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,        FOREIGN KEY (webhookId) REFERENCES Webhook (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE Configuration (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        webhookId TEXT NOT NULL,        name TEXT NOT NULL,        value TEXT NOT NULL,        FOREIGN KEY (webhookId) REFERENCES Webhook (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE Alert (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        webhookId TEXT NOT NULL,        threshold INTEGER NOT NULL,        triggerCount INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,        lastTriggered TIMESTAMP,        FOREIGN KEY (webhookId) REFERENCES Webhook (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE Tag (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,        description TEXT );  CREATE TABLE Metric (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        webhookId TEXT NOT NULL,        type TEXT NOT NULL,        value REAL NOT NULL,        timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,        FOREIGN KEY (webhookId) REFERENCES Webhook (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE Header (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        webhookId TEXT NOT NULL,        name TEXT NOT NULL,        value TEXT NOT NULL,        FOREIGN KEY (webhookId) REFERENCES Webhook (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE Permission (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        userId TEXT NOT NULL,        resourceId TEXT NOT NULL,        level INTEGER NOT NULL,        FOREIGN KEY (userId) REFERENCES User (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE Notification (        id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,        userId TEXT NOT NULL,        message TEXT NOT NULL,        read BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,        timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,        FOREIGN KEY (userId) REFERENCES User (id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE INDEX idx_deliverystatus_eventid ON DeliveryStatus (eventId);  CREATE INDEX idx_payload_eventid ON Payload (eventId);  CREATE INDEX idx_retryattempt_eventid ON RetryAttempt (eventId);  CREATE INDEX idx_errorlog_eventid ON ErrorLog (eventId); ### Question:
What is the total number of members who have accessed items stored in locations with storage slots of type 'can_request' and also have submitted at least one storage request for items in those locations, grouped by the member's handle?

SELECT m.fname || ' ' || m.lname AS handle, COUNT(*) AS total FROM (     SELECT DISTINCT a.member_id     FROM access_log a     JOIN storage_location l ON a.item_id = l.location_id     WHERE l.location_id IN (         SELECT location_id         FROM storage_slot         WHERE type_id = (             SELECT type_id             FROM storage_slot_type             WHERE name = 'can_request'         )     ) ) AS accessed_members JOIN (     SELECT DISTINCT r.member_id     FROM storage_request r     JOIN storage_slot s ON r.slot_id = s.slot_id     JOIN storage_location l ON s.location_id = l.location_id     WHERE l.location_id IN (         SELECT location_id         FROM storage_slot         WHERE type_id = (             SELECT type_id             FROM storage_slot_type             WHERE name = 'can_request'         )     ) ) AS requested_members ON accessed_members.member_id = requested_members.member_id JOIN members m ON accessed_members.member_id = m.member_id GROUP BY m.fname || ' ' || m.lname;

CREATE TABLE defis_lectures (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   image_url Varchar NOT NULL,   nbre_total_livre Int NOT NULL,   titre Varchar NOT NULL,   date_debut Date NOT NULL,   date_fin Date NOT NULL,   utilisateur_id Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE hibernate_sequence (   next_val BigInt NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE livres (   isbn Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   auteur Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   image_url Varchar NOT NULL,   titre Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE livres_defis (   defis_lectures_id Int NOT NULL,   livres_isbn Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE utili_livre_done (   note Int NOT NULL,   user_done_id Int NOT NULL,   livre_done_isbn Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE utilisateur_roles (   utilisateur_id Int NOT NULL,   roles Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE utilisateurs (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   password Varchar NOT NULL,   username Varchar NOT NULL,   image_name Varchar NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default.png',   nom Varchar NOT NULL,   prenom Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE utilisateurs_defis_lectures (   utilisateur_id Int NOT NULL,   defis_lectures_id Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE utilisateurs_livres (   utilisateurs_id Int NOT NULL,   livres_isbn Varchar NOT NULL );

### Question:
For each user who has completed at least 3 books in the "Fantasy" genre within reading challenges, list their username and the titles of the books they completed. Additionally, determine the average rating these users gave to their completed books, and sort the results by the highest average rating first.

WITH valid_completions AS (     SELECT          u.id AS user_id,         l.titre AS book_title,         uld.note AS rating     FROM utili_livre_done uld     JOIN utilisateurs u ON uld.user_done_id = u.id     JOIN livres l ON uld.livre_done_isbn = l.isbn     JOIN livres_defis ld ON l.isbn = ld.livres_isbn     JOIN utilisateurs_defis_lectures ud ON ud.defis_lectures_id = ld.defis_lectures_id AND ud.utilisateur_id = u.id     WHERE l.description LIKE '%Fantasy%' ) SELECT      u.username,     vc.book_title,     ROUND(AVG(vc.rating) OVER (PARTITION BY vc.user_id), 2) AS avg_rating FROM (     SELECT          user_id,         book_title,         rating     FROM valid_completions     WHERE user_id IN (         SELECT user_id         FROM valid_completions         GROUP BY user_id         HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3     ) ) vc JOIN utilisateurs u ON vc.user_id = u.id ORDER BY avg_rating DESC;

### Question:
What is the average rating of all products that have at least 5 reviews, grouped by the product's vendor?

SELECT      v.id AS vendor_id,     v.name AS vendor_name,     AVG(r.rating) AS average_rating FROM      c4_product_review r JOIN cart4j_product_c4_product p ON r.product_id = p.id JOIN c4_vendor v ON p.vendor_id = v.id GROUP BY      v.id, v.name HAVING      COUNT(r.id) >= 5;

CREATE TABLE users (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   login Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   email Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   password Varchar NOT NULL,   roleId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (roleId) REFERENCES roles (id) );  CREATE TABLE roles (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   role Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   privileges Text NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE pages (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   creator Int NOT NULL,   date Datetime NOT NULL,   url Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   lead Varchar NOT NULL,   content Text NOT NULL,   modified Timestamp NOT NULL,   blog Varchar NOT NULL DEFAULT 'N',   FOREIGN KEY (creator) REFERENCES users (id) );  CREATE TABLE tags (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description Text,   created_at Datetime NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE post_tags (   post_id Int NOT NULL,   tag_id Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (post_id) REFERENCES posts (id),   FOREIGN KEY (tag_id) REFERENCES tags (id),   PRIMARY KEY (post_id, tag_id) );  CREATE TABLE categories (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description Text,   created_at Datetime NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE user_preferences (   user_id Int NOT NULL,   key Varchar NOT NULL,   value Text NOT NULL,   created_at Datetime NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id),   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, key) );  CREATE TABLE page_views (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   page_id Int NOT NULL,   viewed_by Int NOT NULL,   viewed_at Datetime NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (page_id) REFERENCES pages (id),   FOREIGN KEY (viewed_by) REFERENCES users (id) );  CREATE TABLE likes (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   post_id Int,   page_id Int,   user_id Int NOT NULL,   created_at Datetime NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (post_id) REFERENCES posts (id),   FOREIGN KEY (page_id) REFERENCES pages (id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) ); ### Question:
List the total number of likes for each post that has been reported and has a rating of 3 or lower.

SELECT l.post_id, COUNT(*) AS total_likes FROM likes l WHERE l.post_id IN (     SELECT r.target_id     FROM reports r     WHERE r.target_type = 'post'     AND EXISTS (         SELECT 1         FROM post_ratings pr         WHERE pr.post_id = r.target_id         AND pr.rating_int <= 3     ) ) GROUP BY l.post_id;

### Question:
What is the average rating for notes that contain the hashtag "#Research" and are owned by a user named "johndoe"?

SELECT AVG(nr.rating) FROM note_ratings nr JOIN hashtags h ON nr.note_id = h.note_id JOIN note_ownerships no ON h.note_id = no.note_id JOIN users u ON no.user_id = u.id WHERE h.hashtag = '#Research' AND u.username = 'johndoe';

### Question:
What is the total amount spent by users who have a 'Premium' subscription plan, grouped by their device types?

SELECT udi.device_type, SUM(CAST(od.total_price AS REAL)) AS total_spent FROM user_subscription_plans usp JOIN user_device_info udi ON usp.user_id = udi.user_id JOIN order_details od ON usp.user_id = od.user_id WHERE usp.plan_type = 'Premium' GROUP BY udi.device_type;

### Question:
Retrieve all event participants who have RSVP'd as 'attending' to events created in the last 30 days, have at least one unread message, have a profile connection with another user, have a social link to a LinkedIn profile, and have earned at least one achievement badge in the last year.

SELECT ep.*  FROM sd_event_participants ep JOIN sd_event_calendar ec ON ep.event_id = ec.event_id JOIN sd_profile_users su ON ep.participant_user_id = su.user_id WHERE ep.rsvp_status = 'attending'   AND ec.created_at >= date('now', '-30 days')   AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1      FROM sd_user_messages m     WHERE m.receiver_profile_id = su.profile_id        AND m.is_read = 0   )   AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1      FROM sd_social_links sl     WHERE sl.profile_id = su.profile_id        AND sl.platform = 'LinkedIn'   )   AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1      FROM sd_profile_connections pc     WHERE pc.source_profile_id = su.profile_id         OR pc.target_profile_id = su.profile_id   )   AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1      FROM sd_achievement_badges ab     WHERE ab.user_id = su.user_id        AND ab.earned_time >= datetime('now', '-1 year')   );

### Question:
What is the total number of transactions involving spare parts with a stock level below 10 units?
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT dt.kode_transaksi) FROM detail_transaksi dt JOIN sparepart s ON dt.id_sparepart = s.id_sparepart WHERE s.stok < 10;

### Question:
List the organizations that have at least one project with a compliance event of type 'Safety Inspection' in the last six months.

SELECT DISTINCT o.Name, o.OrgUrl FROM Organizations o JOIN Projects p ON o.OrgId = p.OrgId JOIN ProjectComplianceEvents c ON p.ProjectId = c.ProjectId WHERE c.EventType = 'Safety Inspection'   AND c.EventDate BETWEEN DATE('now', '-6 months') AND DATE('now');

### Question:
What is the total count of sellers and users, and what is the ratio of users to sellers?

SELECT      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users) AS user_count,     (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sellers) AS seller_count,     (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users) * 1.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sellers) AS user_to_seller_ratio;

### Question:
Find the users who have applied for seller status and have also placed orders on items from at least three different sellers.
SELECT sa.user_id FROM seller_applications sa JOIN orders o ON sa.user_id = o.user_id GROUP BY sa.user_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT o.seller_id) >= 3;

CREATE TABLE PERSONNEL (   SSN Char NOT NULL UNIQUE,   FIRST_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   SECOND_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   THIRD_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   ADDRESS Varchar NOT NULL,   WORKING_HOURS Int NOT NULL,   TELEPHONE_NUMBER Char NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (SSN) );  CREATE TABLE SUBJECT_ROOMS (   NUMBER Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (NUMBER) );  CREATE TABLE TEACHERS (   SSN Char NOT NULL UNIQUE,   FIRST_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   SECOND_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   THIRD_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   ADDRESS Varchar NOT NULL,   WORKING_HOURS Int NOT NULL,   TELEPHONE_NUMBER Char NOT NULL,   HEADMASTER Char NOT NULL,   VICE_HEADMASTER Char NOT NULL,   HEAD_OF_ROOM Int NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (SSN),   FOREIGN KEY (HEADMASTER) REFERENCES TEACHERS (SSN),   FOREIGN KEY (VICE_HEADMASTER) REFERENCES TEACHERS (SSN),   FOREIGN KEY (HEAD_OF_ROOM) REFERENCES SUBJECT_ROOMS (NUMBER) );  CREATE TABLE MAJORS (   NAME Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   PRIMARY KEY (NAME) );  CREATE TABLE CLASSES (   NUMBER Int NOT NULL,   LETTER Char NOT NULL,   MAJOR Varchar NOT NULL,   LEADING_TEACHER Char NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (NUMBER, LETTER),   FOREIGN KEY (MAJOR) REFERENCES MAJORS (NAME),   FOREIGN KEY (LEADING_TEACHER) REFERENCES TEACHERS (SSN) );  CREATE TABLE GUARDIANS (   SSN Char NOT NULL UNIQUE,   FIRST_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   SECOND_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   THIRD_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   ADDRESS Varchar NOT NULL,   TELEPHONE_NUMBER Char NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (SSN) );  CREATE TABLE STUDENTS (   NUMBER Int NOT NULL,   FIRST_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   SECOND_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   THIRD_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   CLASS_NUMBER Int NOT NULL,   CLASS_LETTER Char NOT NULL,   GUARDIAN Char NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (NUMBER, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER),   FOREIGN KEY (GUARDIAN) REFERENCES GUARDIANS (SSN),   FOREIGN KEY (CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER) REFERENCES CLASSES (NUMBER, LETTER) );  CREATE TABLE MEDICAL_RECORDS (   MEDICAL_AUXILIARY Char NOT NULL,   CLASS_NUMBER Int NOT NULL,   CLASS_LETTER Char NOT NULL,   STUDENT_NUMBER Int NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (MEDICAL_AUXILIARY, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER, STUDENT_NUMBER),   FOREIGN KEY (MEDICAL_AUXILIARY) REFERENCES MEDICAL_AUXILIARIES (SSN),   FOREIGN KEY (CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER, STUDENT_NUMBER) REFERENCES STUDENTS (CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER, NUMBER) );  CREATE TABLE SUBJECTS (   NAME Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   PRIMARY KEY (NAME) );  CREATE TABLE SUBJECTS_IN_MAJORS (   MAJOR Varchar NOT NULL,   SUBJECT Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (MAJOR, SUBJECT),   FOREIGN KEY (MAJOR) REFERENCES MAJORS (NAME),   FOREIGN KEY (SUBJECT) REFERENCES SUBJECTS (NAME) );  CREATE TABLE ACCESSES (   GUARDIAN Char NOT NULL,   STUDENT Int NOT NULL,   CLASS_NUMBER Int NOT NULL,   CLASS_LETTER Char NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (GUARDIAN, STUDENT, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER),   FOREIGN KEY (GUARDIAN) REFERENCES GUARDIANS (SSN),   FOREIGN KEY (STUDENT, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER) REFERENCES E_GRADEBOOKS (STUDENT_NUMBER, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER) );  CREATE TABLE FILLS (   TEACHER Char NOT NULL,   STUDENT Int NOT NULL,   CLASS_NUMBER Int NOT NULL,   CLASS_LETTER Char NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (TEACHER, STUDENT, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER),   FOREIGN KEY (TEACHER) REFERENCES TEACHERS (SSN),   FOREIGN KEY (STUDENT, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER) REFERENCES E_GRADEBOOKS (STUDENT_NUMBER, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER) );  CREATE TABLE GRADES (   VALUE Decimal NOT NULL,   TEACHER Char NOT NULL,   SUBJECT Varchar NOT NULL,   STUDENT Int NOT NULL,   CLASS_NUMBER Int NOT NULL,   CLASS_LETTER Char NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (TEACHER, SUBJECT, STUDENT, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER),   FOREIGN KEY (TEACHER) REFERENCES TEACHERS (SSN),   FOREIGN KEY (SUBJECT) REFERENCES SUBJECTS (NAME),   FOREIGN KEY (STUDENT, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER) REFERENCES E_GRADEBOOKS (STUDENT_NUMBER, CLASS_NUMBER, CLASS_LETTER) );

### Question:
What is the total number of students in each major who have a medical record associated with a medical auxiliary whose working hours exceed 40 hours per week, along with the name of the head of the room for their respective class and the telephone number of the student's guardian?

SELECT      CLASSES.MAJOR,     COUNT(STUDENTS.NUMBER) AS total_students,     CLASSES.LEADING_TEACHER AS head_of_room,     GUARDIANS.TELEPHONE_NUMBER AS guardian_telephone FROM      STUDENTS JOIN CLASSES ON STUDENTS.CLASS_NUMBER = CLASSES.NUMBER AND STUDENTS.CLASS_LETTER = CLASSES.LETTER JOIN MEDICAL_RECORDS ON STUDENTS.NUMBER = MEDICAL_RECORDS.STUDENT_NUMBER JOIN MEDICAL_AUXILIARIES ON MEDICAL_RECORDS.MEDICAL_AUXILIARY = MEDICAL_AUXILIARIES.SSN JOIN ACCESSES ON STUDENTS.NUMBER = ACCESSES.STUDENT JOIN GUARDIANS ON ACCESSES.GUARDIAN = GUARDIANS.SSN WHERE      MEDICAL_AUXILIARIES.WORKING_HOURS > 40 GROUP BY      CLASSES.MAJOR,      CLASSES.LEADING_TEACHER,      GUARDIANS.TELEPHONE_NUMBER;

 CREATE TABLE user (   user_id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   username Varchar NOT NULL,   email Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   bio Text NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE comment_reaction (   reaction_id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id Int NOT NULL,   comment_id Int NOT NULL,    reaction_type Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Datetime NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user (user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (comment_id) REFERENCES comment (comment_id) );  CREATE TABLE comment_update (   update_id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   comment_id Int NOT NULL,   new_text Text NOT NULL,   updated_at Datetime NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (comment_id) REFERENCES comment (comment_id) );  CREATE TABLE user_block (   blocker_id Int NOT NULL,   blocked_id Int NOT NULL,   blocked_at Datetime NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (blocker_id) REFERENCES user (user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (blocked_id) REFERENCES user (user_id) ); ### Question:
What is the total number of likes for the post with the most views?
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_likes FROM post_like WHERE post_id = (     SELECT post_id     FROM post_view     GROUP BY post_id     ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC     LIMIT 1 );

CREATE TABLE category (   id BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   parentId BigInt NOT NULL,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   metaTitle Varchar NOT NULL,   slug Varchar NOT NULL,   content Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (parentId) REFERENCES category (id) );  CREATE TABLE post_category (   postId BigInt NOT NULL,   categoryId BigInt NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (categoryId) REFERENCES category (id) );  CREATE TABLE post_comment (   id BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   postId BigInt NOT NULL,   parentId BigInt NOT NULL,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   published TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   createdAt Datetime NOT NULL,   publishedAt Datetime NOT NULL,   content Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (parentId) REFERENCES post_comment (id) );  CREATE TABLE post_tag (   postId BigInt NOT NULL,   tagId BigInt NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (tagId) REFERENCES tag (id) );  CREATE TABLE tag (   id BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   metaTitle Varchar NOT NULL,   slug Varchar NOT NULL,   content Text NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE post (   id BigInt NOT NULL,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   content Text NOT NULL,   authorId BigInt NOT NULL,   published TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   createdAt Datetime NOT NULL,   publishedAt Datetime NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (authorId) REFERENCES users (id) );  CREATE TABLE page (   id BigInt NOT NULL,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   content Text NOT NULL,   slug Varchar NOT NULL,   published TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   createdAt Datetime NOT NULL,   publishedAt Datetime NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id) );  CREATE TABLE menu (   id BigInt NOT NULL,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   isPrimary TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   PRIMARY KEY (id) );  CREATE TABLE menu_item (   id BigInt NOT NULL,   menuId BigInt NOT NULL,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   url Varchar NOT NULL,   parentId BigInt,   position Int NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (menuId) REFERENCES menu (id),   FOREIGN KEY (parentId) REFERENCES menu_item (id) );  CREATE TABLE author (   id BigInt NOT NULL,   userId BigInt NOT NULL,   featuredMediaId BigInt,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (userId) REFERENCES users (id),   FOREIGN KEY (featuredMediaId) REFERENCES media (id) );  CREATE TABLE media (   id BigInt NOT NULL,   url Varchar NOT NULL,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   type Varchar NOT NULL,   uploadedAt Datetime NOT NULL,   authorId BigInt NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (authorId) REFERENCES users (id) );  CREATE TABLE site_setting (   id BigInt NOT NULL,   keyName Varchar NOT NULL,   value Text NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   UNIQUE (keyName) );  CREATE TABLE role (   id BigInt NOT NULL,   roleName Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   PRIMARY KEY (id) );  CREATE TABLE user_role (   userId BigInt NOT NULL,   roleId BigInt NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (userId, roleId),   FOREIGN KEY (userId) REFERENCES users (id),   FOREIGN KEY (roleId) REFERENCES role (id) );  CREATE TABLE permission (   id BigInt NOT NULL,   permissionName Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   PRIMARY KEY (id) );  CREATE TABLE role_permission (   roleId BigInt NOT NULL,   permissionId BigInt NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (roleId, permissionId),   FOREIGN KEY (roleId) REFERENCES role (id),   FOREIGN KEY (permissionId) REFERENCES permission (id) );  CREATE TABLE post_revision (   id BigInt NOT NULL,   postId BigInt NOT NULL,   content Text NOT NULL,   revisionNumber Int NOT NULL,   createdAt Datetime NOT NULL,   authorId BigInt NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (postId) REFERENCES post (id),   FOREIGN KEY (authorId) REFERENCES users (id) );  CREATE TABLE user_preference (   userId BigInt NOT NULL,   preferenceKey Varchar NOT NULL,   preferenceValue Text NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (userId, preferenceKey),   FOREIGN KEY (userId) REFERENCES users (id) );  CREATE TABLE subscription (   email Varchar NOT NULL,   subscribedAt Datetime NOT NULL,   lastNotified Datetime,   unsubscribed TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   PRIMARY KEY (email) );  CREATE TABLE author_bio (   authorId BigInt NOT NULL,   biography Text NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (authorId),   FOREIGN KEY (authorId) REFERENCES author (id) );  CREATE TABLE post_view (   postId BigInt NOT NULL,   viewCount BigInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   lastViewed Datetime NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (postId),   FOREIGN KEY (postId) REFERENCES post (id) );

### Question:
Show the names of the primary menus and the number of menu items in each.
SELECT m.name, COUNT(mi.id) AS itemCount FROM menu m JOIN menu_item mi ON m.id = mi.menuId WHERE m.isPrimary = 1 GROUP BY m.id;

CREATE TABLE users (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   country_code Int NOT NULL,   default_language Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (country_code) REFERENCES countries (id),   FOREIGN KEY (default_language) REFERENCES languages (id) );  CREATE TABLE languages (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   code Varchar NOT NULL,   name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE countries (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   code Varchar NOT NULL,   name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE path_owners (   user_id Int NOT NULL,   path_id Int NOT NULL,   type Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id),   FOREIGN KEY (path_id) REFERENCES paths (id) );  CREATE TABLE paths (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE path_steps (   step_id Int NOT NULL,   path_id Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (step_id) REFERENCES steps (id),   FOREIGN KEY (path_id) REFERENCES paths (id) );  CREATE TABLE steps (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE step_materials (   path_id Int NOT NULL,   step_id Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (path_id) REFERENCES materials (id),   FOREIGN KEY (step_id) REFERENCES steps (id) );  CREATE TABLE materials (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   link Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE material_attachments (   material_id Int NOT NULL,   attachment_id Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (material_id) REFERENCES materials (id),   FOREIGN KEY (attachment_id) REFERENCES attachments (id) );  CREATE TABLE attachments (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   description Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   updated_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE user_languages (   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   language_id Int NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, language_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id),   FOREIGN KEY (language_id) REFERENCES languages (id) );  CREATE TABLE path_ratings (   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   path_id Int NOT NULL,   rating Int NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, path_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id),   FOREIGN KEY (path_id) REFERENCES paths (id) );  CREATE TABLE material_views (   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   material_id Int NOT NULL,   viewed_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, material_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id),   FOREIGN KEY (material_id) REFERENCES materials (id) );  CREATE TABLE tags (   id Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE attachments_tags (   attachment_id Int NOT NULL,   tag_id Int NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (attachment_id, tag_id),   FOREIGN KEY (attachment_id) REFERENCES attachments (id),   FOREIGN KEY (tag_id) REFERENCES tags (id) );  CREATE TABLE step_completion (   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   step_id Int NOT NULL,   completed_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, step_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id),   FOREIGN KEY (step_id) REFERENCES steps (id) );  CREATE TABLE path_comments (   id Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   path_id Int NOT NULL,   comment_text Varchar NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id),   FOREIGN KEY (path_id) REFERENCES paths (id) );  CREATE TABLE comment_likes (   id Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   comment_id Int NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id),   FOREIGN KEY (comment_id) REFERENCES path_comments (id) );  CREATE TABLE user_preferences (   user_id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   theme Varchar,   notification_sound Int,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id) );  CREATE TABLE saved_paths (   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   path_id Int NOT NULL,   saved_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, path_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users (id),   FOREIGN KEY (path_id) REFERENCES paths (id) );

### Question:
List the total number of customer returns for products that have a stock quantity less than 30 in all warehouses, a product description length exceeding 1000 characters, and a product weight exceeding 1500 grams.

SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_returns FROM customer_returns WHERE product_id IN (     SELECT p.product_id     FROM products p     JOIN product_stock ps ON p.product_id = ps.product_id     WHERE CAST(p.product_description_lenght AS INTEGER) > 1000       AND CAST(p.product_weight_g AS INTEGER) > 1500     GROUP BY p.product_id     HAVING MAX(ps.stock_quantity) < 30 );

### Question:
Identify the top 5 most frequently used skills among active users, considering only those users who have at least one completed task in any activity and are not administrators.

SELECT      activity_skills.skillName,     COUNT(*) AS skillCount FROM      users u JOIN      user_skills us ON u.id = us.userId JOIN      activity_skills ON us.skillId = activity_skills.id WHERE      u.isActive = TRUE     AND NOT EXISTS (         SELECT 1         FROM users_roles ur         JOIN roles r ON ur.roleId = r.id         WHERE ur.userId = u.id           AND r.roleName = 'Administrator'     )     AND EXISTS (         SELECT 1         FROM users_activities a         JOIN activity_tasks t ON a.activityId = t.activityId         WHERE a.activityPerformer = u.id           AND t.isCompleted = TRUE     ) GROUP BY      activity_skills.skillName ORDER BY      skillCount DESC LIMIT 5;

### Question:
What is the total count of post views by users who have a notification preference enabled for type 12 and have not blocked the post's author?

SELECT SUM(pv.viewCount) FROM PostView pv JOIN Post p ON pv.postId = p.id JOIN NotificationPreference np ON pv.userId = np.userId     AND np.type = 12     AND np.isEnabled = 1 LEFT JOIN Block b ON pv.userId = b.userId AND b.blockedId = p.userId WHERE b.id IS NULL;

### Question:
Which FuelType has the highest average FillAmount for tanks associated with TTCs having a Weight less than 5000?

SELECT AVG(fr.Amount) AS AvgDieselFillAmount FROM dbo_FuelRecords fr JOIN dbo_TTCs t ON fr.TankId = t.TankId JOIN dbo_FuelTypes ft ON fr.FuelTypeId = ft.Id WHERE t.Velocity > 100 AND ft.Name = 'Diesel';

### Question:
Calculate total email sends and complaints per user, including the ratio and average retry count, for users with active sessions and feedback ratings above 3, excluding those with over five monthly webhooks.

SELECT      metrics.user_email_id,     SUM(metrics.total_send_count) AS total_sends,     SUM(metrics.complaints) AS total_complaints,     COALESCE(SUM(metrics.total_send_count) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(metrics.complaints), 0), 0) AS send_complaint_ratio,     COALESCE(retries.avg_retry_count, 0) AS avg_retry_count FROM      flask_email_validation_email_metrics metrics LEFT JOIN (     SELECT          logs.user_email_id,         AVG(restarts.retry_count) AS avg_retry_count     FROM          flask_email_validation_email_logs logs     JOIN          flask_email_validation_email_restarts restarts ON restarts.email_log_id = logs.id     GROUP BY          logs.user_email_id ) retries ON retries.user_email_id = metrics.user_email_id WHERE      EXISTS (         SELECT 1          FROM flask_email_validation_session_tokens tokens          WHERE tokens.user_email_id = metrics.user_email_id          AND tokens.session_expired > CURRENT_TIMESTAMP     )     AND EXISTS (         SELECT 1          FROM flask_email_validation_contact_feedback feedback          WHERE feedback.user_email_id = metrics.user_email_id          AND feedback.rating > 3     ) GROUP BY      metrics.user_email_id;

### Question:
Determine the total deposit amounts collected for each manufacturer, considering only those deposits that are part of orders with a status indicating they have been shipped.

SELECT m.name, SUM(d.deposit * CAST(od.product_quantity AS REAL)) AS total_deposit FROM fcs_deposits d JOIN fcs_product p ON d.id_product = p.id_product JOIN fcs_order_detail od ON d.id_product = od.product_id AND d.id_product_attribute = od.product_attribute_id JOIN fcs_orders o ON od.id_order = o.id_order JOIN fcs_manufacturer m ON p.id_manufacturer = m.id_manufacturer WHERE o.current_state = 'shipped' GROUP BY m.name;

### Question:
Which alumni have received job offers with a salary higher than the average salary for their position title, and what companies offered them those positions?

WITH avg_salary_per_position AS (     SELECT position_title, AVG(salary_offered) AS avg_salary     FROM employment_offers_gt_interns     GROUP BY position_title ) SELECT e.id_alumni, e.company_name, e.position_title, e.salary_offered FROM employment_offers_gt_interns e JOIN avg_salary_per_position a ON e.position_title = a.position_title WHERE e.salary_offered > a.avg_salary;

### Question:
List each food group along with the count of foods that have a weight measurement standard deviation above 10g and are referenced in at least two different data source lines, while also having a composite food component with a percentage less than 25%?

SELECT      fdg.FdGrp_Cd,      fdg.FdGrp_Desc,      COUNT(DISTINCT fd.NDB_No) AS food_count FROM      FOOD_DES fd INNER JOIN      FD_GROUP fdg ON fd.FdGrp_Cd = fdg.FdGrp_Cd INNER JOIN      COMPOSITE_FOOD cf ON fd.NDB_No = cf.Ndb_No AND cf.Percentage < 25 INNER JOIN      WEIGHT w ON fd.NDB_No = w.NDB_No AND w.Std_Dev > 10 INNER JOIN (     SELECT NDB_No     FROM DATSRCLN     GROUP BY NDB_No     HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT DataSrc_ID) >= 2 ) ds ON fd.NDB_No = ds.NDB_No GROUP BY      fdg.FdGrp_Cd,      fdg.FdGrp_Desc;

### Question:
For each admin, provide the total number of community posts (comunity) they have moderated (post_moderation) where the moderation action was 'approved', the average number of likes per approved post, and the count of those posts that received more than 10 comments. Include the admin's name and the count of unread notifications they have received. Filter out admins who have not moderated any posts in the last 6 months using the user_activity_logs table.

SELECT      admin.name,     COUNT(pm.post_id) AS total_approved_posts,     AVG(c.like_count) AS avg_likes_per_post,     SUM(CASE WHEN c.comment_count > 10 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_comment_count,     COUNT(n.id) AS unread_notifications FROM admin JOIN post_moderation pm ON admin.id = pm.admin_id AND pm.moderation_action = 'approved' JOIN comunity c ON pm.post_id = c.post_id LEFT JOIN notifications n ON admin.id = n.user_id AND n.is_read = 0 WHERE EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM user_activity_logs ual     WHERE ual.user_id = admin.id     AND ual.timestamp >= datetime('now', '-6 months') ) GROUP BY admin.id, admin.name;

### Question:
Calculate the total quantity of products on hand for customers who have placed orders with a quantity greater than 5.

SELECT SUM(poh.quantity)  FROM products_on_hand poh WHERE poh.product_id IN (     SELECT DISTINCT o.product_id     FROM orders o     WHERE o.purchaser IN (         SELECT DISTINCT o2.purchaser         FROM orders o2         WHERE o2.quantity > 5     ) );

### Question:
Show the names of teachers who have scheduled appointments for projects in multiple education groups.

SELECT E.idTeacher FROM Teckmeb_Appointment A JOIN Teckmeb_Project P ON A.idProject = P.idProject JOIN Teckmeb_Education E ON P.idGroup = E.idGroup GROUP BY E.idTeacher HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT E.idGroup) > 1;

### Schema:
CREATE TABLE device (   device_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   device_state TinyInt NOT NULL,   device_name Varchar NOT NULL,   account_id TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (device_id) );  CREATE TABLE grouping (   grouping_name Varchar NOT NULL,   grouping_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   account_id TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (grouping_id) );  CREATE TABLE traffic (   traffic_ip Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   traffic_block TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   PRIMARY KEY (traffic_ip) );  CREATE TABLE traffic_detail (   traffic_ip Varchar NOT NULL,   traffic_time Datetime NOT NULL,   traffic_id TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   FOREIGN KEY (traffic_ip) REFERENCES traffic (traffic_ip) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

### Question:
For each account_id that has at least one grouping with grouping_name 'Security', calculate the average device_state of devices with device_name containing 'Camera' that have at least one traffic_detail record from the last 14 days where traffic_block = 1, and ensure the traffic_ip is associated with at least five traffic_detail records in total.

WITH security_accounts AS (     SELECT DISTINCT account_id     FROM grouping     WHERE grouping_name = 'Security' ), camera_devices AS (     SELECT d.device_id     FROM device d     WHERE d.device_name LIKE '%Camera%'     AND d.account_id IN (SELECT account_id FROM security_accounts) ), qualified_ips AS (     SELECT td.traffic_ip     FROM traffic_detail td     WHERE td.traffic_time >= DATE('now', '-14 days')     AND EXISTS (         SELECT 1         FROM traffic t         WHERE t.traffic_ip = td.traffic_ip         AND t.traffic_block = 1     )     GROUP BY td.traffic_ip     HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5 ) SELECT      sa.account_id,     AVG(d.device_state) AS average_device_state FROM security_accounts sa JOIN device d ON sa.account_id = d.account_id WHERE d.device_id IN (     SELECT cd.device_id     FROM camera_devices cd ) AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM qualified_ips q     WHERE q.traffic_ip IN (         SELECT td.traffic_ip         FROM traffic_detail td         WHERE td.traffic_time >= DATE('now', '-14 days')         AND EXISTS (             SELECT 1             FROM traffic t             WHERE t.traffic_ip = td.traffic_ip             AND t.traffic_block = 1         )     ) ) GROUP BY sa.account_id;

### Question:
For workflow runs tagged with 'environment' as 'production', what is the distribution of their associated 'region' tags?

SELECT     r.value AS region,     COUNT(*) AS count FROM     workflow_run_tag AS e JOIN     workflow_run_tag AS r ON e.workflow_run_id = r.workflow_run_id WHERE     e.tag = 'environment' AND e.value = 'production'      AND r.tag = 'region' GROUP BY r.value;

What is the average product rating given by users in the last 30 days?

CREATE TABLE person (   cpf Text NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Text NOT NULL,   phone Text NOT NULL,   birth Text NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (cpf) );  CREATE TABLE piece (   id Text NOT NULL UNIQUE,   value Real NOT NULL,   date Text NOT NULL,   person_cpf Text NOT NULL,   title Text NOT NULL,   "desc" Text NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (person_cpf) REFERENCES person (cpf) ON DELETE SET NULL );  CREATE TABLE exposition (   id Text NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title Text NOT NULL,   date Text NOT NULL,   time Text NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id) );  CREATE TABLE creates (   person_cpf Text NOT NULL,   piece_id Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (person_cpf) REFERENCES person (cpf) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (piece_id) REFERENCES piece (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE visits (   person_cpf Text NOT NULL,   exposition_id Text NOT NULL,   enter Text NOT NULL,   leave Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (person_cpf) REFERENCES person (cpf) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (exposition_id) REFERENCES exposition (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE displays (   piece_id Text NOT NULL,   exposition_id Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (piece_id) REFERENCES piece (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (exposition_id) REFERENCES exposition (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );

### Question:
List the names of all persons who have created at least one piece that is currently displayed in an exposition.
SELECT DISTINCT person.name FROM person JOIN piece ON person.cpf = piece.person_cpf JOIN displays ON piece.id = displays.piece_id;

### Question:
Find the average time between a task's creation and its first log entry, for tasks that have at least one log.

SELECT AVG(JULIANDAY(fl.create_time) - JULIANDAY(t.create_time)) AS average_time_difference FROM sys_task_assignment t JOIN (   SELECT task_id, MIN(create_time) AS create_time   FROM sys_task_log   GROUP BY task_id ) fl ON t.task_id = fl.task_id;

### Question:
For each unit, show the unit name and the count of active tasks (status not "completed") assigned to users in that unit.

SELECT      ud.unitName,     COUNT(ut.taskId) AS activeTaskCount FROM      t_unit_details ud LEFT JOIN      t_user_unit_assignment uua ON ud.unitId = uua.unitId AND uua.removed = 0 LEFT JOIN      t_unit_task ut ON uua.userId = ut.assignedTo AND ut.status != 'completed' GROUP BY      ud.unitName;

### Question:
Find the total number of messages with high priority level and calculate their average views count compared to non-high priority messages.

SELECT      COUNT(CASE WHEN mp.priority_level = 'high' THEN ma.message_id END) AS total_high_priority,     AVG(CASE WHEN mp.priority_level = 'high' THEN ma.views_count END) AS avg_high_views,     AVG(CASE WHEN mp.priority_level != 'high' OR mp.priority_level IS NULL THEN ma.views_count END) AS avg_non_high_views FROM yzn_message_analytics ma LEFT JOIN yzn_message_priority mp ON ma.message_id = mp.message_id;

### Question:
Find the latest login time for each user who has at least one active two-factor authentication method enrolled.
SELECT l.user_id, MAX(l.login_time) AS latest_login FROM login_history l INNER JOIN two_factor_enrollments tfe ON l.user_id = tfe.user_id WHERE tfe.verified = 1 GROUP BY l.user_id;

### Question:
Retrieve the planned liquid transfers that have associated robotic arm positions, calibration records, and maintenance schedules. For each such transfer, show the transfer type, the number of robotic arm positions involved, the last calibration date of the equipment used, the next scheduled maintenance date, and the technician who performed the last calibration. Filter to include only transfers with more than two robotic arm positions and where the equipment was last calibrated more than six months ago.

SELECT plt.transfer_type,        COUNT(rap.position_id) AS num_positions,        (SELECT cr.calibration_date FROM calibration_records cr WHERE cr.equipment_name = 'robotic_arm' ORDER BY cr.calibration_date DESC LIMIT 1) AS last_calibration_date,        (SELECT ms.scheduled_date FROM maintenance_schedule ms WHERE ms.equipment_name = 'robotic_arm' ORDER BY ms.scheduled_date ASC LIMIT 1) AS next_maintenance_date,        (SELECT cr.technician FROM calibration_records cr WHERE cr.equipment_name = 'robotic_arm' ORDER BY cr.calibration_date DESC LIMIT 1) AS last_calibration_technician FROM planned_liquid_transfer plt JOIN robotic_arm_positions rap ON plt.planned_liquid_transfer_id = rap.planned_liquid_transfer_id GROUP BY plt.planned_liquid_transfer_id, plt.transfer_type HAVING COUNT(rap.position_id) > 2    AND (SELECT cr.calibration_date FROM calibration_records cr WHERE cr.equipment_name = 'robotic_arm' ORDER BY cr.calibration_date DESC LIMIT 1) < DATE('now', '-6 months');

### Question:
What is the total number of hours worked by employees who have completed the "Python for Data Analysis" training program?

SELECT SUM(W.HOURS) AS total_hours_worked FROM WORKS_ON W WHERE W.ESSN IN (     SELECT E.SSN     FROM EMPLOYEE E     JOIN EMPLOYEE_TRAINING ET ON E.SSN = ET.ESSN     JOIN TRAINING_PROGRAMS TP ON ET.TRAINING_ID = TP.TRAINING_ID     WHERE TP.TITLE = 'Python for Data Analysis' );

### Question:
For every bulk_load_partition with more than 10,000 rows, include the job date from bulk_load_job, the associated window_id from data_import_window_stats, and the count of temp_hashed_set_member entries for the job. Filter for partitions created in 2023.

SELECT      bj.job_date,     dws.window_id,     (bp.end_row - bp.start_row + 1) AS row_count,     COUNT(thsm.data_order) AS temp_hashed_set_member_count FROM      bulk_load_partition bp JOIN      bulk_load_job bj ON bp.job_id = bj.bulk_load_job_id JOIN      data_import_window_stats dws JOIN      temp_hashed_set_member thsm ON thsm.job = bp.job_id WHERE      strftime('%Y', bj.job_date) = '2023'     AND (bp.end_row - bp.start_row + 1) > 10000 GROUP BY      bp.partition_id;

### Question:
Describe the event names, performance dates, and seat types for which customers from the state of California have made purchases, including the total amount spent by each customer per event.

SELECT e.event_name, p.performance_date, s.seat_type, o.customer_id, SUM(1.0 * oi.item_price * oi.quantity) AS total_spent FROM "order" o JOIN customer_address ca ON o.customer_id = ca.customer_id JOIN order_item oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN inventory i ON oi.inventory_id = i.inventory_id JOIN seat s ON i.seat_id = s.seat_id JOIN performance p ON i.event_id = p.event_id JOIN event e ON p.event_id = e.event_id WHERE ca.state = 'CA' GROUP BY e.event_name, p.performance_date, s.seat_type, o.customer_id;
