### Question:
For users with custom theme preferences, show those who have sent private messages but never participated in public rooms, have no active friendships, and have content filters that block specific keywords while having at least one badge awarded.

SELECT utc.user_id FROM user_theme_customizations utc WHERE EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM private_message_conversations pmc     WHERE pmc.user1_id = utc.user_id OR pmc.user2_id = utc.user_id ) AND NOT EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM room_participants rp     WHERE rp.user_id = utc.user_id ) AND NOT EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM friendship f     WHERE (f.initiator_id = utc.user_id OR f.receiver_id = utc.user_id)     AND f.status = 'active' ) AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM user_content_filters ucf     WHERE ucf.user_id = utc.user_id     AND ucf.filter_type = 'block'     AND ucf.enabled = 1 ) AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM user_badges ub     WHERE ub.user_id = utc.user_id );

### Question:
Which VHF accessory type has the highest number of accessories assigned to it, and how many accessories are there in total for that type?

SELECT      t.libelleVhfAccessoireType AS type_name,     COUNT(a.idVhfAccessoire) AS total_accessories FROM      VHF_ACCESSOIRES a JOIN      VHF_ACCESSOIRES_TYPES t  ON      a.idVhfAccessoireType = t.idVhfAccessoireType GROUP BY      t.idVhfAccessoireType ORDER BY      total_accessories DESC LIMIT 1;

### Question:
For each cluster, how many back-up configurations exist on its nodes, and what is the average number of patches installed on those nodes? Include the cluster name and type, and filter out clusters without any node or backup data.

SELECT      clusters.cluster_name,     clusters.clustertype,     COUNT(node_backups.entid) AS backup_count,     AVG(COALESCE(patches.patch_count, 0)) AS average_patch_count FROM clusters JOIN clusters_node ON clusters.entid = clusters_node.clusters_entid JOIN node ON clusters_node.nodes_entid = node.entid LEFT JOIN node_backups ON node.entid = node_backups.node_entid LEFT JOIN (     SELECT node_entid, COUNT(*) AS patch_count     FROM system_patches     GROUP BY node_entid ) AS patches ON node.entid = patches.node_entid GROUP BY clusters.entid, clusters.cluster_name, clusters.clustertype HAVING backup_count > 0;

### Question:
What is the total donation value for projects in the country named 'Canada'?

### Question:
Find the names of teams that have at least one goal due in the next month and are working on a product with a rating above 4.

SELECT c.name FROM cycles_team c WHERE EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM product_reviews pr     WHERE pr.product_id = c.product_id     AND pr.rating > 4 ) AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM team_goals g     WHERE g.team_id1 = c.idteam1     AND g.team_id2 = c.idteam2     AND g.due_date BETWEEN '2023-10-01' AND '2023-10-31' );

CREATE TABLE validators (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   public_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE signed_blocks (   validator_id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   slot Numeric NOT NULL UNIQUE,   signing_root TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (validator_id) REFERENCES validators (id) );  CREATE TABLE signed_attestations (   validator_id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   source_epoch Numeric NOT NULL,   target_epoch Numeric NOT NULL UNIQUE,   signing_root TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (validator_id) REFERENCES validators (id) );  CREATE TABLE slashing_events (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   validator_id TEXT NOT NULL,   slot NUMERIC NOT NULL,   reason TEXT,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (validator_id) REFERENCES validators (id) );  CREATE TABLE validator_rewards (   validator_id TEXT NOT NULL,   epoch NUMERIC NOT NULL,   amount INTEGER NOT NULL,   type TEXT NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (validator_id) REFERENCES validators (id) );  CREATE TABLE validator_penalties (   validator_id TEXT NOT NULL,   epoch NUMERIC NOT NULL,   amount INTEGER NOT NULL,   reason TEXT NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (validator_id) REFERENCES validators (id) );  CREATE TABLE validator_stake_history (   validator_id TEXT NOT NULL,   epoch NUMERIC NOT NULL,   stake_before INTEGER NOT NULL,   stake_after INTEGER NOT NULL,   event_type TEXT NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (validator_id) REFERENCES validators (id) );  CREATE TABLE slash_protection_records (   validator_id TEXT NOT NULL,   slot NUMERIC NOT NULL,   event_type TEXT NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (validator_id) REFERENCES validators (id) );  CREATE INDEX idx_slashing_events_validator ON slashing_events (validator_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_validator_rewards_validator ON validator_rewards (validator_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_validator_penalties_validator ON validator_penalties (validator_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_validator_stake_history_validator ON validator_stake_history (validator_id);

### Question:
List the public keys of validators who have signed attestations with a source epoch of 1000 and have a stake after value greater than 32000000 in epoch 1500.

SELECT DISTINCT CAST(sa.validator_id AS TEXT) AS validator_id, v.public_key FROM signed_attestations sa JOIN validators v ON CAST(sa.validator_id AS TEXT) = v.id WHERE sa.source_epoch = 1000   AND sa.validator_id IN (     SELECT vs.validator_id     FROM validator_stake_history vs     WHERE vs.epoch = 1500 AND vs.stake_after > 32000000   );

### Question:
Find the average cache size of playlists that have a cache TTL greater than the default TTL for their resource type.

WITH playlist_info AS (     SELECT pci.playlist_id, pci.cache_size, pttl.ttl_seconds     FROM playlist_cache_info AS pci     JOIN playlist_cache_ttl AS pttl       ON pci.playlist_id = pttl.playlist_id ), default_playlist_ttl AS (     SELECT default_ttl_seconds AS playlist_default_ttl_seconds     FROM cache_ttl_settings     WHERE resource_type = 'playlist' ) SELECT AVG(playlist_info.cache_size) AS average_cache_size FROM playlist_info WHERE playlist_info.ttl_seconds > (SELECT playlist_default_ttl_seconds FROM default_playlist_ttl);

CREATE TABLE USERS (   user_id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   full_name Varchar NOT NULL,   email_address Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   phone_number Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_password Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id) ); ### Question:
List the number of employees in each department who have a primary address in New York City.

SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(*) AS employee_count FROM t_employee e JOIN t_employee_address ea ON e.id = ea.employee_id AND ea.primary_address = 1 JOIN t_address a ON ea.address_id = a.id JOIN t_department d ON e.dept_id = d.id WHERE a.city = 'New York City' GROUP BY d.id;

### Question:
Which alert type has the highest number of enabled alerts across all clusters, and what is the total count?

SELECT ad.alert_type, COUNT(ai.id) AS total_count FROM alert_instances ai JOIN alert_definitions ad ON ai.alert_definition_id = ad.id WHERE ad.enabled = 1 GROUP BY ad.alert_type ORDER BY total_count DESC LIMIT 1;

### Schema:
CREATE TABLE Schedules (   scheduleID Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   schedule_name Varchar,   schedule_description Varchar,   PRIMARY KEY (scheduleID) );  CREATE TABLE Transitions (   transitionID Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   scheduleID Integer,   transition_offset Int NOT NULL,   transition_state TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (transitionID),   FOREIGN KEY (scheduleID) REFERENCES Schedules (scheduleID) );  CREATE TABLE Sensors (   sensorID Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   sensor_name Varchar,   sensor_description Varchar,   sensor_value Int,   sensor_readtime Timestamp,   PRIMARY KEY (sensorID) );  CREATE TABLE Channels (   channelID Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   channel_name Varchar,   channel_description Varchar,   scheduleID Integer,   sensorID Integer,   channel_enabled TEXT NOT NULL,   channel_state TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (channelID),   FOREIGN KEY (scheduleID) REFERENCES Schedules (scheduleID),   FOREIGN KEY (sensorID) REFERENCES Sensors (sensorID) );  CREATE TABLE TokenQ (   tokenID Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   token Varchar NOT NULL,   type Varchar NOT NULL,   value Integer,   PRIMARY KEY (tokenID) );  CREATE TABLE commandQ (   commandID Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   command Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (commandID) );  CREATE TABLE KeyWords (   keywordID Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   keyword Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description Varchar,   keycode Integer NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (keywordID) );

### Question:
Which channels are associated with schedules that have a description containing the word 'maintenance', and what are the corresponding sensor values and transition offsets for each transition linked to those schedules, sorted by the most recent sensor read time?

SELECT Channels.channelID, Channels.channel_name, Transitions.transition_offset, Sensors.sensor_value, Sensors.sensor_readtime FROM Schedules JOIN Channels ON Schedules.scheduleID = Channels.scheduleID JOIN Transitions ON Schedules.scheduleID = Transitions.scheduleID JOIN Sensors ON Channels.sensorID = Sensors.sensorID WHERE Schedules.schedule_description LIKE '%maintenance%' ORDER BY Sensors.sensor_readtime DESC;

### Question:
Determine the average number of items per shopping cart for users who have more than five items in their carts, excluding carts created in the last 24 hours.

SELECT AVG(total_items) AS average_items_per_cart FROM (     SELECT SUM(tb_itemcarrinho.quantidade) AS total_items     FROM tb_carrinho     JOIN tb_itemcarrinho ON tb_carrinho.id_carrinho = tb_itemcarrinho.id_carrinho     WHERE tb_carrinho.data_criacao < datetime('now', '-24 hours')     GROUP BY tb_carrinho.id_carrinho     HAVING total_items > 5 );

### Schema:
CREATE TABLE album (   id Char NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   artist Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE track (   album Char NOT NULL,   dsk Integer NOT NULL,   posn Integer NOT NULL,   song Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (album) REFERENCES album (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE artist (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   name VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE,   bio TEXT );  CREATE TABLE album_artist (   album CHAR NOT NULL,   artist_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (album, artist_id),   FOREIGN KEY (album) REFERENCES album (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (artist_id) REFERENCES artist (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE genre (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   name VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE album_genre (   album CHAR NOT NULL,   genre_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (album, genre_id),   FOREIGN KEY (album) REFERENCES album (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (genre_id) REFERENCES genre (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE track_genre (   album CHAR NOT NULL,   dsk INTEGER NOT NULL,   posn INTEGER NOT NULL,   genre_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (album, dsk, posn, genre_id),   FOREIGN KEY (album, dsk, posn) REFERENCES track (album, dsk, posn) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (genre_id) REFERENCES genre (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   username VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE,   email VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE user_rating (   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   album CHAR NOT NULL,   rating INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5),   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, album),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (album) REFERENCES album (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );

### Question:
For each genre, identify the top 3 most frequently used genres in track listings that are not the same as the album's primary genre, along with the average rating of those albums by users, sorted by genre popularity in descending order.

WITH non_primary_genres AS (     SELECT DISTINCT t.album, tg.genre_id     FROM track t     JOIN track_genre tg ON t.album = tg.album AND t.dsk = tg.dsk AND t.posn = tg.posn     JOIN album_genre ag ON t.album = ag.album     WHERE tg.genre_id != ag.genre_id ), album_avg_ratings AS (     SELECT n.album, AVG(ur.rating) AS avg_rating     FROM non_primary_genres n     JOIN user_rating ur ON n.album = ur.album     GROUP BY n.album ), genre_counts AS (     SELECT tg.genre_id, COUNT(*) AS track_count     FROM track t     JOIN track_genre tg ON t.album = tg.album AND t.dsk = tg.dsk AND t.posn = tg.posn     JOIN album_genre ag ON t.album = ag.album     WHERE tg.genre_id != ag.genre_id     GROUP BY tg.genre_id ), genre_avg_ratings AS (     SELECT n.genre_id, AVG(aar.avg_rating) AS avg_rating     FROM non_primary_genres n     JOIN album_avg_ratings aar ON n.album = aar.album     GROUP BY n.genre_id ) SELECT g.name AS genre_name, gc.track_count, gar.avg_rating FROM genre_counts gc JOIN genre_avg_ratings gar ON gc.genre_id = gar.genre_id JOIN genre g ON gc.genre_id = g.id ORDER BY gc.track_count DESC LIMIT 3;

### Schema:
CREATE TABLE s_customer (   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   customer_name TEXT NOT NULL,   phone TEXT NOT NULL,   address TEXT NOT NULL,   city TEXT NOT NULL,   state TEXT NOT NULL,   country TEXT NOT NULL,   zip_code TEXT NOT NULL,   credit_rating TEXT NOT NULL,   sales_rep_id TEXT NOT NULL,   region_id TEXT NOT NULL,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   preferred_customer TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'N',   shipping_method TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'M',   FOREIGN KEY (sales_rep_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id),   FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_department (   department_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   department_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   region_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_employee (   employee_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   employee_last_name TEXT NOT NULL,   employee_first_name TEXT NOT NULL,   userid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   start_date Date NOT NULL,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   manager_id TEXT NOT NULL,   title TEXT NOT NULL,   department_id TEXT NOT NULL,   salary TEXT NOT NULL,   commission_pct TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (manager_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id),   FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES s_department (department_id),   FOREIGN KEY (title) REFERENCES s_title (title) );  CREATE TABLE s_inventory (   product_id TEXT NOT NULL,   warehouse_id TEXT NOT NULL,   amount_in_stock TEXT NOT NULL,   reorder_point TEXT NOT NULL,   max_in_stock TEXT NOT NULL,   out_of_stock_explanation TEXT NOT NULL,   restock_date Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id),   FOREIGN KEY (warehouse_id) REFERENCES s_warehouse (warehouse_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_item (   order_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   item_id TEXT NOT NULL,   product_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   price TEXT NOT NULL,   quantity TEXT NOT NULL,   quantity_shipped TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES s_order (order_id),   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_longtext (   longtext_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   use_filename TEXT NOT NULL,   filename TEXT NOT NULL,   text TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE s_order (   order_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   date_ordered Date NOT NULL,   date_shipped Date NOT NULL,   sales_rep_id TEXT NOT NULL,   total TEXT NOT NULL,   payment_type TEXT NOT NULL,   order_filled TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES s_customer (customer_id),   FOREIGN KEY (sales_rep_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_product (   product_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   product_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   short_desc TEXT NOT NULL,   longtext_id TEXT NOT NULL,   image_id TEXT NOT NULL,   suggested_wholesale_price TEXT NOT NULL,   wholesale_units TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (longtext_id) REFERENCES s_longtext (longtext_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_region (   region_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   region_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE s_title (   title TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE s_warehouse (   warehouse_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   region_id TEXT NOT NULL,   phone TEXT NOT NULL,   address TEXT NOT NULL,   city TEXT NOT NULL,   state TEXT NOT NULL,   country TEXT NOT NULL,   zip_code TEXT NOT NULL,   manager_id TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (manager_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id),   FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_supplier (   supplier_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   supplier_name TEXT NOT NULL,   contact_name TEXT NOT NULL,   phone TEXT NOT NULL,   address TEXT NOT NULL,   city TEXT NOT NULL,   state TEXT NOT NULL,   country TEXT NOT NULL,   zip_code TEXT NOT NULL,   region_id TEXT,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_purchase_order (   purchase_order_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   supplier_id TEXT NOT NULL,   order_date DATE NOT NULL,   expected_delivery_date DATE NOT NULL,   total_amount TEXT NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (supplier_id) REFERENCES s_supplier (supplier_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_shipper (   shipper_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   shipper_name TEXT NOT NULL,   phone TEXT NOT NULL,   address TEXT NOT NULL,   region_id TEXT,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_order_shipping (   order_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   shipper_id TEXT NOT NULL,   tracking_number TEXT NOT NULL,   shipping_cost TEXT NOT NULL,   expected_delivery_date DATE NOT NULL,   actual_delivery_date DATE,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES s_order (order_id),   FOREIGN KEY (shipper_id) REFERENCES s_shipper (shipper_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_customer_return (   return_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   order_id TEXT NOT NULL,   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   return_date DATE NOT NULL,   total_amount TEXT NOT NULL,   reason_id TEXT NOT NULL,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES s_order (order_id),   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES s_customer (customer_id),   FOREIGN KEY (reason_id) REFERENCES s_return_reason (reason_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_return_reason (   reason_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   reason_name TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE s_employee_shift (   shift_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,   shift_start DATE NOT NULL,   shift_end DATE NOT NULL,   location TEXT NOT NULL,   comments TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_employee_training (   training_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,   training_topic TEXT NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE NOT NULL,   completion_status TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_product_rating (   product_id TEXT NOT NULL,   customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   rating_date DATE NOT NULL,   rating_value TEXT NOT NULL,   comment TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id),   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES s_customer (customer_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_campaign (   campaign_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   campaign_name TEXT NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE NOT NULL,   target_region_id TEXT,   budget TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (target_region_id) REFERENCES s_region (region_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_promotion (   promotion_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   product_id TEXT NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE NOT NULL,   discount_percent TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_payment_method (   method_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   method_name TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE s_invoice (   invoice_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   order_id TEXT NOT NULL,   issue_date DATE NOT NULL,   due_date DATE NOT NULL,   total_amount TEXT NOT NULL,   payment_status TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES s_order (order_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_discount (   discount_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   product_id TEXT NOT NULL,   customer_id TEXT,   discount_amount TEXT NOT NULL,   start_date DATE NOT NULL,   end_date DATE NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id),   FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES s_customer (customer_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_inventory_audit (   audit_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   product_id TEXT NOT NULL,   warehouse_id TEXT NOT NULL,   audit_date DATE NOT NULL,   old_stock TEXT NOT NULL,   new_stock TEXT NOT NULL,   reason TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES s_product (product_id),   FOREIGN KEY (warehouse_id) REFERENCES s_warehouse (warehouse_id) );  CREATE TABLE s_system_log (   log_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   log_date DATE NOT NULL,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   action TEXT NOT NULL,   details TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES s_employee (userid) );  CREATE TABLE s_website_content_update (     update_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,     page_url TEXT NOT NULL,     update_date DATE NOT NULL,     updated_by_employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,     summary TEXT NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (updated_by_employee_id) REFERENCES s_employee (employee_id) );

### Question:
List the names of all customers who have a preferred customer status and are served by a sales representative from the 'Sales Manager' department.

SELECT DISTINCT c.customer_name FROM s_customer c JOIN s_order o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN s_employee e ON o.sales_rep_id = e.employee_id JOIN s_department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id WHERE d.department_name = 'Sales Manager';

 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS customer (   customer_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   customer_name TEXT NOT NULL,   contact_info TEXT );  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS author (   author_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   author_name TEXT NOT NULL ); ### Question:
Determine the percentage of purchases made by students in their final year compared to the total purchases in each department, including the department name and the total number of students in that department who have completed at least one purchase.

SELECT      d.dptName,     COUNT(DISTINCT fys.stdNo) AS total_students_with_purchases,     COUNT(fyp.purchase_id) * 100.0 / COUNT(ap.purchase_id) AS final_year_purchase_percentage FROM      department d LEFT JOIN (     SELECT          s.dptNo, p.purchase_id     FROM          student s     JOIN          member m ON s.stdNo = m.stdNo     JOIN          purchase p ON m.member_id = p.member_id     WHERE          s.stdYear = 'Final Year' ) fyp ON d.dptNo = fyp.dptNo LEFT JOIN (     SELECT          s.dptNo, p.purchase_id     FROM          student s     JOIN          member m ON s.stdNo = m.stdNo     JOIN          purchase p ON m.member_id = p.member_id ) ap ON d.dptNo = ap.dptNo LEFT JOIN (     SELECT         s.dptNo, s.stdNo     FROM         student s     JOIN          member m ON s.stdNo = m.stdNo     JOIN          purchase p ON m.member_id = p.member_id     WHERE         s.stdYear = 'Final Year' ) fys ON d.dptNo = fys.dptNo GROUP BY      d.dptName;

CREATE TABLE item_type_mapping (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   central_item_type SmallInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   material_type_id TEXT NOT NULL,   central_server_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   created_by Varchar NOT NULL,   created_date Timestamp NOT NULL,   last_modified_by Varchar NOT NULL,   last_modified_date Timestamp NOT NULL );

### Question:
For each unique created_by user who has modified at least 3 different entries, list the number of item_type_mappings they created versus the number they last modified, and calculate the difference between these counts.

WITH cte_modified AS (     SELECT last_modified_by AS user, COUNT(*) AS modified_count     FROM item_type_mapping     GROUP BY last_modified_by     HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3 ), cte_created AS (     SELECT created_by AS user, COUNT(*) AS created_count     FROM item_type_mapping     GROUP BY created_by ) SELECT      m.user,     COALESCE(c.created_count, 0) AS created_count,     m.modified_count,     COALESCE(c.created_count, 0) - m.modified_count AS difference FROM cte_modified m LEFT JOIN cte_created c ON m.user = c.user;

CREATE TABLE PERMISSIONS_SRC_TEMP (   ROLE_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   FUNCTION_NAME Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE PERMISSIONS_TEMP (   ROLE_KEY Integer NOT NULL,   FUNCTION_KEY Integer NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE MFR_HIDDEN_GROUPS_T (   ID BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   VERSION Int NOT NULL,   a_surrogateKey BigInt,   GROUP_ID Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (ID) );

### Question:
What is the total number of unique ROLE_NAMEs in PERMISSIONS_SRC_TEMP that have at least two distinct FUNCTION_NAMEs with FUNCTION_KEY values under 100 in PERMISSIONS_TEMP, and are associated with more than one GROUP_ID in MFR_HIDDEN_GROUPS_T where a_surrogateKey is not null, after performing multiple self-joins on MFR_HIDDEN_GROUPS_T and cross-joins with both PERMISSIONS_SRC_TEMP and PERMISSIONS_TEMP?

WITH FunctionCount AS (     SELECT ps.ROLE_NAME     FROM PERMISSIONS_SRC_TEMP ps     JOIN PERMISSIONS_TEMP pt ON ps.FUNCTION_NAME = pt.FUNCTION_KEY     WHERE pt.FUNCTION_KEY < 100     GROUP BY ps.ROLE_NAME     HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ps.FUNCTION_NAME) >= 2 ), GroupCount AS (     SELECT ps.ROLE_NAME     FROM PERMISSIONS_SRC_TEMP ps     JOIN MFR_HIDDEN_GROUPS_T mhg1 ON ps.ROLE_NAME = mhg1.GROUP_ID     JOIN MFR_HIDDEN_GROUPS_T mhg2 ON ps.ROLE_NAME = mhg2.GROUP_ID     WHERE mhg1.a_surrogateKey IS NOT NULL AND mhg2.a_surrogateKey IS NOT NULL AND mhg1.ID <> mhg2.ID     GROUP BY ps.ROLE_NAME     HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT mhg1.GROUP_ID) > 1 ) SELECT COUNT(*) FROM FunctionCount WHERE ROLE_NAME IN (SELECT ROLE_NAME FROM GroupCount);

CREATE TABLE RM_AFFIX (   ID BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   BS_KEYWORD Varchar NOT NULL,   RECORD_ID Varchar NOT NULL,   ORDER_NUMBER Int NOT NULL,   TITLE Varchar NOT NULL,   OLD_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   SAVE_NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   SAVE_SIZE BigInt NOT NULL,   MIME_TYPE Varchar NOT NULL,   ENCODING Varchar NOT NULL,   DESCRIPTION Varchar NOT NULL,   AUTHOR Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (ID) );  CREATE TABLE RM_CODE_DATA (   ID BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   CODE_TYPE_ID BigInt NOT NULL,   DATA_KEY Varchar NOT NULL,   ENABLE_STATUS Char NOT NULL,   DATA_VALUE Varchar NOT NULL,   TOTAL_CODE Varchar NOT NULL,   REMARK Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (ID),   FOREIGN KEY (CODE_TYPE_ID) REFERENCES RM_CODE_TYPE (ID) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE RM_CODE_TYPE (   ID BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   TYPE_KEYWORD Varchar NOT NULL,   NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   REMARK Varchar NOT NULL,   MULTI_VALUE_DESC Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (ID) );  CREATE TABLE RM_NODE_HEARTBEAT (   ID Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   VERSION BigInt NOT NULL,   SHARDING_PREFIX BigInt NOT NULL,   LAST_HEARTBEAT Timestamp NOT NULL,   BASE_URL Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (ID) );

### Question:
For each unique combination of code type and data key, determine the number of affix records that share the same title and author, where the affix records have a specific encoding format, and the associated code data entries are enabled. Furthermore, calculate the average, minimum, and maximum save size across all such affix records, and identify the most recent heartbeat timestamp from the node heartbeat table that corresponds to the shard where these affix records are stored, based on the sharding prefix derived from the affix record's ID.

SELECT     ct.TYPE_KEYWORD AS code_type,     cd.DATA_KEY,     COUNT(a.ID) AS affix_count,     AVG(a.SAVE_SIZE) AS avg_save_size,     MIN(a.SAVE_SIZE) AS min_save_size,     MAX(a.SAVE_SIZE) AS max_save_size,     (SELECT MAX(n.LAST_HEARTBEAT) FROM RM_NODE_HEARTBEAT n WHERE n.SHARDING_PREFIX = a.ID) AS latest_heartbeat FROM RM_AFFIX a JOIN RM_CODE_DATA cd ON a.RECORD_ID = cd.DATA_VALUE JOIN RM_CODE_TYPE ct ON cd.CODE_TYPE_ID = ct.ID WHERE cd.ENABLE_STATUS = 'Y'   AND a.BS_KEYWORD = 'specific_encoding' GROUP BY ct.TYPE_KEYWORD, cd.DATA_KEY;

### Question:
Determine the top 5 products by total sales volume where the product has at least one comment with a rating above 4 stars, is associated with at least two categories, has an active discount, and has available inventory in at least one warehouse. Include product name, total quantity sold, average customer rating, discount percentage, and the highest available stock level across all warehouses.

WITH qualified_products AS (     SELECT p.productID     FROM products p     WHERE (         SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.category_name)         FROM product_categories c         WHERE c.product_id = p.productID     ) >= 2     AND (         SELECT 1         FROM comments cm         WHERE cm.productID = p.productID           AND cm.rating > 4         LIMIT 1     ) IS NOT NULL     AND (         SELECT 1         FROM discounts d         WHERE d.product_id = p.productID           AND d.valid_from <= CURRENT_TIMESTAMP           AND d.valid_to >= CURRENT_TIMESTAMP         LIMIT 1     ) IS NOT NULL     AND (         SELECT 1         FROM inventory_levels i         WHERE i.product_id = p.productID           AND i.stock > 0         LIMIT 1     ) IS NOT NULL ), total_sales AS (     SELECT od.productID, SUM(od.quantity) AS total_quantity     FROM orderdetails od     GROUP BY od.productID ) SELECT      p.name as product_name,     ts.total_quantity as total_quantity_sold,     AVG(c.rating) as average_rating,     d.discount_percent,     MAX(il.stock) as max_stock FROM products p JOIN qualified_products qp ON p.productID = qp.productID JOIN total_sales ts ON ts.productID = p.productID LEFT JOIN comments c ON c.productID = p.productID LEFT JOIN discounts d ON d.product_id = p.productID LEFT JOIN inventory_levels il ON il.product_id = p.productID WHERE d.valid_from <= CURRENT_TIMESTAMP   AND d.valid_to >= CURRENT_TIMESTAMP GROUP BY p.name, ts.total_quantity, d.discount_percent ORDER BY ts.total_quantity DESC LIMIT 5;

### Question:
List all blog posts that have the tag 'technology' and were created by users with the role 'admin'.

SELECT blog.*  FROM blog JOIN blog_tags ON blog.id = blog_tags.blog_id JOIN tags ON blog_tags.tag_id = tags.id JOIN user_favorites_blog ON blog.id = user_favorites_blog.blog_id JOIN users ON user_favorites_blog.user_id = users.id JOIN user_roles ON users.id = user_roles.user_id JOIN roles ON user_roles.role_id = roles.id WHERE tags.name = 'technology' AND roles.name = 'admin';

CREATE TABLE users (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   username Varchar NOT NULL,   full_name Varchar NOT NULL,   password Varchar NOT NULL,   settings Text NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE company (   c_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   c_name Varchar NOT NULL,   c_desc Varchar NOT NULL,   address1 Varchar NOT NULL,   address2 Varchar NOT NULL,   contact Varchar NOT NULL,   visible TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '1',   is_default TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0' );  CREATE TABLE location (   el_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   address Varchar NOT NULL,   city Varchar NOT NULL,   state Varchar NOT NULL,   zip Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE estimator (   e_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   us_key Int NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   e_name Varchar NOT NULL,   e_admin TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   visible TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '1',   FOREIGN KEY (us_key) REFERENCES users (id) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE project (   pp_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   el_key TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   e_key TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   c_key TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   pp_name Varchar NOT NULL,   tax Decimal NOT NULL DEFAULT '0.0000',   pp_status Varchar NOT NULL,   pp_date Date NOT NULL,   pp_owner Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (el_key) REFERENCES location (el_key) ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (e_key) REFERENCES estimator (e_key) ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (c_key) REFERENCES company (c_key) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE proposal (   pr_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   pp_key TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   pr_date Datetime NOT NULL,   pr_name Varchar NOT NULL,   editable TinyInt NOT NULL,   p_pr Decimal NOT NULL,   p_pr_type TEXT NOT NULL,   pr_status Varchar NOT NULL,   authorize Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (pp_key) REFERENCES project (pp_key) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE task (   pst_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   pr_key TEXT NOT NULL,   pst_descr mediumtext NOT NULL,   c_cost Decimal NOT NULL DEFAULT '0.00',   pst_id Varchar NOT NULL,   s_name Varchar NOT NULL,   t_name Varchar NOT NULL,   c_qty Decimal NOT NULL DEFAULT '0.000',   c_unit Varchar NOT NULL,   t_pr Decimal NOT NULL,   t_pr_type TEXT NOT NULL,   t_type Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (pr_key) REFERENCES proposal (pr_key) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE component_materials (   cm_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   pst_key TEXT NOT NULL,   material_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   unit VARCHAR NOT NULL,   quantity Decimal NOT NULL,   unit_cost Decimal NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (pst_key) REFERENCES task (pst_key) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE suppliers (   s_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   description VARCHAR,   contact_info TEXT );  CREATE TABLE supplier_contacts (   sc_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   s_key TEXT NOT NULL,   contact_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   phone VARCHAR,   email VARCHAR,   FOREIGN KEY (s_key) REFERENCES suppliers (s_key) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE invoices (   i_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   pp_key TEXT NOT NULL,   invoice_date DATE NOT NULL,   due_date DATE NOT NULL,   total_amount DECIMAL NOT NULL,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (pp_key) REFERENCES project (pp_key) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE payments (   p_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   i_key TEXT NOT NULL,   payment_date DATE NOT NULL,   amount DECIMAL NOT NULL,   method_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   note TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (i_key) REFERENCES invoices (i_key) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE payment_methods (   pm_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   method_name VARCHAR NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE audit_logs (   log_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   us_key INTEGER NOT NULL,   action_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   action_desc TEXT NOT NULL,   affected_table TEXT NOT NULL,   affected_id TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (us_key) REFERENCES users (id) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE project_categories (   pc_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   pp_key TEXT NOT NULL,   category_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (pp_key) REFERENCES project (pp_key) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE task_notes (   tn_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   pst_key TEXT NOT NULL,   note_text TEXT NOT NULL,   created_by INTEGER NOT NULL,   created_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (pst_key) REFERENCES task (pst_key) ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES users (id) ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE project_budgets (   pb_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   pp_key TEXT NOT NULL,   budget_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   total_budget DECIMAL NOT NULL,   allocated_budget DECIMAL NOT NULL DEFAULT '0.00',   created_date DATE NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (pp_key) REFERENCES project (pp_key) ON UPDATE CASCADE );

### Question:
What is the full name of the user who owns the project with the project name "Solar Panel Installation at Greenfield Park"?
SELECT u.full_name FROM project p JOIN estimator e ON p.e_key = e.e_key JOIN users u ON e.us_key = u.id WHERE p.pp_name = "Solar Panel Installation at Greenfield Park";

### Question:
List the names of employees who are assigned to projects that require a specific equipment with a condition rating of 'poor'.
SELECT p.employee_id FROM project_equipment pe JOIN equipment e ON pe.equipment_id = e.equipment_id JOIN project_employee p ON pe.project_id = p.project_id WHERE e.condition = 'poor';

### Question:
List the courses that have the highest number of prerequisite requirements.

WITH PrereqCounts AS (     SELECT Course_ID, COUNT(Prereq_Course_ID) AS num_prereqs     FROM prerequisite     GROUP BY Course_ID ), MaxCount AS (     SELECT MAX(num_prereqs) AS max_count     FROM PrereqCounts ) SELECT c.Course_ID, c.Course_Title FROM PrereqCounts pc JOIN course c ON pc.Course_ID = c.Course_ID JOIN MaxCount mc ON pc.num_prereqs = mc.max_count;

### Question:
How many guild members have never attended any guild event, based on their event attendance records?

SELECT      COUNT(*) AS never_attended_count FROM      public_members m LEFT JOIN      public_event_attendance e      ON m.character_name = e.character_name AND e.attended = 1 WHERE      e.attended IS NULL;

CREATE TABLE Autor (   id_autor Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nombre Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE Genero (   id_genero Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nombre Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE Editorial (   id_editorial Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nombre Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE OpcionBusqueda (   id_opcion_busqueda Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nombre Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE BusquedaGuardada (   id_busqueda TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   id_usuario Varchar NOT NULL,   nombre Varchar NOT NULL,   parametros Text NOT NULL,   fecha_guardada Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_usuario) REFERENCES Usuario (id_usuario) );  CREATE TABLE Caracteristica (   id_caracteristica Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nombre Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE LibroHasCaracteristica (   id_lhc Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   id_libro TEXT NOT NULL,   id_caracteristica Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_libro) REFERENCES Libro (isbn),   FOREIGN KEY (id_caracteristica) REFERENCES Caracteristica (id_caracteristica) );  CREATE TABLE Pregunta (   id_pregunta TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   id_usuario Varchar NOT NULL,   id_libro TEXT NOT NULL,   texto Text NOT NULL,   fecha Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_usuario) REFERENCES Usuario (id_usuario),   FOREIGN KEY (id_libro) REFERENCES Libro (isbn) );  CREATE TABLE Respuesta (   id_respuesta TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   id_pregunta TEXT NOT NULL,   id_usuario Varchar NOT NULL,   texto Text NOT NULL,   fecha Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_pregunta) REFERENCES Pregunta (id_pregunta),   FOREIGN KEY (id_usuario) REFERENCES Usuario (id_usuario) );  CREATE TABLE Sugerencia (   id_sugerencia TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   id_usuario Varchar NOT NULL,   id_libro TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_usuario) REFERENCES Usuario (id_usuario),   FOREIGN KEY (id_libro) REFERENCES Libro (isbn) );  CREATE TABLE Visita (   id_visita TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   id_usuario Varchar NOT NULL,   id_libro TEXT NOT NULL,   fecha Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_usuario) REFERENCES Usuario (id_usuario),   FOREIGN KEY (id_libro) REFERENCES Libro (isbn) );  CREATE TABLE Coleccion (   id_coleccion INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nombre TEXT NOT NULL,   descripcion TEXT,   id_usuario TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_usuario) REFERENCES Usuario(id_usuario) );  CREATE TABLE LibroHasColeccion (   id_lhc INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   id_libro TEXT NOT NULL,   id_coleccion INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_libro) REFERENCES Libro(isbn),   FOREIGN KEY (id_coleccion) REFERENCES Coleccion(id_coleccion) );  CREATE TABLE Formato (   id_formato INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nombre TEXT NOT NULL );

### Question:
Show the number of comments made on books by genre, including the name of the genre and the count of comments.

SELECT g.nombre AS genre,         COUNT(c.id_comentario) AS comment_count FROM Comentario c JOIN LibroHasGenero lhg ON c.id_libro = lhg.id_libro JOIN Genero g ON lhg.id_genero = g.id_genero GROUP BY g.nombre;

### Question:

### Question:
What is the average productivity of employees who have at least one approved leave and are associated with devices in maintenance?

SELECT AVG(rp.produtividade) FROM relatorio_produtividade rp WHERE rp.id_funcionario IN (     SELECT f.id_funcionario     FROM ferias_disponiveis f     WHERE f.dias_disponiveis > 0       AND EXISTS (           SELECT 1           FROM dispositivos d           WHERE d.id_funcionario = f.id_funcionario             AND d.status_dispositivo = 'manutencao'       ) );

### Question:
Show the names of labels that have at least one release with a status of "Official" and are linked to a person via a credit role of "Executive Producer". Additionally, include the number of such releases per label and the average number of genres associated with these releases.

WITH executive_releases AS (     SELECT rc.release_id     FROM release_credits rc     WHERE rc.role = 'Executive Producer' ), official_executive_releases AS (     SELECT er.release_id     FROM executive_releases er     JOIN release_status rs ON er.release_id = rs.release_id     WHERE rs.status = 'Official' ) SELECT      l.name AS label_name,     COUNT(DISTINCT oer.release_id) AS release_count,     AVG(genre_count) AS avg_genre_count FROM official_executive_releases oer JOIN release_label rl ON oer.release_id = rl.release_id JOIN label l ON rl.label_id = l.id JOIN (     SELECT release_id, COUNT(*) AS genre_count     FROM release_genre     GROUP BY release_id ) genre_counts ON oer.release_id = genre_counts.release_id GROUP BY l.name;

### Question:
For each client with at least one required action, how many active protocol sessions are currently open?

SELECT CLIENT_ID, active_protocol_sessions FROM (     SELECT car.CLIENT_ID,            COUNT(pps.ID) OVER (PARTITION BY car.CLIENT_ID) AS active_protocol_sessions     FROM public_CLIENT_REQUIRED_ACTIONS car     LEFT JOIN public_CLIENT_PROTOCOL_SESSIONS pps          ON car.CLIENT_ID = pps.CLIENT_ID         AND pps.EXPIRES_AT > strftime('%s', 'now') ) GROUP BY CLIENT_ID;

CREATE TABLE Authority (   Id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   Name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE User (   Id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   Username Varchar NOT NULL,   Password Varchar NOT NULL,   FirstName Varchar NOT NULL,   LastName Varchar NOT NULL,   Email Varchar NOT NULL,   Enabled TEXT NOT NULL,   LastPasswordResetDate Datetime NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE UserAuthority (   UserId Int NOT NULL,   AuthorityId Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (UserId) REFERENCES User (Id),   FOREIGN KEY (AuthorityId) REFERENCES Authority (Id) );  CREATE TABLE AuditLog (   Id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   UserId INT NOT NULL,   EventType TEXT NOT NULL,   EventTime DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   Details TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (UserId) REFERENCES User (Id) );  CREATE TABLE UserSession (   SessionId TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   UserId INT NOT NULL,   StartTime DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   EndTime DATETIME,   IsActive BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1,   FOREIGN KEY (UserId) REFERENCES User (Id) );  CREATE TABLE PasswordHistory (   Id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   UserId INT NOT NULL,   PasswordHash TEXT NOT NULL,   ChangedAt DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (UserId) REFERENCES User (Id) );  CREATE TABLE Token (   Id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   UserId INT NOT NULL,   TokenValue TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   TokenType TEXT NOT NULL,   ExpiryTime DATETIME NOT NULL,   Used BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0,   FOREIGN KEY (UserId) REFERENCES User (Id) );  CREATE TABLE UserSetting (   UserId INT NOT NULL,   SettingKey TEXT NOT NULL,   SettingValue TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (UserId, SettingKey),   FOREIGN KEY (UserId) REFERENCES User (Id) );  CREATE INDEX AuditLog_UserId_Index ON AuditLog (UserId);  CREATE INDEX PasswordHistory_UserId_Index ON PasswordHistory (UserId);  CREATE INDEX Token_TokenValue_Index ON Token (TokenValue);  CREATE INDEX Token_ExpiryTime_Index ON Token (ExpiryTime);

### Question:
Retrieve the details of the most recent audit log entries for each user who has a specific user setting enabled, along with their associated authorities and current session status.

WITH LatestAudit AS (     SELECT          al.UserId,          al.EventTime,          al.EventType,          al.Details,         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY al.UserId ORDER BY al.EventTime DESC) as rnk     FROM AuditLog al ) SELECT      u.Id AS UserId,      u.Username,      u.Enabled,      u.LastPasswordResetDate,      la.EventTime,      la.EventType,      la.Details,     GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT a.Name) AS Authorities,     COALESCE(         CASE WHEN us.IsActive = 1 THEN 'Active' END,          'Inactive'     ) AS SessionStatus FROM User u JOIN UserSetting uset ON u.Id = uset.UserId JOIN LatestAudit la ON la.UserId = u.Id LEFT JOIN UserAuthority ua ON u.Id = ua.UserId LEFT JOIN Authority a ON a.Id = ua.AuthorityId LEFT JOIN UserSession us ON u.Id = us.UserId AND us.IsActive = 1 WHERE      uset.SettingKey = 'specific_setting_key'      AND uset.SettingValue = 'enabled_value'     AND la.rnk = 1 GROUP BY      u.Id, u.Username, la.EventTime, la.EventType, la.Details, us.IsActive ORDER BY la.EventTime DESC;

### Question:
For each 'like' in t6, count how many times it appears in both t6 and t7.

SELECT t6.like,         (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t6 AS t6_sub WHERE t6_sub.like = t6.like) AS count_t6,        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t7 AS t7_sub WHERE t7_sub.like = t6.like) AS count_t7 FROM t6 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t7 WHERE t7.like = t6.like) GROUP BY t6.like;

### Question:
Provide the count of distinct training programs attended by employees, grouped by their department and role title, for employees who are assigned to at least one project and have a work schedule ending before 5 PM.

WITH filtered_employees AS (     SELECT e.id     FROM employee e     JOIN project_assignment pa ON e.id = pa.employee_id     JOIN work_schedule ws ON e.id = ws.employee_id     WHERE pa.id IS NOT NULL       AND ws.end_time < '17:00:00'     GROUP BY e.id     HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pa.id) > 0       AND COUNT(DISTINCT ws.id) > 0 ) SELECT d.name AS department, r.title AS role_title, COUNT(DISTINCT tp.id) AS distinct_trainings_attended FROM employee e JOIN roles r ON e.roles_id = r.id JOIN department d ON r.department_id = d.id JOIN employee_training et ON e.id = et.employee_id JOIN training_program tp ON et.training_id = tp.id WHERE e.id IN (SELECT id FROM filtered_employees) GROUP BY d.name, r.title;

### Question:
Identify users who have used more than 3 different IP addresses in the last 7 days, have at least one invitation token issued by another user, have a role with "editor" in the name, and whose most recent password change was more than 90 days ago.

SELECT u.username FROM users u WHERE EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM user_sessions s     WHERE s.username = u.username     AND s.login_time >= DATE('now', '-7 days')     GROUP BY s.username     HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT s.ip_address) > 3 ) AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM user_invitation_tokens it     WHERE it.invited_email = u.username     AND it.issuer_username != u.username ) AND EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM user_roles r     WHERE r.username = u.username     AND r.role LIKE '%editor%' ) AND (     SELECT MAX(changed_date)     FROM user_password_history ph     WHERE ph.username = u.username ) < DATE('now', '-90 days');

### Question:
Show the count of tickets sold per ticket type for movies that have a domestic gross higher than $100 million and are shown in 3D format.

SELECT tt.Type, COUNT(t.TicketId) AS TicketsSold FROM Movie_Ticket t JOIN Movie_Showtimes s ON t.ShowtimeId = s.ShowtimeId JOIN Movie_BoxOffice b ON s.MovieId = b.MovieId JOIN Movie_MovieFormats mf ON s.MovieId = mf.MovieId JOIN Movie_Format f ON mf.FormatId = f.FormatId JOIN Movie_TicketType tt ON t.TicketTypeId = tt.TicketTypeId WHERE b.DomesticGross > 100000000.00   AND f.Type = '3D' GROUP BY tt.Type;

CREATE TABLE Officers (   officer_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   started_monitoring_time Datetime NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Detainees (   member_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   roles MEDIUMTEXT NOT NULL,   date Datetime NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE LeaveTimes (   officer_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   date_start Datetime NOT NULL,   date_end Datetime NOT NULL,   reason Text NOT NULL,   request_id TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (officer_id) REFERENCES Officers (officer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE TimeLog (   entry_number Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   officer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   start_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   end_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (officer_id) REFERENCES Officers (officer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE MessageActivityLog (   entry_number Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   officer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   channel_id TEXT NOT NULL,   message_id TEXT NOT NULL,   send_time Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (officer_id) REFERENCES Officers (officer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE VRChatNames (   officer_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   vrc_name Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (officer_id) REFERENCES Officers (officer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE UserStrikes (   member_id TEXT NOT NULL,   reason Text NOT NULL,   date Datetime NOT NULL,   entry_number Int NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE RenewalTimes (   renewal_id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   officer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   renewed_time Datetime NOT NULL,   renewed_by TEXT NOT NULL,   reason Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (officer_id) REFERENCES Officers (officer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (renewed_by) REFERENCES Officers (officer_id) ON DELETE SET NULL );  CREATE TABLE OfficerRoles (   officer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   role_name TEXT NOT NULL,   assigned_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   unassigned_date DATETIME,   PRIMARY KEY (officer_id, role_name),   FOREIGN KEY (officer_id) REFERENCES Officers(officer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE EquipmentAssignments (   assignment_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   officer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   equipment_type TEXT NOT NULL,   serial_number TEXT NOT NULL,   assigned_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   returned_date DATETIME,   FOREIGN KEY (officer_id) REFERENCES Officers(officer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE TrainingRecords (   training_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   officer_id TEXT NOT NULL,   training_type TEXT NOT NULL,   completion_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (officer_id) REFERENCES Officers(officer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );

### Question:
How many officers have an active equipment assignment and have sent messages in the "Operations" channel but have not been assigned the "Security Lead" role?

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT e.officer_id) FROM EquipmentAssignments e INNER JOIN MessageActivityLog m ON e.officer_id = m.officer_id WHERE e.returned_date IS NULL   AND m.channel_id = 'Operations'   AND e.officer_id NOT IN (       SELECT officer_id FROM OfficerRoles WHERE role_name = 'Security Lead'   );

### Question:
What is the total difference between planned and actual amounts for each fund in the most recent month available?
SELECT fund, SUM(planned - actual) AS difference FROM budget_funds WHERE month = (SELECT MAX(month) FROM budget_funds) GROUP BY fund;

### Schema:
CREATE TABLE laporan (   id_laporan Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   username Varchar NOT NULL,   judul Varchar NOT NULL,   isi Text NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE pemasukan (   id_pemasukan Int NOT NULL,   tgl_pemasukan Date NOT NULL,   jumlah Int NOT NULL,   keterangan Text NOT NULL,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user (id_user) );  CREATE TABLE pengeluaran (   id_pengeluaran Int NOT NULL,   tgl_pengeluaran Date NOT NULL,   jumlah Int NOT NULL,   keterangan Text NOT NULL,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user (id_user) );  CREATE TABLE user (   id_user Int NOT NULL,   nama Varchar NOT NULL,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   noHP Varchar NOT NULL,   username Varchar NOT NULL,   pass Varchar NOT NULL,   type TEXT NOT NULL,   status Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE kategori_pengeluaran (   id_kategori_pengeluaran Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   nama_kategori Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE pengeluaran_kategori (   id_pengeluaran Int NOT NULL,   id_kategori_pengeluaran Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_pengeluaran) REFERENCES pengeluaran(id_pengeluaran),   FOREIGN KEY (id_kategori_pengeluaran) REFERENCES kategori_pengeluaran(id_kategori_pengeluaran) );  CREATE TABLE kategori_pemasukan (   id_kategori_pemasukan Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   nama_kategori Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE pemasukan_kategori (   id_pemasukan Int NOT NULL,   id_kategori_pemasukan Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_pemasukan) REFERENCES pemasukan(id_pemasukan),   FOREIGN KEY (id_kategori_pemasukan) REFERENCES kategori_pemasukan(id_kategori_pemasukan) );  CREATE TABLE anggaran (   id_anggaran Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   jumlah Int NOT NULL,   keterangan Text,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user(id_user) );  CREATE TABLE notifikasi (   id_notifikasi Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   pesan Text NOT NULL,   tgl_notifikasi Date NOT NULL,   status Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user(id_user) );  CREATE TABLE riwayat_login (   id_riwayat Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   tgl_login Datetime NOT NULL,   ip_address Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user(id_user) );  CREATE TABLE feedback_pengguna (   id_feedback Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   isi_feedback Text NOT NULL,   tanggal Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user(id_user) );  CREATE TABLE metode_pembayaran (   id_metode Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   nama_metode Varchar NOT NULL,   detail Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user(id_user) );  CREATE TABLE rekening_bank (   id_rekening Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   nama_bank Varchar NOT NULL,   nomor_rekening Varchar NOT NULL,   pemilik_rekening Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user(id_user) );  CREATE TABLE tujuan_keuangan (   id_tujuan Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   nama_tujuan Varchar NOT NULL,   target_jumlah Int NOT NULL,   tanggal_target Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user(id_user) );  CREATE TABLE laporan_keuangan (   id_laporan_keuangan Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   bulan Varchar NOT NULL,   tahun Int NOT NULL,   total_pemasukan Int NOT NULL,   total_pengeluaran Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user(id_user) );  CREATE TABLE rekomendasi_anggaran (   id_rekomendasi Int NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   id_user Int NOT NULL,   detail Text NOT NULL,   tanggal_rekomendasi Date NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES user(id_user) );  CREATE TABLE recurring_transaksi (   id_recurring INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   id_pemasukan INTEGER,   id_pengeluaran INTEGER,   type_transaksi TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (type_transaksi IN ('pemasukan', 'pengeluaran')),   frekuensi TEXT NOT NULL,   next_date DATE NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_pemasukan) REFERENCES pemasukan (id_pemasukan),   FOREIGN KEY (id_pengeluaran) REFERENCES pengeluaran (id_pengeluaran) );

### Question:
Show a list of users who have set a financial goal and have at least one recurring transaction that is either income or expense. Include the goal name, the recurring transaction frequency, the associated bank account number, and the type of transaction.

SELECT t.nama_tujuan, r.frekuensi, b.nomor_rekening, r.type_transaksi FROM tujuan_keuangan t INNER JOIN (     SELECT r.type_transaksi, r.frekuensi, COALESCE(p.id_user, pe.id_user) AS id_user     FROM recurring_transaksi r     LEFT JOIN pemasukan p ON r.id_pemasukan = p.id_pemasukan     LEFT JOIN pengeluaran pe ON r.id_pengeluaran = pe.id_pengeluaran     WHERE COALESCE(p.id_user, pe.id_user) IS NOT NULL ) AS r ON t.id_user = r.id_user INNER JOIN rekening_bank b ON t.id_user = b.id_user;

### Schema:
CREATE TABLE tipo_user (   id_tipo Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   tipo_user Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE usuario (   id_user Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nome Varchar NOT NULL,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   telefone Varchar NOT NULL,   cpf Varchar NOT NULL,   senha Text NOT NULL,   ativo Integer NOT NULL,   id_tipo Integer NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_tipo) REFERENCES tipo_user (id_tipo) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE fornecedor (   id_forn Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nome Varchar NOT NULL,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   cnpj Varchar NOT NULL,   telefone Varchar NOT NULL,   ativo Integer NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE endereco (   id_end Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   estado Varchar NOT NULL,   cidade Varchar NOT NULL,   rua Varchar NOT NULL,   numero Integer NOT NULL,   cep Varchar NOT NULL,   id_forn Integer NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (id_forn) REFERENCES fornecedor (id_forn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE categoria_produto (id_categoria INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, nome_categoria VARCHAR NOT NULL);  CREATE TABLE produto (id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, nome VARCHAR NOT NULL, descricao TEXT, preco REAL NOT NULL, id_categoria INTEGER NOT NULL, id_forn INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_categoria) REFERENCES categoria_produto (id_categoria), FOREIGN KEY (id_forn) REFERENCES fornecedor (id_forn));  CREATE TABLE estoque (id_estoque INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, quantidade INTEGER NOT NULL, id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL, id_forn INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_produto) REFERENCES produto (id_produto), FOREIGN KEY (id_forn) REFERENCES fornecedor (id_forn));  CREATE TABLE compra (id_compra INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, data_compra DATE NOT NULL, valor_total REAL NOT NULL, id_forn INTEGER NOT NULL, id_user INTEGER NOT NULL, concluida INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, FOREIGN KEY (id_forn) REFERENCES fornecedor (id_forn), FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user));  CREATE TABLE item_compra (id_item INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, quantidade INTEGER NOT NULL, preco_total REAL NOT NULL, id_compra INTEGER NOT NULL, id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_compra) REFERENCES compra (id_compra), FOREIGN KEY (id_produto) REFERENCES produto (id_produto));  CREATE TABLE cliente (id_cliente INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, nome VARCHAR NOT NULL, email VARCHAR NOT NULL, telefone VARCHAR NOT NULL, cpf VARCHAR NOT NULL, endereco_rua VARCHAR NOT NULL, endereco_numero INTEGER NOT NULL, endereco_cep VARCHAR NOT NULL, ativo INTEGER NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (id_cliente));  CREATE TABLE endereco_cliente (id_end_cliente INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, estado VARCHAR NOT NULL, cidade VARCHAR NOT NULL, rua VARCHAR NOT NULL, numero INTEGER NOT NULL, cep VARCHAR NOT NULL, id_cliente INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_cliente) REFERENCES cliente (id_cliente));  CREATE TABLE relatorio_atividade (id_relatorio INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, data_registro TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, descricao TEXT NOT NULL, id_user INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user));  CREATE TABLE historico_usuario (id_registro INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, data_alteracao TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, detalhes TEXT NOT NULL, id_user INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user));  CREATE TABLE favoritos_usuario (id_favorito INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, id_user INTEGER NOT NULL, id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user), FOREIGN KEY (id_produto) REFERENCES produto (id_produto));  CREATE TABLE empresa_usuario (id_empresa INTEGER NOT NULL, nome_empresa VARCHAR NOT NULL, cnpj VARCHAR NOT NULL, telefone_empresa VARCHAR NOT NULL, id_user INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user));  CREATE TABLE permissao_usuario (id_permisao INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, descricao VARCHAR NOT NULL, id_user INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user));  CREATE TABLE telefone_usuario (id_tel_usuario INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, numero VARCHAR NOT NULL, principal INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, id_user INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user));  CREATE TABLE telefone_fornecedor (id_tel_forn INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, numero VARCHAR NOT NULL, principal INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, id_forn INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_forn) REFERENCES fornecedor (id_forn));  CREATE TABLE telefone_cliente (id_tel_cliente INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, numero VARCHAR NOT NULL, principal INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, id_cliente INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_cliente) REFERENCES cliente (id_cliente));  CREATE TABLE log_acesso (id_log INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, data_acesso TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, status_acesso INTEGER NOT NULL, ip_usuario VARCHAR NOT NULL, id_user INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user));  CREATE TABLE avaliacao_produto (id_avaliacao INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, nota INTEGER NOT NULL, comentario TEXT, data_avaliacao TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, id_user INTEGER NOT NULL, id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user), FOREIGN KEY (id_produto) REFERENCES produto (id_produto));  CREATE TABLE promocao_produto (id_promocao INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, data_inicio TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, data_fim TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, desconto_percentual REAL NOT NULL, id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_produto) REFERENCES produto (id_produto));  CREATE TABLE tag_produto (id_tag INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, nome_tag VARCHAR NOT NULL, id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_produto) REFERENCES produto (id_produto));  CREATE TABLE imagem_produto (id_imagem INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, url VARCHAR NOT NULL, legenda TEXT, principal INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_produto) REFERENCES produto (id_produto));  CREATE TABLE pagamento_compra (id_pagamento INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, forma_pagamento VARCHAR NOT NULL, valor_pago REAL NOT NULL, data_pagamento DATE NOT NULL, id_compra INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_compra) REFERENCES compra (id_compra));  CREATE TABLE nota_fiscal_compra (id_nota INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, numero_nota VARCHAR NOT NULL, data_emissao DATE NOT NULL, serie VARCHAR NOT NULL, id_compra INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (id_compra) REFERENCES compra (id_compra));  CREATE INDEX idx_produto_id_forn ON produto (id_forn);  CREATE INDEX idx_produto_id_categoria ON produto (id_categoria);  CREATE TABLE detalhe_pedidos (     id_pedido INTEGER NOT NULL,     id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL,     quantidade INTEGER NOT NULL,     preco_unitario REAL NOT NULL,     PRIMARY KEY (id_pedido, id_produto),     FOREIGN KEY (id_pedido) REFERENCES pedidos (id_pedido),     FOREIGN KEY (id_produto) REFERENCES produto (id_produto) );  CREATE TABLE pedidos (     id_pedido INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     data_pedido TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,  
valor_total REAL NOT NULL,     status_pedido TEXT NOT NULL,     id_cliente INTEGER NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (id_cliente) REFERENCES cliente (id_cliente) );  CREATE TABLE historico_precos (     id_historico INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL,     data_alteracao TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,     valor_anterior REAL NOT NULL,     valor_novo REAL NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (id_produto) REFERENCES produto (id_produto) );  CREATE TABLE relatorio_vendas (     id_relatorio VARCHAR NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,  
data_registro TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,     periodo_inicio TEXT NOT NULL,     periodo_fim TEXT NOT NULL,     total_vendas REAL NOT NULL,     id_user INTEGER NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user) );  CREATE TABLE devolucao_pedido (     id_devolucao INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     data_devolucao TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,     status_devolucao TEXT NOT NULL,     quantidade_devolvida INTEGER NOT NULL,     motivo TEXT NOT NULL,     id_pedido INTEGER NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (id_pedido) REFERENCES pedidos (id_pedido) );  CREATE TABLE fluxo_caixa (     id_fluxo INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     data_registro TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,     tipo_movimentacao TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (tipo_movimentacao IN ('Entrada', 'Saida')),     descricao TEXT NOT NULL,     valor REAL NOT NULL,     id_user INTEGER NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user) );  CREATE TABLE itens_carrinho (     id_carrinho INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     id_user INTEGER NOT NULL,     id_produto INTEGER NOT NULL,     quantidade INTEGER NOT NULL,     data_adicionado TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,     FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user),     FOREIGN KEY (id_produto) REFERENCES produto (id_produto) );  CREATE TABLE desconto_cliente (     id_desc_cliente INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     data_inicio TEXT NOT NULL,     data_fim TEXT NOT NULL,     desconto_percentual REAL NOT NULL CHECK (desconto_percentual >= 0 AND desconto_percentual <= 100),     ativo INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,     id_cliente INTEGER NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (id_cliente) REFERENCES cliente (id_cliente) );  CREATE TABLE campanha_marketing (     id_campanha INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     nome_campanha TEXT NOT NULL,     descricao TEXT NOT NULL,     data_inicio TEXT NOT NULL,     data_fim TEXT NOT NULL,     id_user INTEGER NOT NULL,     FOREIGN KEY (id_user) REFERENCES usuario (id_user) );  CREATE TABLE suporte_cliente (     id_ticket INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     assunto TEXT NOT NULL,     descricao TEXT NOT NULL,     data_abertura TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,     status TEXT NOT NULL,     id_cliente INTEGER NOT NULL,     id_user_atendente INTEGER,     FOREIGN KEY (id_cliente) REFERENCES cliente (id_cliente),     FOREIGN KEY (id_user_atendente) REFERENCES usuario (id_user) );  CREATE INDEX idx_historico_precos_id_produto ON historico_precos (id_produto);  CREATE INDEX idx_relatorio_vendas_id_user ON relatorio_vendas (id_user);  CREATE INDEX idx_devolucao_pedido_id_pedido ON devolucao_pedido (id_pedido);  CREATE INDEX idx_fluxo_caixa_id_user ON fluxo_caixa (id_user);  CREATE INDEX idx_itens_carrinho_id_user ON itens_carrinho (id_user);  CREATE INDEX idx_itens_carrinho_id_produto ON itens_carrinho (id_produto);  CREATE INDEX idx_desconto_cliente_id_cliente ON desconto_cliente (id_cliente);  CREATE INDEX idx_campanha_marketing_id_user ON campanha_marketing (id_user);  CREATE INDEX idx_suporte_cliente_id_cliente ON suporte_cliente (id_cliente);  CREATE INDEX idx_suporte_cliente_id_user_atendente ON suporte_cliente (id_user_atendente);

### Question:
List the suppliers who have delivered at least three purchases to customers in the city of São Paulo.

SELECT f.nome  FROM fornecedor f JOIN compra c ON f.id_forn = c.id_forn JOIN cliente cl ON c.id_user = cl.id_cliente JOIN endereco_cliente ec ON cl.id_cliente = ec.id_cliente WHERE ec.cidade = 'São Paulo' GROUP BY f.id_forn HAVING COUNT(c.id_compra) >= 3;

CREATE TABLE oauth_access_tokens (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   account_id TEXT NOT NULL,   oauth_client_id TEXT NOT NULL,   access_token Varchar NOT NULL,   refresh_token Varchar NOT NULL,   createdAt Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE email_queue (   id TEXT NOT NULL,   template_id TEXT NOT NULL,   delivery_service_id TEXT NOT NULL,   recipient_email TEXT NOT NULL,   subject TEXT NOT NULL,   body TEXT NOT NULL,   scheduled_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   attempts INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (template_id) REFERENCES email_templates(id),   FOREIGN KEY (delivery_service_id) REFERENCES email_delivery_services(id) );  CREATE TABLE email_logs (   id TEXT NOT NULL,   queue_id TEXT NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   response_code INT,   response_message TEXT,   delivered_at DATETIME,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (queue_id) REFERENCES email_queue(id) );  CREATE TABLE email_attachments (   attachment_id TEXT NOT NULL,   template_id TEXT,   queue_id TEXT,   filename TEXT NOT NULL,   file_type TEXT NOT NULL,   file_data BLOB NOT NULL,   created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (attachment_id),   FOREIGN KEY (template_id) REFERENCES email_templates(id),   FOREIGN KEY (queue_id) REFERENCES email_queue(id) );  CREATE TABLE attachment_file_types (   file_type_id TEXT NOT NULL,   mime_type TEXT NOT NULL,   is_allowed BOOLEAN NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (file_type_id) );  CREATE TABLE email_delivery_config (   service_id TEXT NOT NULL,   max_daily_emails INT,   max_recipients_per_send INT,   retry_interval_minutes INT,   last_config_update DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (service_id),   FOREIGN KEY (service_id) REFERENCES email_delivery_services(id) );  CREATE TABLE template_versions (   id TEXT NOT NULL,   template_id TEXT NOT NULL,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   subject TEXT NOT NULL,   body TEXT NOT NULL,   created_by TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (template_id) REFERENCES email_templates(id) );  CREATE TABLE user_email_preferences (   account_id TEXT NOT NULL,   notification_type TEXT NOT NULL,   is_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (account_id, notification_type),   FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES account_password(account_id) );  CREATE TABLE password_reset_tokens (   token TEXT NOT NULL,   account_id TEXT NOT NULL,   created_by TEXT NOT NULL,   expires_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   used BOOLEAN NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (token),   FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES account_password(account_id) );  CREATE TABLE email_headers (   header_id TEXT NOT NULL,   queue_id TEXT NOT NULL,   header_name TEXT NOT NULL,   header_value TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (header_id),   FOREIGN KEY (queue_id) REFERENCES email_queue(id) );  CREATE TABLE email_forwarding_rules (   rule_id TEXT NOT NULL,   account_id TEXT NOT NULL,   source_email TEXT NOT NULL,   destination_email TEXT NOT NULL,   active BOOLEAN NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (rule_id),   FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES account_password(account_id) );  CREATE TABLE campaign_analytic_segments (   segment_id TEXT NOT NULL,   campaign_id TEXT NOT NULL,   segment_name TEXT NOT NULL,   criteria TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (segment_id),   FOREIGN KEY (campaign_id) REFERENCES email_campaigns(id) );  CREATE TABLE read_receipt_tracks (   receipt_id TEXT NOT NULL,   queue_id TEXT NOT NULL,   received_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   device TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY (receipt_id),   FOREIGN KEY (queue_id) REFERENCES email_queue(id) );  CREATE TABLE failed_retry_attempts (   retry_id TEXT NOT NULL,   log_id TEXT NOT NULL,   retry_time DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (retry_id),   FOREIGN KEY (log_id) REFERENCES email_logs(id) );  CREATE TABLE template_snippets (   snippet_id TEXT NOT NULL,   template_id TEXT NOT NULL,   snippet_name TEXT NOT NULL,   content TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (snippet_id),   FOREIGN KEY (template_id) REFERENCES email_templates(id) );  CREATE TABLE timezone_schedule_opts (   tz_id TEXT NOT NULL,   schedule_id TEXT NOT NULL,   timezone TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (tz_id),   FOREIGN KEY (schedule_id) REFERENCES email_schedule_times(id) );  CREATE TABLE email_whitelist (   whitelist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   email TEXT NOT NULL,   service_id TEXT,   expires_at DATETIME,   PRIMARY KEY (whitelist_id),   FOREIGN KEY (service_id) REFERENCES email_delivery_services(id) );  CREATE TABLE content_approval_workflows (   workflow_id TEXT NOT NULL,   template_id TEXT NOT NULL,   step_number INT NOT NULL,   approver_id TEXT NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (workflow_id),   FOREIGN KEY (template_id) REFERENCES email_templates(id) );  CREATE TABLE quota_usage_stats (   usage_id TEXT NOT NULL,   service_id TEXT NOT NULL,   daily_limit INT NOT NULL,   daily_used INT NOT NULL,   monthly_used INT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (usage_id),   FOREIGN KEY (service_id) REFERENCES email_delivery_services(id) );

### Question:
List the top 5 email templates by the sum of campaign metric sent counts, where the template has at least one approved workflow step, has associated attachments with allowed file types, and is linked to a campaign with a remaining budget exceeding 50% of the limit.

SELECT      ct.template_id,     SUM(cm.sent_count) AS total_sent FROM      campaign_metrics cm JOIN email_campaigns ec ON cm.campaign_id = ec.id JOIN campaign_template_link ct ON ec.id = ct.campaign_id WHERE      EXISTS (         SELECT 1         FROM content_approval_workflows caw         WHERE caw.template_id = ct.template_id             AND caw.status = 'approved'     )     AND EXISTS (         SELECT 1         FROM email_attachments ea         JOIN attachment_file_types afts ON ea.file_type = afts.mime_type         WHERE ea.template_id = ct.template_id             AND afts.is_allowed = 1     )     AND EXISTS (         SELECT 1         FROM campaign_budgets cb         WHERE cb.campaign_id = ec.id             AND (cb.budget_limit - cb.used_budget) > 0.5 * cb.budget_limit     ) GROUP BY ct.template_id ORDER BY total_sent DESC LIMIT 5;

### Question:
What is the average amount of transactions created between 1:00 PM and 5:00 PM?

CREATE TABLE CLIENTE (   idCliente Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nomeCliente Varchar NOT NULL,   sexo TEXT NOT NULL,   cpf Varchar NOT NULL,   email Varchar NOT NULL,   celular Varchar NOT NULL,   statusCliente TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (idCliente) );  CREATE TABLE FUNCIONARIOS (   idFuncionario Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nomeFuncionario Varchar NOT NULL,   cargo Varchar NOT NULL,   sexo TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (idFuncionario) );  CREATE TABLE LOGIN (   idUsuarioLogin Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   idFuncionario Int NOT NULL,   nomeUsuario Varchar NOT NULL,   senha Varchar NOT NULL,   permissao TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (idUsuarioLogin),   FOREIGN KEY (idFuncionario) REFERENCES FUNCIONARIOS (idFuncionario) );  CREATE TABLE AUTORES (   idAutor Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nomeAutor Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (idAutor) );  CREATE TABLE CATEGORIA (   idCategoria Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nomeCategoria Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (idCategoria) );  CREATE TABLE EDITORA (   idEditora Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nomeEditora Varchar NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (idEditora) );  CREATE TABLE LIVROS (   idLivro Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   idCategoria Int NOT NULL,   idAutor Int NOT NULL,   idEditora Int NOT NULL,   nomeLivro Varchar NOT NULL,   isbn Varchar NOT NULL,   qtd Int NOT NULL,   precoUnidade Float NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (idLivro),   FOREIGN KEY (idCategoria) REFERENCES CATEGORIA (idCategoria),   FOREIGN KEY (idAutor) REFERENCES AUTORES (idAutor),   FOREIGN KEY (idEditora) REFERENCES EDITORA (idEditora) );  CREATE TABLE RESERVA (   idReserva Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   idCliente Int NOT NULL,   idFuncionario Int NOT NULL,   idLivro Int NOT NULL,   idEmprestimo Int NOT NULL,   dataReserva Datetime NOT NULL,   dataRetiradaPrevista Datetime NOT NULL,   dataRetirada Datetime NOT NULL,   statusReserva TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (idReserva),   FOREIGN KEY (idLivro) REFERENCES LIVROS (idLivro),   FOREIGN KEY (idCliente) REFERENCES CLIENTE (idCliente),   FOREIGN KEY (idFuncionario) REFERENCES FUNCIONARIOS (idFuncionario) );  CREATE TABLE TIPOS_MULTA (   idTipoMulta Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   nomeTipo Varchar NOT NULL,   valor Float NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (idTipoMulta) );  CREATE TABLE MULTA (   idMulta Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   idTipoMulta Int NOT NULL,   idEmprestimo Int NOT NULL,   idFuncionario Int NOT NULL,   observacoes Varchar NOT NULL,   statusMulta TEXT NOT NULL,   totalMulta Float NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (idMulta),   FOREIGN KEY (idTipoMulta) REFERENCES TIPOS_MULTA (idTipoMulta),   FOREIGN KEY (idFuncionario) REFERENCES FUNCIONARIOS (idFuncionario) );

### Question:
What are the average fines imposed for each type of fine, considering only those fines associated with reservations that were completed before their expected return date and processed by female employees?

SELECT TIPOS_MULTA.nomeTipo, AVG(MULTA.totalMulta) AS average_fine FROM MULTA JOIN RESERVA ON MULTA.idEmprestimo = RESERVA.idEmprestimo JOIN FUNCIONARIOS ON RESERVA.idFuncionario = FUNCIONARIOS.idFuncionario JOIN TIPOS_MULTA ON MULTA.idTipoMulta = TIPOS_MULTA.idTipoMulta WHERE RESERVA.dataRetirada < RESERVA.dataRetiradaPrevista   AND FUNCIONARIOS.sexo = 'F' GROUP BY TIPOS_MULTA.nomeTipo;

 CREATE TABLE SongGenre (   Song INTEGER NOT NULL,   Genre INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Song) REFERENCES Song (Id),   FOREIGN KEY (Genre) REFERENCES Genre (Id) );  CREATE TABLE TrackDetails (   Song INTEGER NOT NULL,   TrackNumber INTEGER NOT NULL,   Duration TIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Song) REFERENCES Song (Id) );  CREATE TABLE ReleaseCountry (   Album INTEGER NOT NULL,   Country TEXT NOT NULL,   ReleaseDate DATE NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Album) REFERENCES Album (Id) );  CREATE TABLE SongWriter (   Song INTEGER NOT NULL,   Artist INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Song) REFERENCES Song (Id),   FOREIGN KEY (Artist) REFERENCES Artist (Id) );  CREATE TABLE SongProducer (   Song INTEGER NOT NULL,   Artist INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Song) REFERENCES Song (Id),   FOREIGN KEY (Artist) REFERENCES Artist (Id) );  CREATE TABLE AlbumProducer (   Album INTEGER NOT NULL,   Artist INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Album) REFERENCES Album (Id),   FOREIGN KEY (Artist) REFERENCES Artist (Id) );  CREATE TABLE Engineer (   Id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   Name TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE SongEngineer (   Song INTEGER NOT NULL,   Engineer INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Song) REFERENCES Song (Id),   FOREIGN KEY (Engineer) REFERENCES Engineer (Id) );  CREATE TABLE AlbumEngineer (   Album INTEGER NOT NULL,   Engineer INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (Album) REFERENCES Album (Id),   FOREIGN KEY (Engineer) REFERENCES Engineer (Id) );  CREATE TABLE Studio (   Id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   Name TEXT NOT NULL,   Location TEXT NOT NULL ); CREATE TABLE users1 (   id BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   password Varchar NOT NULL,   age Integer NOT NULL,   email Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE news1 (   id BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title Varchar NOT NULL,   text Varchar NOT NULL,   date Varchar NOT NULL,   views Integer NOT NULL,   author_id BigInt NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users1 (id) );  CREATE TABLE user_settings (   user_id BigInt NOT NULL UNIQUE,   theme Varchar NOT NULL,   language Varchar NOT NULL,   notifications_enabled Integer NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users1 (id) );  CREATE TABLE user_subscriptions (   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   subscription_type Varchar NOT NULL,   is_active Integer NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users1 (id) );  CREATE TABLE news_recommendations (   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   news_id BigInt NOT NULL,   similarity_score REAL NOT NULL,   recommended_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, news_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users1 (id),   FOREIGN KEY (news_id) REFERENCES news1 (id) );  CREATE TABLE analytics_user_clicks (   user_id BigInt NOT NULL,   element_type Varchar NOT NULL,   element_id BigInt NOT NULL,   clicked_at DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users1 (id) );

### Question:
List the titles of news articles that have associated media uploads, were written by users with active subscriptions, and have received at least one rating above 3 from any user.

SELECT n.title FROM news1 n JOIN news_uploads nu ON n.id = nu.news_id JOIN user_subscriptions s ON n.author_id = s.user_id     AND s.is_active = 1 WHERE EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM user_rating_history h     WHERE h.rated_entity_type = 'news'         AND h.rated_entity_id = n.id         AND h.rating > 3 );
