 CREATE TABLE WikiPage (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   title VARCHAR NOT NULL,   content TEXT NOT NULL,   version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   last_updated_by INTEGER,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (last_updated_by) REFERENCES User (id) );  CREATE TABLE PlaylistFolderShare (   folder_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   permission_level VARCHAR NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES PlaylistFolder (id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id),   UNIQUE (folder_id, user_id) );  CREATE TABLE PlaylistFolderPermission (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   folder_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   permission_role VARCHAR NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (id),   FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES PlaylistFolder (id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id) );  CREATE TABLE TrackVote (   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   track_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   vote_type VARCHAR CHECK (vote_type IN ('UP', 'DOWN')),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES User (id),   FOREIGN KEY (track_id) REFERENCES Track (id),   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, track_id) ); Which artists have a track that has been awarded in the "Best Performance" category and also have a collaboration with at least one other artist?

SELECT a.id FROM Artist a JOIN Album al ON a.id = al.artist JOIN Track t ON al.id = t.album JOIN TrackAward ta ON t.id = ta.track JOIN Award award ON ta.award = award.id JOIN ArtistCollaboration ac ON a.id IN (ac.artist1, ac.artist2) WHERE award.category = 'Best Performance' GROUP BY a.id;

CREATE TABLE user (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   username Text NOT NULL UNIQUE,   password Text NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE data_files (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   owner_id Integer NOT NULL,   filename Text NOT NULL,   longitude Text NOT NULL,   latitude Text NOT NULL,   info Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (owner_id) REFERENCES user (id) );  CREATE TABLE revisions (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   data_file_id Integer NOT NULL,   created Timestamp NOT NULL,   filepath Text NOT NULL,   revision Integer NOT NULL,   file_type Text NOT NULL,   info Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_file_id) REFERENCES data_files (id) );  CREATE TABLE steps (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   data_file_id Integer NOT NULL,   step Text NOT NULL,   parameters Text NOT NULL,   up_to_date Integer NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_file_id) REFERENCES data_files (id) );  CREATE TABLE user_profiles (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   full_name TEXT,   email TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(id) );  CREATE TABLE user_preferences (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   theme TEXT,   language TEXT,   notification_frequency TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(id) );  CREATE TABLE data_file_tags (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   data_file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   tag TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_file_id) REFERENCES data_files(id) );  CREATE TABLE file_metadata (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   data_file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   format TEXT NOT NULL,   size INTEGER NOT NULL,   data_source TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_file_id) REFERENCES data_files(id) );  CREATE TABLE revision_logs (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   revision_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   action TEXT NOT NULL,   timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (revision_id) REFERENCES revisions(id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(id) );  CREATE TABLE revision_status (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   revision_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   reviewer_id INTEGER,   updated TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (revision_id) REFERENCES revisions(id),   FOREIGN KEY (reviewer_id) REFERENCES user(id) );  CREATE TABLE step_dependencies (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   step_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   dependent_step_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (step_id) REFERENCES steps(id),   FOREIGN KEY (dependent_step_id) REFERENCES steps(id) );  CREATE TABLE workflow_steps (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   data_file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   workflow_name TEXT NOT NULL,   step_order INTEGER NOT NULL,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_file_id) REFERENCES data_files(id) );  CREATE TABLE access_permissions (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   data_file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   permission_type TEXT NOT NULL,   granted TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (data_file_id) REFERENCES data_files(id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(id) );  CREATE TABLE audit_trail (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   action_type TEXT NOT NULL,   target_table TEXT NOT NULL,   target_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   timestamp TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(id) );  CREATE TABLE file_sharing (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   data_file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   recipient_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   expires_at TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (data_file_id) REFERENCES data_files(id),   FOREIGN KEY (recipient_user_id) REFERENCES user(id) );  CREATE TABLE data_file_versions (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   data_file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   version_number INTEGER NOT NULL,   base_revision_id INTEGER,   is_current INTEGER DEFAULT 0,   FOREIGN KEY (data_file_id) REFERENCES data_files(id),   FOREIGN KEY (base_revision_id) REFERENCES revisions(id) );  CREATE TABLE notification_settings (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   email_notifications INTEGER DEFAULT 1,   in_app_notifications INTEGER DEFAULT 1,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(id) );  CREATE TABLE task_schedule (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   data_file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   task_name TEXT NOT NULL,   next_run_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   schedule_interval TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (data_file_id) REFERENCES data_files(id) );  CREATE TABLE data_quality (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   data_file_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   completeness_score REAL NOT NULL,   accuracy_score REAL NOT NULL,   assessed_by INTEGER NOT NULL,   assessed_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (data_file_id) REFERENCES data_files(id),   FOREIGN KEY (assessed_by) REFERENCES user(id) );  CREATE TABLE system_logs (   id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   log_level TEXT NOT NULL,   message TEXT NOT NULL,   component TEXT NOT NULL,   timestamp TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  CREATE INDEX idx_user_profiles_user_id ON user_profiles (user_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_revision_status_reviewer_id ON revision_status (reviewer_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_step_dependencies_step_id ON step_dependencies (step_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_step_dependencies_dependent_step_id ON step_dependencies (dependent_step_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_audit_trail_user_id ON audit_trail (user_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_audit_trail_target_table_id ON audit_trail (target_table, target_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_file_sharing_data_file_id ON file_sharing (data_file_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_notification_settings_user_id ON notification_settings (user_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_task_schedule_data_file_id ON task_schedule (data_file_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_data_quality_data_file_id ON data_quality (data_file_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_data_quality_assessed_by ON data_quality (assessed_by);

### Question:
Find the average accuracy score of data files with "ocean" tag, associated with a workflow, and assessed by users in the "marine" group.

SELECT AVG(dq.accuracy_score) FROM data_quality dq JOIN data_file_tags dft ON dq.data_file_id = dft.data_file_id JOIN workflow_steps ws ON dq.data_file_id = ws.data_file_id JOIN user_profiles up ON dq.assessed_by = up.user_id WHERE dft.tag = 'ocean'    AND up.email LIKE '%marine%';

CREATE TABLE cws_versions (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   schema_version Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   cws_version Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   db_vendor Varchar NOT NULL,   installed Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE cws_circles (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   external_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   external_key TEXT NOT NULL,   altered Timestamp NOT NULL,   added Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE cws_trustees (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   member_id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   circle_id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   key_id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   trust_level Varchar NOT NULL,   circle_key Varchar NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Not Applicable',   altered Timestamp NOT NULL,   added Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (circle_id) REFERENCES cws_circles (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (key_id) REFERENCES cws_keys (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_datatypes (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   datatype_name Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   datatype_value Varchar NOT NULL,   altered Timestamp NOT NULL,   added Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE cws_metadata (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   external_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   parent_id Integer NOT NULL,   circle_id Integer NOT NULL,   datatype_id Integer NOT NULL,   name Varchar NOT NULL,   altered Timestamp NOT NULL,   added Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (circle_id) REFERENCES cws_circles (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (datatype_id) REFERENCES cws_datatypes (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_data (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   metadata_id Integer NOT NULL,   key_id Integer NOT NULL,   encrypted_data TEXT NOT NULL,   initial_vector Varchar NOT NULL,   checksum Varchar NOT NULL,   sanity_status Varchar NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Ok',   sanity_checked Timestamp NOT NULL,   altered Timestamp NOT NULL,   added Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (metadata_id) REFERENCES cws_metadata (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (key_id) REFERENCES cws_keys (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE cws_signatures (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   public_key Varchar NOT NULL,   checksum Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   member_id Integer NOT NULL,   expires Timestamp NOT NULL,   verifications Integer NOT NULL,   altered Timestamp NOT NULL,   added Timestamp NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE cws_audit_logs (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   action_type TEXT NOT NULL,   entity_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   entity_type TEXT NOT NULL,   old_value TEXT,   new_value TEXT,   changed_by INTEGER NOT NULL,   changed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   ip_address TEXT,   session_token TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (changed_by) REFERENCES cws_members (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_notifications (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   member_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   message TEXT NOT NULL,   is_read BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   redirect_url TEXT,   notification_type TEXT NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE cws_tokens (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   member_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   token_value TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   token_type TEXT NOT NULL,   issued_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   used BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   issued_ip TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE cws_permissions (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   circle_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   member_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   permission_type TEXT NOT NULL,   granted_by INTEGER NOT NULL,   granted_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   revoked BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   revoked_at TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (circle_id) REFERENCES cws_circles (id),   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id),   FOREIGN KEY (granted_by) REFERENCES cws_members (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_sessions (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   member_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   session_token TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   start_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   end_time TIMESTAMP,   ip_address TEXT,   user_agent TEXT,   is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE cws_webhooks (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   event_type TEXT NOT NULL,   target_url TEXT NOT NULL,   is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   last_fired_at TIMESTAMP,   failure_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   auth_type TEXT,   secret_key TEXT );  CREATE TABLE cws_backup_records (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   backup_type TEXT NOT NULL,   start_time TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   end_time TIMESTAMP,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   size_in_bytes INTEGER NOT NULL,   file_path TEXT NOT NULL,   initiated_by INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (initiated_by) REFERENCES cws_members (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_jobs (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   job_name TEXT NOT NULL,   job_type TEXT NOT NULL,   schedule TEXT NOT NULL,   last_run TIMESTAMP,   next_run TIMESTAMP,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   created_by INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (created_by) REFERENCES cws_members (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_cache (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   key_value TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   value TEXT NOT NULL,   expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   hit_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 );  CREATE TABLE cws_translations (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   language_code TEXT NOT NULL,   key_value TEXT NOT NULL,   translated_value TEXT NOT NULL,   last_updated TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   creator_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (creator_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_api_keys (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   key_value TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   description TEXT,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   expires_at TIMESTAMP,   is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   member_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE cws_events (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   event_name TEXT NOT NULL,   event_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   location TEXT NOT NULL,   organizer_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   capacity INTEGER,   attendees_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   FOREIGN KEY (organizer_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_alerts (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   alert_type TEXT NOT NULL,   message TEXT NOT NULL,   is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   triggered_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   resolved_at TIMESTAMP,   related_entity TEXT,   related_id INTEGER NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE cws_plugins (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   plugin_name TEXT NOT NULL,   version TEXT NOT NULL,   is_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   configuration TEXT,   installed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   last_updated TIMESTAMP,   author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_preferences (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   member_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   preference_key TEXT NOT NULL,   preference_value TEXT NOT NULL,   last_updated TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE cws_integration (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   service_name TEXT NOT NULL,   service_type TEXT NOT NULL,   configuration TEXT NOT NULL,   is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   last_sync TIMESTAMP,   status TEXT NOT NULL,   member_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE cws_history (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   entity_type TEXT NOT NULL,   entity_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   action_type TEXT NOT NULL,   performed_by INTEGER NOT NULL,   timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   details TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (performed_by) REFERENCES cws_members (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_invitations (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   email TEXT NOT NULL,   creator_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   invite_token TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   is_accepted BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   accepted_at TIMESTAMP,   FOREIGN KEY (creator_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_access_logs (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   member_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   accessed_entity TEXT NOT NULL,   accessed_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   accessed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   ip_address TEXT,   user_agent TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id) );  CREATE TABLE cws_logs_archive (   id INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,   archived_from_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   archived_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   log_type TEXT NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   severity TEXT NOT NULL,   entity_id INTEGER,   entity_type TEXT,   member_id INTEGER,   FOREIGN KEY (member_id) REFERENCES cws_members (id),   FOREIGN KEY (archived_from_id) REFERENCES cws_logs (id) );

How many distinct members have both a valid token (not expired and not used) with type 'RESET_PASSWORD' and at least one notification with type 'SECURITY_ALERT' that is unread and has a redirect URL?

CREATE TABLE cpa (   cpa_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   cpa Text NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE url_mapping (   source Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   destination Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE certificate_mapping (   id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   source TEXT NOT NULL,   destination TEXT NOT NULL,   cpa_id Varchar UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE delivery_task (   cpa_id Varchar NOT NULL,   send_channel_id Varchar,   receive_channel_id Varchar NOT NULL,   message_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   time_to_live Datetime,   time_stamp Datetime NOT NULL,   is_confidential TEXT NOT NULL,   retries SmallInt NOT NULL,   server_id Varchar );  CREATE TABLE delivery_log (   message_id Varchar NOT NULL,   time_stamp Datetime NOT NULL,   uri Varchar,   status SmallInt NOT NULL,   error_message Text );  CREATE TABLE message_event (   message_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   event_type SmallInt NOT NULL,   time_stamp Datetime NOT NULL,   processed SmallInt NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE channel (   channel_id VARCHAR NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   channel_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   cpa_id VARCHAR,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY(cpa_id) REFERENCES cpa(cpa_id) );  CREATE TABLE server (   server_id VARCHAR NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   location VARCHAR,   status VARCHAR,   cpa_id VARCHAR,   FOREIGN KEY(cpa_id) REFERENCES cpa(cpa_id) );  CREATE TABLE message_content (   message_id VARCHAR NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   content_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   content_data BLOB NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY(message_id) REFERENCES delivery_task(message_id) );  CREATE TABLE user_accounts (   user_id VARCHAR NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   cpa_id VARCHAR,   role VARCHAR NOT NULL,   email VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE,   FOREIGN KEY(cpa_id) REFERENCES cpa(cpa_id) );  CREATE TABLE audit_log (   log_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   action VARCHAR NOT NULL,   user_id VARCHAR,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES user_accounts(user_id) );  CREATE TABLE configuration (   config_key VARCHAR NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   value VARCHAR NOT NULL,   cpa_id VARCHAR,   FOREIGN KEY(cpa_id) REFERENCES cpa(cpa_id) );  CREATE TABLE security_policy (   policy_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   cpa_id VARCHAR,   policy_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   details TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY(cpa_id) REFERENCES cpa(cpa_id) );  CREATE TABLE error_codes (   code SMALLINT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   resolution TEXT );  CREATE TABLE delivery_retry (   retry_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   message_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   attempt_time DATETIME NOT NULL,   status SMALLINT,   FOREIGN KEY(message_id) REFERENCES delivery_task(message_id) );  CREATE TABLE message_statistics (   stat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   cpa_id VARCHAR,   date DATE NOT NULL,   total_messages INTEGER NOT NULL,   success_count INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY(cpa_id) REFERENCES cpa(cpa_id) );  CREATE TABLE certificate_authority (   ca_id VARCHAR NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   certificate_data BLOB NOT NULL,   cpa_id VARCHAR,   FOREIGN KEY(cpa_id) REFERENCES cpa(cpa_id) );  CREATE TABLE task_scheduler (   task_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   message_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   scheduled_time DATETIME,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY(message_id) REFERENCES delivery_task(message_id) );  CREATE TABLE system_logs (   log_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   event_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   details TEXT,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE message_attachment (   attachment_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   message_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   file_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   file_data BLOB NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY(message_id) REFERENCES delivery_task(message_id) );  CREATE TABLE access_control (   access_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   resource VARCHAR NOT NULL,   permission VARCHAR NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES user_accounts(user_id) );  CREATE INDEX idx_channel_cpa_id ON channel(cpa_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_server_cpa_id ON server(cpa_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_user_cpa_id ON user_accounts(cpa_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_audit_user_id ON audit_log(user_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_config_cpa_id ON configuration(cpa_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_policy_cpa_id ON security_policy(cpa_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_retry_message_id ON delivery_retry(message_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_stat_cpa_id ON message_statistics(cpa_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_ca_cpa_id ON certificate_authority(cpa_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_task_message_id ON task_scheduler(message_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_notify_user_id ON notification_preferences(user_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_attachment_message_id ON message_attachment(message_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_access_user_id ON access_control(user_id);  CREATE TABLE message_queue_status (   queue_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   message_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   last_processed DATETIME,   FOREIGN KEY(message_id) REFERENCES delivery_task(message_id) );  CREATE TABLE status_history (   history_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   message_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   status TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,   details TEXT,   FOREIGN KEY(message_id) REFERENCES delivery_task(message_id) );  CREATE TABLE system_metrics (   metric_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   metric_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   value REAL NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE secure_transmission (    transmission_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   message_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   encryption_method VARCHAR,   FOREIGN KEY(message_id) REFERENCES delivery_task(message_id) );  CREATE TABLE certificate_expiration (   expiration_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   ca_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   expiration_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY(ca_id) REFERENCES certificate_authority(ca_id) );  CREATE TABLE user_preferences (   user_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   preference_key VARCHAR NOT NULL,   preference_value TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY(user_id, preference_key),   FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES user_accounts(user_id) );  CREATE TABLE security_incidents (   incident_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_id VARCHAR,   details TEXT,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES user_accounts(user_id) );  CREATE TABLE anomaly_detection (   anomaly_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   system_component VARCHAR NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE maintenance_intervals (   interval_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   resource_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   last_maintenance DATETIME,   next_maintenance DATETIME );  CREATE TABLE communication_protocols (   protocol_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   protocol_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   details TEXT,   cpa_id VARCHAR,   FOREIGN KEY(cpa_id) REFERENCES cpa(cpa_id) );  CREATE TABLE remote_access (   access_log_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   ip_address VARCHAR,   access_time DATETIME,   FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES user_accounts(user_id) );  CREATE TABLE archival_storage (   archive_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   message_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   storage_location VARCHAR,   archived_time DATETIME,   FOREIGN KEY(message_id) REFERENCES message_attachment(attachment_id) );  CREATE TABLE system_errors (   error_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   error_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE system_components (   component_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   component_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   status VARCHAR,   last_updated DATETIME );  CREATE TABLE performance_analytics (   analytics_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   component_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   metric_key VARCHAR NOT NULL,   value REAL,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE system_notifications (   notification_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   notification_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   message TEXT,   recipient_list TEXT,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE resource_usage (   usage_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   resource_name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   value REAL NOT NULL,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE system_health (   health_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   health_status VARCHAR NOT NULL,   critical_metrics TEXT,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE license_keys (   license_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   product_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   license_key VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE,   validity_start DATETIME,   validity_end DATETIME );  CREATE TABLE session_data (   session_id VARCHAR NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   user_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,   login_time DATETIME,   logout_time DATETIME,   FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES user_accounts(user_id) );  CREATE TABLE system_backups (   backup_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   backup_time DATETIME NOT NULL,   storage_location VARCHAR,   status VARCHAR NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE activity_logs (   log_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   user_id VARCHAR,   action_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,   description TEXT,   timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES user_accounts(user_id) ); ### Question:
List the user roles that have access to at least one secure transmission method, along with their associated CPA identifiers.
SELECT DISTINCT ua.role, ua.cpa_id FROM user_accounts ua JOIN access_control ac ON ua.user_id = ac.user_id WHERE ac.resource IN (SELECT encryption_method FROM secure_transmission);

### Question:
What is the distribution of student enrollments across courses by instructor, considering only courses with more than 10 enrollments, and also include the average student grade for each course and the total attendance count for each course?

WITH CourseEnrollmentSummary AS (     SELECT CourseName, StudentPhone     FROM course_enrollment ), GradingSummary AS (     SELECT CourseName, StudentPhone, Grade     FROM student_grades ), AttendanceSummary AS (     SELECT CourseName, StudentPhone     FROM attendance_log ) SELECT      i.FULL_NAME AS Instructor,     ces.CourseName,     COUNT(DISTINCT ces.StudentPhone) AS StudentCount,     AVG(CAST(gs.Grade AS REAL)) AS AverageGrade,     COUNT(DISTINCT asl.StudentPhone) AS TotalAttendance FROM CourseEnrollmentSummary ces JOIN GradingSummary gs ON ces.CourseName = gs.CourseName AND ces.StudentPhone = gs.StudentPhone JOIN AttendanceSummary asl ON ces.CourseName = asl.CourseName AND ces.StudentPhone = asl.StudentPhone JOIN instractor_information i ON ces.CourseName = i.COURS GROUP BY i.FULL_NAME, ces.CourseName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ces.StudentPhone) > 10;

### Question:
What is the most common product tag among products that have been returned in orders placed by customers with active subscriptions?

WITH active_customers AS (     SELECT customer_id     FROM customer_subscriptions     WHERE start_date <= date('now') AND end_date >= date('now') ), returned_orders AS (     SELECT o.id     FROM orders o     JOIN active_customers ac ON o.customer_id = ac.customer_id     JOIN order_returns orr ON o.id = orr.order_id ) SELECT pt.tag FROM order_items oi JOIN returned_orders ro ON oi.order_id = ro.id JOIN product_tags pt ON oi.product_id = pt.product_id GROUP BY pt.tag ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;

### Question:
List the most recent 5 comments on posts that have at least one media attachment, along with the username of the commenter.

SELECT c.id AS comment_id, c.content, c.comment_date, u.username FROM comments c JOIN users u ON c.user_id = u.id WHERE c.post_id IN (SELECT post_id FROM post_media) ORDER BY c.comment_date DESC LIMIT 5;

### Question:
What is the email address of the contact person for the MTU located in the city with the highest number of associated case studies?

WITH city_case_study_count AS (     SELECT mtu_list.city, COUNT(DISTINCT case_study.id) AS num_case_studies     FROM mtu_list     JOIN mtu_analysis ON mtu_list.id = mtu_analysis.mtu     JOIN analysis_data ON mtu_analysis.id = analysis_data.analysis_id     JOIN data ON analysis_data.data_id = data.id     JOIN data_case_study ON data.id = data_case_study.data_id     JOIN case_study ON data_case_study.case_study_id = case_study.id     GROUP BY mtu_list.city ) SELECT mtu_contact_info.email FROM mtu_contact_info JOIN mtu_list ON mtu_contact_info.mtu_id = mtu_list.id WHERE mtu_list.city = (     SELECT city     FROM city_case_study_count     ORDER BY num_case_studies DESC     LIMIT 1 );

### Question:
For each observer with a specific configuration key, what is the total number of annotations they have made on tests that are dependencies of other tests and have experienced at least one error event in the past month?

WITH eligible_tests AS (     SELECT DISTINCT d.depends_on_test_fk AS test_id     FROM tbl_serv_dependency_test d     INNER JOIN tbl_serv_error_log e ON d.depends_on_test_fk = e.test_fk     WHERE e.error_time > DATE('now', '-30 days') ) SELECT      c.observer_fk,      COUNT(a.annotation_id) AS total_annotations FROM      eligible_tests t INNER JOIN      tbl_serv_annotation_test a ON t.test_id = a.test_fk INNER JOIN      tbl_serv_conf_observer c ON a.annotator = c.observer_fk WHERE      c.config_key = 'specific_key' GROUP BY      c.observer_fk;

CREATE TABLE "match" (   team1 Text NOT NULL,   team2 Text NOT NULL,   date Datetime NOT NULL,   venue Varchar NOT NULL,   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   playedOn Datetime NOT NULL,   source Text NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE match_events (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   event Text NOT NULL,   half Int NOT NULL,   kpi Text NOT NULL,   location Text NOT NULL,   player Text NOT NULL,   rawtime Text NOT NULL,   sector Int NOT NULL,   team Text NOT NULL,   time Int NOT NULL,   match_id Int NOT NULL,   lColumn Int NOT NULL,   lRow Varchar NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (match_id) REFERENCES "match" (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE match_plays (   id Int NOT NULL UNIQUE,   endtime Int NOT NULL,   events Text NOT NULL,   next Int NOT NULL,   origin Text NOT NULL,   origin_attacks Int NOT NULL,   origin_team Text NOT NULL,   outcome Text NOT NULL,   outcome_attacks Int NOT NULL,   outcome_team Text NOT NULL,   previous Int NOT NULL,   starttime Text NOT NULL,   match_id Int NOT NULL,   reduced_outcome Text NOT NULL,   playid Int NOT NULL,   reduced_origin Text NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (match_id) REFERENCES "match" (id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE teams (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   region TEXT NOT NULL,   coach TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE positions (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE );  CREATE TABLE players (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL,   team_id INTEGER,   position_id INTEGER,   number INTEGER,   FOREIGN KEY (team_id) REFERENCES teams(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (position_id) REFERENCES positions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL );

### Question:
Which team has the highest number of players assigned to the position with the highest average player number?

WITH highest_avg_position AS (     SELECT position_id     FROM players     GROUP BY position_id     ORDER BY AVG(number) DESC     LIMIT 1 ), team_player_count AS (     SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS player_count     FROM players     WHERE position_id = (SELECT position_id FROM highest_avg_position)     GROUP BY team_id     ORDER BY player_count DESC     LIMIT 1 ) SELECT t.name FROM team_player_count tpc JOIN teams t ON t.id = tpc.team_id;

CREATE TABLE Student (   ID Integer NOT NULL,   Name Char NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE Enrollment (   StudentID Integer NOT NULL,   CourseID Integer NOT NULL,   EnrollmentDate Text,   Grade Text,   PRIMARY KEY (StudentID, CourseID),   FOREIGN KEY (StudentID) REFERENCES Student (ID),   FOREIGN KEY (CourseID) REFERENCES Course (ID) );  CREATE TABLE CourseInstructor (   CourseID Integer NOT NULL,   ProfessorID Integer NOT NULL,   AssignmentDate Text,   PRIMARY KEY (CourseID, ProfessorID),   FOREIGN KEY (CourseID) REFERENCES Course (ID),   FOREIGN KEY (ProfessorID) REFERENCES Professor (ID) );  CREATE TABLE Department (   ID Integer NOT NULL,   Name Char NOT NULL,   HeadProfessorID Integer,   PRIMARY KEY (ID),   FOREIGN KEY (HeadProfessorID) REFERENCES Professor (ID) );  CREATE TABLE ProfessorDepartment (   ProfessorID Integer NOT NULL,   DepartmentID Integer NOT NULL,   AssignmentDate Text,   PRIMARY KEY (ProfessorID, DepartmentID),   FOREIGN KEY (ProfessorID) REFERENCES Professor (ID),   FOREIGN KEY (DepartmentID) REFERENCES Department (ID) );  CREATE TABLE CoursePrerequisites (   CourseID Integer NOT NULL,   PrerequisiteCourseID Integer NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (CourseID, PrerequisiteCourseID),   FOREIGN KEY (CourseID) REFERENCES Course (ID),   FOREIGN KEY (PrerequisiteCourseID) REFERENCES Course (ID) );  CREATE TABLE Attendance (   StudentID Integer NOT NULL,   CourseID Integer NOT NULL,   ScheduleDate Text NOT NULL,   StartTime Text NOT NULL,   Status Char NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (StudentID, CourseID, ScheduleDate, StartTime),   FOREIGN KEY (StudentID) REFERENCES Student (ID),   FOREIGN KEY (CourseID) REFERENCES Course (ID),   FOREIGN KEY (ScheduleDate, StartTime) REFERENCES CourseSchedule (ScheduleDate, StartTime) );  CREATE TABLE Exam (   CourseID Integer NOT NULL,   ExamType Char NOT NULL,   Date Text NOT NULL,   MaxScore Integer,   PRIMARY KEY (CourseID, ExamType),   FOREIGN KEY (CourseID) REFERENCES Course (ID) ); CREATE TABLE event_date (   id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   event_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   date_order TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   start_at Datetime NOT NULL,   finish_at Datetime NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE event_tag (   id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE event_tag_link (   event_id TEXT NOT NULL,   tag_id TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (event_id, tag_id),   FOREIGN KEY (event_id) REFERENCES event (id),   FOREIGN KEY (tag_id) REFERENCES event_tag (id) );  CREATE TABLE ticket_type (   id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   event_id TEXT NOT NULL,   type_name TEXT NOT NULL,   price REAL,   quantity_available INTEGER,   is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (event_id) REFERENCES event (id) );  CREATE TABLE ticket_sale (   id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   ticket_type_id TEXT NOT NULL,   sale_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   quantity_sold INTEGER NOT NULL,   revenue REAL,   is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (ticket_type_id) REFERENCES ticket_type (id) );  CREATE TABLE attendance (   id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   event_id TEXT NOT NULL,   person_id TEXT NOT NULL,   attendance_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (event_id) REFERENCES event (id),   FOREIGN KEY (person_id) REFERENCES person (id) );  CREATE TABLE event_review (   id TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,   event_id TEXT NOT NULL,   person_id TEXT NOT NULL,   rating INTEGER NOT NULL,   review_text TEXT,   review_date DATETIME NOT NULL,   is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (event_id) REFERENCES event (id),   FOREIGN KEY (person_id) REFERENCES person (id) );  CREATE INDEX idx_ticket_type_event_id ON ticket_type (event_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_ticket_sale_ticket_type_id ON ticket_sale (ticket_type_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_attendance_event_id ON attendance (event_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_attendance_person_id ON attendance (person_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_event_review_event_id ON event_review (event_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_event_review_person_id ON event_review (person_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_program_item_event_id ON program_item (event_id);  CREATE INDEX idx_sponsorship_event_id ON sponsorship (event_id);

### Question:
What is the total revenue generated from ticket sales for event with ID "E12345", considering only non-deleted ticket types and sales?

SELECT SUM(ticket_sale.revenue) AS total_revenue FROM ticket_sale JOIN ticket_type ON ticket_sale.ticket_type_id = ticket_type.id WHERE ticket_type.event_id = 'E12345'   AND ticket_type.is_deleted = 0   AND ticket_sale.is_deleted = 0;

CREATE TABLE playlist_item (   playlist_item_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   content_id TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE user_playlist_permissions (   permission_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   can_edit BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   can_delete BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   can_share BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   PRIMARY KEY (user_id, playlist_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_version_history (   history_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   version_id TEXT NOT NULL,   previous_version_id TEXT,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   change_type TEXT NOT NULL,   change_summary TEXT,   changed_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (history_id),   FOREIGN KEY (version_id) REFERENCES playlist_version(version_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (previous_version_id) REFERENCES playlist_version(version_id) ON DELETE SET NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE content_download_activity (   download_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   content_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   download_ts Timestamp NOT NULL,   device_id TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY (download_id),   FOREIGN KEY (content_id) REFERENCES content(content_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (device_id) REFERENCES device(device_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_access_log (   access_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id TEXT,   access_ts Timestamp NOT NULL,   referer TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY (access_id),   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_recommendation (   recommendation_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   originating_playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   recommended_playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   recommendation_score REAL NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (recommendation_id),   FOREIGN KEY (originating_playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (recommended_playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_item_rating (   item_rating_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   playlist_item_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   rating_value INTEGER NOT NULL,   comment TEXT,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (item_rating_id),   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_item_id) REFERENCES playlist_item(playlist_item_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_statistics (   stats_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   total_views INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   total_edits INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   total_shares INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   last_accessed Timestamp,   PRIMARY KEY (stats_id),   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_search_index (   search_index_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   indexed_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   metadata TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY (search_index_id),   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE content_search_index (   search_index_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   content_id TEXT NOT NULL,   indexed_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   metadata TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY (search_index_id),   FOREIGN KEY (content_id) REFERENCES content(content_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user_playlist_ratings (   playlist_rating_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   rating_value INTEGER NOT NULL,   comment TEXT,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (playlist_rating_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user_playlist_contributions (   contribution_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   contribution_type TEXT NOT NULL,   contribution_ts Timestamp NOT NULL,   details TEXT,   PRIMARY KEY (contribution_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_version_diffs (   diff_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   version_id TEXT NOT NULL,   previous_version_id TEXT NOT NULL,   added_items TEXT,   removed_items TEXT,   modified_items TEXT,   diff_ts Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (diff_id),   FOREIGN KEY (version_id) REFERENCES playlist_version(version_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (previous_version_id) REFERENCES playlist_version(version_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_notification_settings (   notification_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   notifications_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   update_notification BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   comment_notification BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   rating_notification BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,   PRIMARY KEY (notification_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user_playlist_bookmarks (   bookmark_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (bookmark_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_version_tag (   tag_id TEXT NOT NULL,   version_id TEXT NOT NULL,   tag_name TEXT NOT NULL,   tag_description TEXT,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (tag_id, version_id),   FOREIGN KEY (version_id) REFERENCES playlist_version(version_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE user_playlist_recent (   recent_view_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   viewed_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (recent_view_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_sharing (   sharing_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   playlist_id TEXT NOT NULL,   shared_with TEXT NOT NULL,   shared_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   shared_until Timestamp,   PRIMARY KEY (sharing_id),   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_id) REFERENCES playlist(playlist_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE content_offline_queue (   queue_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   content_id TEXT NOT NULL,   queued_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   download_status TEXT NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (queue_id),   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (content_id) REFERENCES content(content_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE playlist_item_comment (   comment_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,   playlist_item_id TEXT NOT NULL,   user_id TEXT NOT NULL,   comment_text TEXT NOT NULL,   parent_comment_id TEXT,   created_at Timestamp NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (comment_id),   FOREIGN KEY (playlist_item_id) REFERENCES playlist_item(playlist_item_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (parent_comment_id) REFERENCES playlist_item_comment(comment_id) ON DELETE CASCADE );

### Question:
Which users have left ratings for playlists they have also contributed to, and what is the average rating of their contributed playlists?
SELECT r.user_id, AVG(r.rating_value) AS average_rating FROM user_playlist_ratings r JOIN user_playlist_contributions c ON r.user_id = c.user_id AND r.playlist_id = c.playlist_id GROUP BY r.user_id;

CREATE TABLE INVENTORY (   PHARMACYID Varchar NOT NULL,   PRODUCTID Int NOT NULL,   PRICE Float NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   STOCK Int NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   QUERYCOUNT Int NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   FOREIGN KEY (PHARMACYID) REFERENCES PHARMACY (CIF) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE ORDER_PRODUCT (   ORDERID Int NOT NULL,   PRODUCTID Int NOT NULL,   QUANTITY Int NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE PHARMACY (   CIF Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   PHONE_NUMBER Int NOT NULL,   DESCRIPTION Varchar NOT NULL,   START_SCHEDULE TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   END_SCHEDULE TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   LATITUDE Double NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   LONGITUDE Double NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   ADDRESS Varchar NOT NULL,   URL_IMG Varchar NOT NULL DEFAULT 'http://localhost:8080/pharmacys/img/img_no_aviable.png' );  CREATE TABLE RESERVATION (   PHARMACYID Varchar NOT NULL,   PRODUCTID Int NOT NULL,   USERID Varchar NOT NULL,   QUANTITY Int NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (PHARMACYID) REFERENCES PHARMACY (CIF) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (USERID) REFERENCES USER (EMAIL) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE USER (   EMAIL Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   SURNAME Varchar NOT NULL,   PASSWORD tinytext NOT NULL,   RESETHASH tinytext NOT NULL,   ROLE TinyInt NOT NULL,   ACTIVE TinyInt NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',   CIFPHARMACY Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE CATEGORY (   CATEGORYID Int PRIMARY KEY,   NAME Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE PRODUCT_CATEGORY (   PHARMACYID Varchar NOT NULL,   PRODUCTID Int NOT NULL,   CATEGORYID Int NOT NULL,   PRIMARY KEY (PHARMACYID, PRODUCTID),   FOREIGN KEY (PHARMACYID, PRODUCTID) REFERENCES INVENTORY (PHARMACYID, PRODUCTID) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (CATEGORYID) REFERENCES CATEGORY (CATEGORYID) );  CREATE TABLE ORDER_STATUS (   STATUSID Int PRIMARY KEY,   ORDERID Int NOT NULL,   STATUS TINYINT NOT NULL,   STATUSDATE DATETIME NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (ORDERID) REFERENCES ORDERS (ORDERID) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE PAYMENTS (   PAYMENTID Int PRIMARY KEY,   ORDERID Int NOT NULL,   PAYMENTDATE DATETIME NOT NULL,   AMOUNT Float NOT NULL,   METHOD Varchar NOT NULL,   STATUS TINYINT DEFAULT 0,   FOREIGN KEY (ORDERID) REFERENCES ORDERS (ORDERID) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE SHIPPING (   SHIPMENTID Int PRIMARY KEY,   ORDERID Int NOT NULL,   SHIPDATE DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   ADDRESS Varchar NOT NULL,   TRACKINGNUM Varchar,   FOREIGN KEY (ORDERID) REFERENCES ORDERS (ORDERID) ON DELETE CASCADE );  CREATE TABLE DELIVERY_AGENT (   AGENTID Int PRIMARY KEY,   NAME Varchar NOT NULL,   PHONE Int NOT NULL,   EMAIL Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   CALENDAR_DATE DATETIME );  CREATE TABLE DELIVERY_ASSIGNMENT (   ASSIGNMENTID Int PRIMARY KEY,   SHIPMENTID Int NOT NULL,   AGENTID Int NOT NULL,   ASSIGNDATE DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,   STATUS TINYINT DEFAULT 0,   FOREIGN KEY (SHIPMENTID) REFERENCES SHIPPING (SHIPMENTID) ON DELETE CASCADE,   FOREIGN KEY (AGENTID) REFERENCES DELIVERY_AGENT (AGENTID) ON DELETE CASCADE );

### Question:
For each delivery agent (DELIVERY_AGENT.AGENTID) who has been assigned to shipments (DELIVERY_ASSIGNMENT.SHIPMENTID) for orders (ORDER_PRODUCT.ORDERID) with products in the 'Heart Health' category (CATEGORY.NAME) from pharmacies (INVENTORY.PHARMACYID) with an average product price higher than the overall average, compute the total number of such shipments and the average shipment quantity (ORDER_PRODUCT.QUANTITY). Include the agent's name, the pharmacy name, and the average quantity. Filter agents with at least 5 such shipments and order by total shipments descending.

SELECT   DA.AGENTID,   DA.NAME AS AGENT_NAME,   P.NAME AS PHARMACY_NAME,   COUNT(DISTINCT ASSIGNMENTS.SHIPMENTID) AS TOTAL_SHIPMENTS,   AVG(OP.QUANTITY) AS AVERAGE_QUANTITY FROM DELIVERY_AGENT DA JOIN DELIVERY_ASSIGNMENT ASSIGNMENTS ON DA.AGENTID = ASSIGNMENTS.AGENTID JOIN SHIPPING SH ON ASSIGNMENTS.SHIPMENTID = SH.SHIPMENTID JOIN ORDER_PRODUCT OP ON SH.ORDERID = OP.ORDERID JOIN PRODUCT_CATEGORY PC ON OP.PRODUCTID = PC.PRODUCTID JOIN CATEGORY C ON PC.CATEGORYID = C.CATEGORYID AND C.NAME = 'Heart Health' JOIN INVENTORY I ON PC.PRODUCTID = I.PRODUCTID AND PC.PHARMACYID = I.PHARMACYID JOIN PHARMACY P ON I.PHARMACYID = P.CIF GROUP BY DA.AGENTID, DA.NAME, P.NAME HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ASSIGNMENTS.SHIPMENTID) >= 5   AND AVG(I.PRICE) > (SELECT AVG(PRICE) FROM INVENTORY) ORDER BY TOTAL_SHIPMENTS DESC;

CREATE TABLE authorizations (   authorization_key Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   email Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   name Varchar NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE cars (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   licence_plate Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   convertible Boolean NOT NULL,   rating Integer NOT NULL,   engine_type Varchar NOT NULL,   colour Varchar NOT NULL,   model Varchar NOT NULL,   seat_count Integer NOT NULL,   speed_limit Varchar NOT NULL,   manufacturer_id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   driver_id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   FOREIGN KEY (manufacturer_id) REFERENCES manufacturers (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE RESTRICT,   FOREIGN KEY (driver_id) REFERENCES drivers (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE drivers (   id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   first_name Varchar NOT NULL,   last_name Varchar NOT NULL,   username Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   password Varchar NOT NULL,   contact_number Varchar NOT NULL,   gender Varchar NOT NULL,   age Integer NOT NULL,   online_status Boolean NOT NULL,   deleted Boolean NOT NULL,   date_created Timestamp NOT NULL,   date_modified Timestamp NOT NULL,   car_assigned_id Integer NOT NULL UNIQUE,   FOREIGN KEY (car_assigned_id) REFERENCES cars (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE manufacturers (   id Varchar NOT NULL UNIQUE,   location Varchar NOT NULL,   date_established Timestamp NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE service_centers (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   address TEXT NOT NULL,   phone VARCHAR NOT NULL,   rating_avg REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 );  CREATE TABLE parts (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   description TEXT NOT NULL,   price REAL NOT NULL,   supplier_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   FOREIGN KEY (supplier_id) REFERENCES part_suppliers (id) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE RESTRICT );  CREATE TABLE part_suppliers (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   name VARCHAR NOT NULL,   contact_email VARCHAR NOT NULL,   phone VARCHAR NOT NULL,   address TEXT NOT NULL );  CREATE TABLE service_center_contacts (   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   service_center_id INTEGER NOT NULL,   contact_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,

### Question:
What is the average number of parts used per service appointment for appointments with a status of 'completed' and a total parts count above 5?

SELECT AVG(parts_used) AS average_parts_used FROM (     SELECT sa.id AS service_id, SUM(sp.quantity) AS parts_used     FROM service_parts sp     JOIN service_appointments sa ON sp.service_id = sa.id     WHERE sa.status = 'completed'     GROUP BY sa.id     HAVING parts_used > 5 ) AS completed_appointments;

### Question:
List all customers who have not made any return requests.

SELECT c.id, c.full_name, c.email, c.phone  FROM customers c  WHERE NOT EXISTS (     SELECT 1      FROM return_items ri      JOIN order_items oi ON ri.item_id = oi.inventory_id      JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id      WHERE o.customer_id = c.id );
